```c
            node->line = t->line; node->col = t->col;
            return node;
        case TOK_IDENTIFIER: {
            /* Might be table.column — check for DOT lookahead */
            if (peek(p)->type == TOK_DOT) {
                advance(p);  /* consume dot */
                const Token *col_tok = peek(p);
                if (col_tok->type != TOK_IDENTIFIER) {
                    parse_error(p, col_tok, "expected column name after '.'");
                    return NULL;
                }
                advance(p);
                node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
                node->line = t->line; node->col = t->col;
                node->as.column_ref.table_alias     = t->start;
                node->as.column_ref.table_alias_len = t->length;
                node->as.column_ref.column          = col_tok->start;
                node->as.column_ref.column_len      = col_tok->length;
                return node;
            }
            node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
            node->line = t->line; node->col = t->col;
            node->as.column_ref.table_alias     = NULL;
            node->as.column_ref.table_alias_len = 0;
            node->as.column_ref.column          = t->start;
            node->as.column_ref.column_len      = t->length;
            return node;
        }
        default:
            parse_error(p, t, "unexpected token in expression");
            return NULL;
    }
}
```
**Key correctness invariant:** `parse_expr` must never consume a token that belongs to the enclosing production rule. Sentinel tokens (`TOK_SEMICOLON`, `TOK_RPAREN`, `TOK_COMMA`, and any keyword with `infix_lbp == -1`) always return from the Pratt loop before being consumed.
---
## 7. Phase 4 — Statement Parsers

![Parser Token Consumption Sequence](./diagrams/tdd-diag-6.svg)

### 7.1 `parser_parse` Dispatch
```c
AstNode *parser_parse(Parser *p) {
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    /* Skip leading semicolons */
    while (peek(p)->type == TOK_SEMICOLON) advance(p);
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_EOF) {
        parse_error(p, peek(p), "empty input");
        return NULL;
    }
    const Token *t = peek(p);
    if (t->type != TOK_KEYWORD) {
        parse_error(p, t, "expected statement keyword");
        return NULL;
    }
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "SELECT")) return parse_select(p);
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "INSERT")) return parse_insert(p);
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "CREATE")) return parse_create_table(p);
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "DROP"))   return parse_drop_table(p);
    parse_error(p, t, "unknown statement type");
    return NULL;
}
```
### 7.2 `parse_select`
```c
static AstNode *parse_select(Parser *p) {
    const Token *kw = advance(p);   /* consume SELECT */
    AstNode *node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_SELECT);
    node->line = kw->line; node->col = kw->col;
    node->as.select.limit = -1;
    /* DISTINCT? */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD && lexeme_equals(peek(p), "DISTINCT")) {
        advance(p);
        node->as.select.distinct = 1;
    }
    /* Projection list: * | col [, col]* */
    AstNode *proj_head = NULL, **proj_tail = &proj_head;
    do {
        AstNode *proj;
        if (peek(p)->type == TOK_STAR) {
            proj = ast_node_alloc(NODE_STAR);
            proj->line = peek(p)->line; proj->col = peek(p)->col;
            advance(p);
        } else if (peek(p)->type == TOK_IDENTIFIER) {
            /* Reuse parse_prefix for column ref (handles table.col) */
            proj = parse_prefix(p);
            if (!proj || p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
        } else {
            parse_error(p, peek(p), "expected column or * in SELECT list");
            ast_free(node); return NULL;
        }
        *proj_tail = proj;
        proj_tail  = &proj->next;
    } while (match_type(p, TOK_COMMA));
    node->as.select.projections = proj_head;
    /* FROM table */
    expect_keyword(p, "FROM", "SELECT");
    if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
    const Token *tbl = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "FROM clause");
    if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
    node->as.select.table_name     = tbl->start;
    node->as.select.table_name_len = tbl->length;
    /* WHERE expr? */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD && lexeme_equals(peek(p), "WHERE")) {
        advance(p);
        node->as.select.where_clause = parse_expr(p, 0);
        if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
    }
    /* ORDER BY col [, col]* ? */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD && lexeme_equals(peek(p), "ORDER")) {
        advance(p);
        expect_keyword(p, "BY", "ORDER BY");
        if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
        AstNode *ob_head = NULL, **ob_tail = &ob_head;
        do {
            AstNode *col = parse_prefix(p);
            if (!col || p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
            *ob_tail = col; ob_tail = &col->next;
        } while (match_type(p, TOK_COMMA));
        node->as.select.order_by = ob_head;
    }
    /* LIMIT n? */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD && lexeme_equals(peek(p), "LIMIT")) {
        advance(p);
        const Token *lim = expect(p, TOK_INTEGER_LIT, "LIMIT");
        if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
        node->as.select.limit = (int)strtol(lim->start, NULL, 10);
    }
    /* Optional trailing semicolon */
    match_type(p, TOK_SEMICOLON);
    return node;
}
```
### 7.3 `parse_insert`
```c
static AstNode *parse_insert(Parser *p) {
    const Token *kw = advance(p);   /* INSERT */
    expect_keyword(p, "INTO", "INSERT");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    const Token *tbl = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "INSERT INTO");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    AstNode *node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INSERT);
    node->line = kw->line; node->col = kw->col;
    node->as.insert.table_name     = tbl->start;
    node->as.insert.table_name_len = tbl->length;
    /* Optional column list: (col, col, ...) */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_LPAREN && peek2(p)->type == TOK_IDENTIFIER) {
        advance(p);  /* ( */
        AstNode *col_head = NULL, **col_tail = &col_head;
        do {
            const Token *col = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "INSERT column list");
            if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
            AstNode *id = ast_node_alloc(NODE_IDENTIFIER);
            id->line = col->line; id->col = col->col;
            id->as.identifier.start  = col->start;
            id->as.identifier.length = col->length;
            *col_tail = id; col_tail = &id->next;
        } while (match_type(p, TOK_COMMA));
        expect(p, TOK_RPAREN, "INSERT column list");
        if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
        node->as.insert.columns = col_head;
    }
    /* VALUES ( expr, expr, ... ) */
    expect_keyword(p, "VALUES", "INSERT");
    expect(p, TOK_LPAREN, "VALUES");
    if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
    AstNode *val_head = NULL, **val_tail = &val_head;
    do {
        AstNode *val = parse_expr(p, 0);
        if (!val || p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
        *val_tail = val; val_tail = &val->next;
    } while (match_type(p, TOK_COMMA));
    expect(p, TOK_RPAREN, "VALUES");
    if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
    node->as.insert.values = val_head;
    match_type(p, TOK_SEMICOLON);
    return node;
}
```
### 7.4 `parse_create_table`
```c
static ColTypeAffinity parse_column_affinity(Parser *p) {
    const Token *t = peek(p);
    if (t->type != TOK_KEYWORD && t->type != TOK_IDENTIFIER) return COL_TYPE_TEXT;
    advance(p);
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "INTEGER") || lexeme_equals(t, "INT"))  return COL_TYPE_INTEGER;
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "REAL")    || lexeme_equals(t, "FLOAT")) return COL_TYPE_REAL;
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "TEXT")    || lexeme_equals(t, "VARCHAR")) return COL_TYPE_TEXT;
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "BLOB"))    return COL_TYPE_BLOB;
    if (lexeme_equals(t, "NUMERIC")) return COL_TYPE_NUMERIC;
    return COL_TYPE_TEXT; /* unknown → TEXT affinity per SQLite rules */
}
static AstNode *parse_col_def(Parser *p) {
    const Token *name = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "column definition");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    AstNode *node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COL_DEF);
    node->line = name->line; node->col = name->col;
    node->as.col_def.name     = name->start;
    node->as.col_def.name_len = name->length;
    node->as.col_def.affinity = parse_column_affinity(p);
    /* Inline constraints: PRIMARY KEY, NOT NULL (order-independent) */
    while (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD) {
        if (lexeme_equals(peek(p), "PRIMARY")) {
            advance(p);
            expect_keyword(p, "KEY", "PRIMARY KEY constraint");
            if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
            node->as.col_def.primary_key = 1;
        } else if (lexeme_equals(peek(p), "NOT")) {
            advance(p);
            expect_keyword(p, "NULL", "NOT NULL constraint");
            if (p->had_error) { ast_free(node); return NULL; }
            node->as.col_def.not_null = 1;
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    return node;
}
static AstNode *parse_create_table(Parser *p) {
    const Token *kw = advance(p);   /* CREATE */
    expect_keyword(p, "TABLE", "CREATE TABLE");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    AstNode *node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_CREATE_TABLE);
    node->line = kw->line; node->col = kw->col;
    /* IF NOT EXISTS */
    if (peek(p)->type == TOK_KEYWORD && lexeme_equals(peek(p), "IF")) {
        advance(p);
        expect_keyword(p, "NOT", "IF NOT EXISTS"); if (p->had_error) goto fail;
        expect_keyword(p, "EXISTS", "IF NOT EXISTS"); if (p->had_error) goto fail;
        node->as.create_table.if_not_exists = 1;
    }
    const Token *tbl = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "CREATE TABLE name");
    if (p->had_error) goto fail;
    node->as.create_table.table_name     = tbl->start;
    node->as.create_table.table_name_len = tbl->length;
    expect(p, TOK_LPAREN, "CREATE TABLE column list");
    if (p->had_error) goto fail;
    AstNode *col_head = NULL, **col_tail = &col_head;
    do {
        /* Skip trailing comma before ) */
        if (peek(p)->type == TOK_RPAREN) break;
        AstNode *col = parse_col_def(p);
        if (!col || p->had_error) goto fail;
        *col_tail = col; col_tail = &col->next;
    } while (match_type(p, TOK_COMMA));
    expect(p, TOK_RPAREN, "CREATE TABLE column list");
    if (p->had_error) goto fail;
    node->as.create_table.col_defs = col_head;
    match_type(p, TOK_SEMICOLON);
    return node;
fail:
    ast_free(node);
    return NULL;
}
```
### 7.5 `parse_drop_table`
```c
static AstNode *parse_drop_table(Parser *p) {
    const Token *kw = advance(p);   /* DROP */
    expect_keyword(p, "TABLE", "DROP TABLE");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    const Token *tbl = expect(p, TOK_IDENTIFIER, "DROP TABLE name");
    if (p->had_error) return NULL;
    AstNode *node = ast_node_alloc(NODE_DROP_TABLE);
    node->line = kw->line; node->col = kw->col;
    node->as.drop_table.name     = tbl->start;
    node->as.drop_table.name_len = tbl->length;
    match_type(p, TOK_SEMICOLON);
    return node;
}
```
---
## 8. Error Reporting Contract
The parser enforces a **single-error-stops-parsing** rule identical to M1:
1. `parse_error(p, tok, msg)` sets `p->had_error = 1`, records line/col and message.
2. Every parse function checks `if (p->had_error) return NULL` at entry and after any sub-call.
3. `parser_parse` returns `NULL` when `had_error` is set; the caller inspects `parser_error_message`.
Error messages must include:
- Source line and column of the offending token.
- A human-readable description of what was expected.
- The actual lexeme (truncated to the first 40 chars if longer) enclosed in single quotes.
Format: `[line L, col C] <expected> (got '<lexeme>')`
**Example outputs:**
```
[line 1, col 15] expected keyword 'FROM' in SELECT (got 'WERE')
[line 3, col 1]  unexpected token in expression (got ';')
[line 2, col 8]  expected column name after '.' (got '42')
```
---
## 9. Memory Management Summary
| Scenario | Owner | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Source SQL string | Caller of `lexer_tokenize_all` | Must remain valid until `ast_free` |
| `Token[]` array | Caller of `lexer_tokenize_all` | Must remain valid until `ast_free` |
| `AstNode*` tree | Caller of `parser_parse` | Call `ast_free(root)` to release |
| `char*` from `ast_to_sql` | Caller of `ast_to_sql` | Call `free(result)` |
| `Parser` struct | Caller | Stack-allocated; no cleanup needed |
**Valgrind command for leak check:**
```bash
valgrind --leak-check=full --error-exitcode=1 ./test_parser
```
Expected: `All heap blocks were freed -- no leaks are possible`.
---
## 10. Test Specifications
### M2-TS-01 — SELECT Shape
**Purpose:** Verify that a multi-column SELECT with WHERE, ORDER BY, and LIMIT produces the correct AST shape and field values.
**Input SQL:**
```sql
SELECT a, b, c FROM users WHERE age > 18 ORDER BY a LIMIT 100
```
**Pipeline:**
```c
int      count;
Token   *toks = lexer_tokenize_all(sql, &count);
Parser   p;
parser_init(&p, toks, count);
AstNode *root = parser_parse(&p);
```
**Assertions:**
```c
/* 1. No parse error */
assert(root != NULL);
assert(p.had_error == 0);
/* 2. Root is SELECT */
assert(root->type == NODE_SELECT);
/* 3. Three projections: a, b, c */
AstNode *proj = root->as.select.projections;
assert(proj != NULL);
/* a */
assert(proj->type == NODE_COLUMN_REF);
assert(proj->as.column_ref.column_len == 1);
assert(proj->as.column_ref.column[0] == 'a');
proj = proj->next;
/* b */
assert(proj != NULL && proj->type == NODE_COLUMN_REF);
assert(proj->as.column_ref.column[0] == 'b');
proj = proj->next;
/* c */
assert(proj != NULL && proj->type == NODE_COLUMN_REF);
assert(proj->as.column_ref.column[0] == 'c');
assert(proj->next == NULL);
/* 4. Table name */
assert(memcmp(root->as.select.table_name, "users", 5) == 0);
assert(root->as.select.table_name_len == 5);
/* 5. WHERE: binary_op '>' with left=age, right=18 */
AstNode *where = root->as.select.where_clause;
assert(where != NULL);
assert(where->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(where->as.binary_op.op, ">") == 0);
assert(where->as.binary_op.left->type == NODE_COLUMN_REF);
assert(where->as.binary_op.right->type == NODE_INTEGER_LIT);
assert(where->as.binary_op.right->as.integer_lit.value == 18);
/* 6. ORDER BY: single column 'a' */
AstNode *ob = root->as.select.order_by;
assert(ob != NULL && ob->type == NODE_COLUMN_REF);
assert(ob->as.column_ref.column[0] == 'a');
assert(ob->next == NULL);
/* 7. LIMIT */
assert(root->as.select.limit == 100);
/* Cleanup */
ast_free(root);
free(toks);
```
---
### M2-TS-02 — Operator Precedence
**Purpose:** Verify that the Pratt engine produces left-to-right tree structure respecting the binding-power table. No parentheses in input — associativity must come from the algorithm alone.
**Input SQL:**
```sql
SELECT a FROM t WHERE 1 + 2 * 3 = 7 AND x > 0 OR y < 10
```
**Expected tree for WHERE clause (fully parenthesised):**
```
OR
├── AND
│   ├── =
│   │   ├── +
│   │   │   ├── 1
│   │   │   └── *
│   │   │       ├── 2
│   │   │       └── 3
│   │   └── 7
│   └── >
│       ├── x
│       └── 0
└── <
    ├── y
    └── 10
```
**Assertions:**
```c
AstNode *where = root->as.select.where_clause;
/* Top level: OR */
assert(where->type == NODE_OR);
/* OR.left: AND */
AstNode *and_node = where->as.binary_op.left;
assert(and_node->type == NODE_AND);
/* AND.left: = */
AstNode *eq = and_node->as.binary_op.left;
assert(eq->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(eq->as.binary_op.op, "=") == 0);
/* = left: +  with right operand being * */
AstNode *plus = eq->as.binary_op.left;
assert(plus->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(plus->as.binary_op.op, "+") == 0);
assert(plus->as.binary_op.left->as.integer_lit.value  == 1);
AstNode *mul = plus->as.binary_op.right;
assert(mul->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(mul->as.binary_op.op, "*") == 0);
assert(mul->as.binary_op.left->as.integer_lit.value  == 2);
assert(mul->as.binary_op.right->as.integer_lit.value == 3);
/* = right: 7 */
assert(eq->as.binary_op.right->as.integer_lit.value == 7);
/* AND.right: > x 0 */
AstNode *gt = and_node->as.binary_op.right;
assert(gt->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(gt->as.binary_op.op, ">") == 0);
assert(gt->as.binary_op.right->as.integer_lit.value == 0);
/* OR.right: < y 10 */
AstNode *lt = where->as.binary_op.right;
assert(lt->type == NODE_BINARY_OP);
assert(strcmp(lt->as.binary_op.op, "<") == 0);
assert(lt->as.binary_op.right->as.integer_lit.value == 10);
```
---
### M2-TS-03 — CREATE TABLE Column Definitions
**Purpose:** Verify that column names, type affinities, and inline constraints are correctly parsed and linked.
**Input SQL:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE employees (
    id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name    TEXT    NOT NULL,
    salary  REAL,
    dept_id INTEGER
);
```
**Assertions:**
```c
assert(root->type == NODE_CREATE_TABLE);
assert(root->as.create_table.if_not_exists == 0);
assert(memcmp(root->as.create_table.table_name, "employees", 9) == 0);
AstNode *col = root->as.create_table.col_defs;
/* id: INTEGER PRIMARY KEY */
assert(col != NULL && col->type == NODE_COL_DEF);
assert(col->as.col_def.affinity    == COL_TYPE_INTEGER);
assert(col->as.col_def.primary_key == 1);
assert(col->as.col_def.not_null    == 0);
/* name: TEXT NOT NULL */
col = col->next;
assert(col != NULL);
assert(col->as.col_def.affinity == COL_TYPE_TEXT);
assert(col->as.col_def.not_null    == 1);
assert(col->as.col_def.primary_key == 0);
/* salary: REAL (no constraints) */
col = col->next;
assert(col != NULL);
assert(col->as.col_def.affinity    == COL_TYPE_REAL);
assert(col->as.col_def.not_null    == 0);
assert(col->as.col_def.primary_key == 0);
/* dept_id: INTEGER (no constraints) */
col = col->next;
assert(col != NULL);
assert(col->as.col_def.affinity    == COL_TYPE_INTEGER);
assert(col->next == NULL);
/* Verify column name (dept_id) */
assert(memcmp(col->as.col_def.name, "dept_id", 7) == 0);
assert(col->as.col_def.name_len == 7);
```
---
### M2-TS-04 — Syntax Error Position Reporting
**Purpose:** Verify that parse errors record correct line and column numbers and do not return a non-NULL root.
**Test cases:**
```c
/* --- Case A: misspelled FROM --- */
{
    const char *sql = "SELECT a FORM t";
    /*                           ^col 10 */
    int count; Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all(sql, &count);
    Parser p; parser_init(&p, toks, count);
    AstNode *root = parser_parse(&p);
    assert(root == NULL);
    assert(p.had_error == 1);
    assert(p.error_line == 1);
    assert(p.error_col  == 10);   /* 'FORM' starts at col 10 */
    free(toks);
}
/* --- Case B: missing closing paren in VALUES --- */
{
    const char *sql = "INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 2\nSELECT";
    /*                                                ^line 2, col 1 */
    int count; Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all(sql, &count);
    Parser p; parser_init(&p, toks, count);
    AstNode *root = parser_parse(&p);
    assert(root == NULL);
    assert(p.had_error == 1);
    assert(p.error_line == 2);
    free(toks);
}
/* --- Case C: empty input --- */
{
    const char *sql = "   ;   ";
    int count; Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all(sql, &count);
    Parser p; parser_init(&p, toks, count);
    AstNode *root = parser_parse(&p);
    /* The semicolon is skipped; then EOF triggers error */
    assert(root == NULL);
    assert(p.had_error == 1);
    free(toks);
}
```
---
### M2-TS-05 — AST Round-Trip via `ast_to_sql`
**Purpose:** Verify that `ast_to_sql` produces SQL that, when re-parsed, produces a semantically equivalent AST. This is the canonical test for AST completeness.
**Procedure:**
```c
static void assert_round_trip(const char *original_sql) {
    /* Parse pass 1 */
    int count1; Token *toks1 = lexer_tokenize_all(original_sql, &count1);
    Parser p1; parser_init(&p1, toks1, count1);
    AstNode *ast1 = parser_parse(&p1);
    assert(ast1 != NULL && !p1.had_error);
    /* Unparse to canonical SQL */
    char *canonical = ast_to_sql(ast1);
    assert(canonical != NULL);
    /* Parse pass 2 */
    int count2; Token *toks2 = lexer_tokenize_all(canonical, &count2);
    Parser p2; parser_init(&p2, toks2, count2);
    AstNode *ast2 = parser_parse(&p2);
    assert(ast2 != NULL && !p2.had_error);
    /* Unparse again — result must be identical to canonical */
    char *canonical2 = ast_to_sql(ast2);
    assert(canonical2 != NULL);
    assert(strcmp(canonical, canonical2) == 0);   /* idempotent */
    free(canonical); free(canonical2);
    ast_free(ast1); ast_free(ast2);
    free(toks1); free(toks2);
}
/* Invoke for all supported statement types */
assert_round_trip("SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1");
assert_round_trip("SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary LIMIT 50");
assert_round_trip("INSERT INTO t (a, b) VALUES (1, 'hello')");
assert_round_trip("CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)");
assert_round_trip("DROP TABLE old_table");
assert_round_trip("SELECT a FROM t WHERE NOT x > 0 AND y < -1");
```
**Idempotency invariant:** `ast_to_sql(parse(ast_to_sql(parse(sql)))) == ast_to_sql(parse(sql))`. Two round-trips must produce the same canonical string.
---
## 11. Build Integration
### Makefile additions
```makefile
# Add to existing Makefile from M1
SRC += src/ast.c src/parser.c
OBJ := $(SRC:.c=.o)
test_parser: tests/test_parser.c $(OBJ)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LDFLAGS)
.PHONY: test-m2
test-m2: test_parser
	./test_parser
	@echo "M2 tests passed."
```
### Recommended `CFLAGS` for this module
```makefile
CFLAGS  = -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wshadow \
           -Wno-unused-parameter \
           -g -fsanitize=address,undefined
```
AddressSanitizer (`-fsanitize=address`) catches use-after-free bugs that arise if `ast_free` is called before `ast_to_sql`, or if a lexeme pointer is read after the source string is freed. Enable it on every test run during development.
---
## 12. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `*` parsed as `BINARY_OP` instead of `NODE_STAR` | `parse_prefix` falls through to binary path | Check `TOK_STAR` **before** the identifier case in `parse_prefix` |
| `WHERE a AND b` gives AND as child of WHERE's equal comparison | infix_lbp for AND (10) is below comparison (30), so AND should be above | Verify binding powers in table: comparison should be lbp=30, AND=20; lower lbp = weaker binding = higher in tree |
| `NOT x > 0` parses as `NOT (x) > 0` | NOT prefix consumes only next atom | Set NOT's rbp to 25 (between AND=20 and comparison=30) |
| Leaked memory under Valgrind | `goto fail` without freeing already-allocated children | Every `goto fail` path must call `ast_free` on any `col_head` or sibling list built before the error |
| `table.column` produces `NODE_IDENTIFIER` not `NODE_COLUMN_REF` | DOT lookahead check in `parse_prefix` not reached | Ensure identifier case checks `peek(p)->type == TOK_DOT` **after** consuming the first identifier |
| `LIMIT -1` stored in node instead of actual value | `match_type(TOK_INTEGER_LIT)` returned 0; default stays -1 | The parser does not support negative LIMIT (SQL standard does not either); emit error if MINUS precedes integer after LIMIT |
| ast_to_sql truncates output | Buffer too small; `snprintf` silently truncated | Use the two-pass strategy: first pass counts chars, second pass allocates exactly enough |
---
## 13. Acceptance Criteria Summary
| ID | Description | Pass Condition |
|---|---|---|
| M2-AC-01 | SELECT shape | All 7 field assertions in M2-TS-01 pass |
| M2-AC-02 | Pratt precedence | OR/AND/comparison/arithmetic tree structure in M2-TS-02 is exact |
| M2-AC-03 | CREATE TABLE columns | All column defs with correct affinity and constraints in M2-TS-03 |
| M2-AC-04 | Error positions | Line/col accurate within ±0 for all M2-TS-04 cases; root is NULL |
| M2-AC-05 | Round-trip idempotency | All 6 assert_round_trip calls pass; `canonical == canonical2` |
| M2-AC-06 | Memory cleanliness | `valgrind --leak-check=full` exits 0 |
| M2-AC-07 | AST API completeness | `ast_node_type_name` returns non-NULL for every `NodeType` value |
| M2-AC-08 | Static linkage | `nm test_parser \| grep ' T '` shows no `parse_*` symbols exported |
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m3 -->
# Module M3 — Bytecode Compiler (VDBE)
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m3`
**Language:** C (ISO C11) | **Depends On:** `build-sqlite-m1` (Lexer), `build-sqlite-m2` (Parser/AST)
**Difficulty:** Expert | **Estimated Effort:** 11–13 hours
---
## 1. Module Charter
The VDBE (Virtual DataBase Engine) module is the bridge between the SQL frontend and the storage backend. It has two halves: a **compiler** that performs a single post-order walk of an `AstNode*` tree and emits a flat, fixed-width array of `Instruction` structs (the *program*), and a **virtual machine** that executes those instructions by cycling through a fetch-decode-execute loop against a register file and a set of open cursors.
The compiler does **not** perform semantic analysis, schema validation, or type checking — those are left to later modules. It assumes the AST is syntactically valid and that table root pages and column indices are supplied by the caller via a `CompileContext` struct. It emits opcodes for `SELECT`, `INSERT`, and `CREATE TABLE`; `UPDATE` and `DELETE` are out of scope for this module.
The VM does **not** implement real B-tree page I/O. It operates through a `Cursor` interface (a struct of function pointers) so that the mock cursor used in tests is interchangeable with the real B-tree cursor introduced in M5. The VM's register file is a fixed-size array of `Value` unions allocated on the heap once at `vm_init`; there is no dynamic register growth.
**Upstream dependencies:** `AstNode*` tree from M2 parser; `Token` metadata for source positions.  
**Downstream consumers:** M5 (B-tree cursor) plugs into the cursor interface; M6 (DML execution) extends the opcode set; M8 (query planner) drives compilation with plan annotations.
**Invariants that must always hold:**
1. Every emitted program ends with exactly one `OP_HALT` as its final instruction.
2. Every conditional jump's target address is a valid index into `program->ops[]`.
3. The compiler never calls `malloc` during expression compilation — all allocation goes through `emit()` and `alloc_register()`.
4. Register index 0 is reserved as a scratch register; user registers start at 1.
5. The VM never advances `pc` past `program->op_count - 1`.
---
## 2. File Structure
Create files in this order (later files `#include` earlier ones):
```
build-sqlite/
├── src/
│   ├── lexer.h          ← M1 (do not modify)
│   ├── lexer.c          ← M1 (do not modify)
│   ├── ast.h            ← M2 (do not modify)
│   ├── ast.c            ← M2 (do not modify)
│   ├── parser.h         ← M2 (do not modify)
│   ├── parser.c         ← M2 (do not modify)
│   ├── vdbe.h           ← Step 1: Opcode enum, Instruction, Value, Program, public API
│   ├── vdbe.c           ← Step 2: Compiler + VM implementation
│   └── cursor.h         ← Step 3: Abstract cursor interface (function-pointer struct)
└── tests/
    └── test_vdbe.c      ← Step 4: Five test groups
```
**Dependency graph:**
```
vdbe.h → ast.h → lexer.h
vdbe.c → vdbe.h, cursor.h
test_vdbe.c → vdbe.h, cursor.h, ast.h
```
---
## 3. Complete Data Model
### 3.1 Opcode Enum
```c
/* src/vdbe.h */
#ifndef VDBE_H
#define VDBE_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "ast.h"
typedef enum {
    /* Control flow */
    OP_INIT        = 0,   /* Jump to p2 (program start); initialise VM state      */
    OP_HALT        = 1,   /* Stop. p1 = return code (0=ok, non-zero=error)        */
    OP_GOTO        = 2,   /* Unconditional jump to address p2                     */
    /* Cursor ops */
    OP_OPEN_READ   = 3,   /* Open cursor p1 on root page p2 with p3 columns       */
    OP_OPEN_WRITE  = 4,   /* Open cursor p1 on root page p2 for writing           */
    OP_CLOSE       = 5,   /* Close cursor p1                                      */
    OP_REWIND      = 6,   /* Position cursor p1 at first row; jump to p2 if empty */
    OP_NEXT        = 7,   /* Advance cursor p1; jump to p2 if more rows           */
    /* Data access */
    OP_COLUMN      = 8,   /* Read column p2 from cursor p1 into register p3       */
    OP_ROWID       = 9,   /* Read rowid from cursor p1 into register p2           */
    OP_RESULT_ROW  = 10,  /* Output registers p1..p1+p2-1 as one result row       */
    /* Record ops */
    OP_MAKE_RECORD = 11,  /* Pack registers p1..p1+p2-1 into a record in reg p3   */
    OP_INSERT      = 12,  /* Insert record reg[p2] into cursor p1 with key reg[p3]*/
    /* Constants */
    OP_INTEGER     = 13,  /* reg[p2] = (int64_t)p1                               */
    OP_STRING8     = 14,  /* reg[p2] = string_pool[p1]  (NUL-terminated)         */
    OP_NULL        = 15,  /* reg[p2] = NULL                                      */
    OP_REAL        = 16,  /* reg[p2] = real_pool[p1]  (double)                   */
    /* Comparison — all jump to p2 if condition is TRUE */
    OP_EQ          = 17,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] == reg[p3]                    */
    OP_NE          = 18,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] != reg[p3]                    */
    OP_LT          = 19,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] <  reg[p3]                    */
    OP_LE          = 20,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] <= reg[p3]                    */
    OP_GT          = 21,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] >  reg[p3]                    */
    OP_GE          = 22,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] >= reg[p3]                    */
    /* NULL tests */
    OP_ISNULL      = 23,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] is NULL                       */
    OP_NOTNULL     = 24,  /* Jump to p2 if reg[p1] is NOT NULL                   */
    /* Arithmetic — result into reg[p3] */
    OP_ADD         = 25,  /* reg[p3] = reg[p1] + reg[p2]                         */
    OP_SUBTRACT    = 26,  /* reg[p3] = reg[p1] - reg[p2]                         */
    OP_MULTIPLY    = 27,  /* reg[p3] = reg[p1] * reg[p2]                         */
    OP_DIVIDE      = 28,  /* reg[p3] = reg[p1] / reg[p2]; halt on div-by-zero    */
    OP_MODULO      = 29,  /* reg[p3] = reg[p1] % reg[p2]                         */
    /* Copy */
    OP_COPY        = 30,  /* reg[p2] = reg[p1]   (deep copy for strings)         */
    OP_SCOPY       = 31,  /* reg[p2] = reg[p1]   (shallow — borrows string ptr)  */
    /* Schema */
    OP_CREATE_TABLE= 32,  /* Allocate new B-tree root; store page id in reg[p1]  */
    /* Constraint */
    OP_NOT_NULL    = 33,  /* If reg[p1] IS NULL: halt with SQLITE_CONSTRAINT(19) */
    /* Row id generation */
    OP_NEW_ROWID   = 34,  /* reg[p2] = max_rowid(cursor p1) + 1                  */
    /* Sentinel — must be last */
    OP_COUNT       = 35
} Opcode;
```
**Design note — three-address format:** Every opcode uses at most three integer operands (`p1`, `p2`, `p3`). This uniformity means the fetch-decode loop is a single `switch` with no variable-length decoding. The string pool index in `OP_STRING8.p1` and the address in `OP_REWIND.p2` are encoded in the same field type — the meaning is opcode-specific, documented in the enum comments above.
### 3.2 Instruction Struct — Byte Layout
```c
typedef struct {
    Opcode  opcode;    /* offset  0, size 4 (enum stored as int)   */
    int32_t p1;        /* offset  4, size 4                        */
    int32_t p2;        /* offset  8, size 4                        */
    int32_t p3;        /* offset 12, size 4                        */
    char   *comment;   /* offset 16, size 8 (64-bit ptr; NULL ok)  */
} Instruction;
/* sizeof(Instruction) == 24 on LP64. No padding needed.           */
/* 4096 instructions fit in 96 KB — well inside L2 cache.         */
```
`comment` is allocated by `emit()` via `strdup` only when `VDBE_TRACE` is defined at compile time (`make CFLAGS=-DVDBE_TRACE`). In production builds, `comment` is always NULL and `emit` never calls `strdup`.
### 3.3 Value Tagged Union — Byte Layout
```c
typedef enum {
    VAL_NULL    = 0,
    VAL_INTEGER = 1,
    VAL_REAL    = 2,
    VAL_STRING  = 3,
    VAL_BLOB    = 4
} ValueType;
typedef struct {
    ValueType type;    /* offset  0, size 4                                    */
    int32_t   _pad;    /* offset  4, size 4 (keeps union at 8-byte alignment)  */
    union {
        int64_t  i;    /* VAL_INTEGER: offset 8, size 8                        */
        double   r;    /* VAL_REAL:    offset 8, size 8                        */
        struct {
            char *data;  /* offset  8, size 8 — may be borrowed or owned       */
            int   len;   /* offset 16, size 4                                  */
            int   owned; /* offset 20, size 4 — 1 iff data was malloc'd        */
        } s;           /* VAL_STRING / VAL_BLOB                                */
    } u;
} Value;
/* sizeof(Value) == 24 on LP64.                                                */
/* 256 registers × 24 bytes = 6 KB register file — fits in L1 cache.          */
```
**String ownership protocol:** When `Value.type == VAL_STRING` and `Value.u.s.owned == 1`, the VM must call `free(v.u.s.data)` when the register is overwritten or the VM is destroyed. When `owned == 0`, the string data is borrowed from the source buffer (the SQL string or the string pool) and must not be freed. `OP_COPY` sets `owned = 1` and calls `malloc` + `memcpy`. `OP_SCOPY` sets `owned = 0` and copies the pointer only.
{{DIAGRAM:tdd-diag-7}}
*Figure 7: Instruction Set Architecture overview — opcode groups (control, cursor, data, arithmetic, comparison) and their operand field semantics. Each cell shows which of p1/p2/p3 carries a register index, cursor id, address, or constant pool index.*
### 3.4 Program Struct
```c
#define VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS   256
#define VDBE_MAX_INSTRUCTIONS 4096
#define VDBE_MAX_STRINGS      512
#define VDBE_MAX_REALS        128
#define VDBE_MAX_CURSORS       16
typedef struct {
    Instruction  *ops;          /* heap-allocated array; capacity managed by emit()    */
    int           op_count;     /* number of valid instructions                        */
    int           op_capacity;  /* allocated capacity (doubles on overflow)            */
    /* String constant pool — indexed by OP_STRING8.p1 */
    char         *strings[VDBE_MAX_STRINGS];
    int           string_count;
    /* Real constant pool — indexed by OP_REAL.p1 */
    double        reals[VDBE_MAX_REALS];
    int           real_count;
    /* Aggregate function type array — indexed by OP_AGG_INIT.p3 (reserved for M6) */
    int           agg_types[32];
    int           agg_count;
    /* Register high-water mark (n_registers needed to run this program) */
    int           n_registers;
} Program;
```
### 3.5 Compiler Struct
```c
typedef struct {
    Program         *program;           /* output target                          */
    int              next_register;     /* monotonically increasing counter        */
    int              next_cursor;       /* cursor index allocator                  */
    /* Schema lookup (caller provides; compiler reads only) */
    const char     **table_names;
    int             *table_root_pages;
    int             *table_col_counts;
    const char    ***table_col_names;
    int              table_count;
    /* Error state */
    bool             had_error;
    char             error_msg[256];
    /* Current scan loop addresses (for backpatching) */
    int              rewind_addr;       /* address of OP_REWIND — needs empty-jump patch */
    int              loop_start_addr;   /* address of first instruction in loop body      */
} Compiler;
```
### 3.6 VM Struct
```c
typedef struct Cursor Cursor;  /* forward-declared in cursor.h */
typedef struct {
    Program        *program;
    int             pc;                                /* program counter               */
    Value           registers[VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS];     /* register file                 */
    Cursor         *cursors[VDBE_MAX_CURSORS];         /* open cursor handles           */
    /* Result callback: called once per OP_RESULT_ROW */
    void          (*result_cb)(Value *row, int ncols, void *ctx);
    void           *result_ctx;
    /* Execution state */
    bool            halted;
    int             halt_code;
    char            error_msg[256];
} VM;
```
---
## 4. Cursor Abstract Interface (`src/cursor.h`)
```c
/* src/cursor.h */
#ifndef CURSOR_H
#define CURSOR_H
#include "vdbe.h"
/* Function-pointer table — allows mock cursor in tests, real B-tree in production */
typedef struct Cursor {
    /* Position at first row. Returns 1 if table is empty (cursor at EOF). */
    int     (*rewind)   (struct Cursor *cur);
    /* Advance to next row. Returns 1 if another row exists, 0 at EOF. */
    int     (*next)     (struct Cursor *cur);
    /* Read column col_idx from current row into *dest. */
    void    (*column)   (struct Cursor *cur, int col_idx, Value *dest);
    /* Read current rowid into *out. */
    void    (*rowid)    (struct Cursor *cur, int64_t *out);
    /* Insert serialised record. key = rowid. */
    int     (*insert)   (struct Cursor *cur, int64_t key,
                          const Value *record, int ncols);
    /* Compute max existing rowid; return 0 if table is empty. */
    int64_t (*max_rowid)(struct Cursor *cur);
    /* Release resources. Cursor pointer is invalid after this call. */
    void    (*close)    (struct Cursor *cur);
} Cursor;
/* Mock cursor factory — used by tests only */
typedef struct {
    Value **rows;       /* rows[r][c] = cell value                     */
    int     row_count;
    int     col_count;
    int     pos;        /* current position; -1 = before first row     */
} MockCursorData;
Cursor *mock_cursor_create(Value **rows, int row_count, int col_count);
void    mock_cursor_destroy(Cursor *cur);
#endif /* CURSOR_H */
```
The `Cursor` struct is a pure interface — it carries no data fields, only function pointers. The `MockCursorData` struct is allocated as a single heap block that embeds both the `Cursor` header and the private data, using a common C trick:
```c
/* tests/mock_cursor.c */
#include "cursor.h"
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
typedef struct {
    Cursor         iface;     /* MUST be first — cast Cursor* ↔ MockImpl* */
    MockCursorData data;
} MockImpl;
static int   mock_rewind(Cursor *c) {
    MockImpl *m = (MockImpl *)c;
    m->data.pos = 0;
    return (m->data.row_count == 0) ? 1 : 0;
}
static int   mock_next(Cursor *c) {
    MockImpl *m = (MockImpl *)c;
    m->data.pos++;
    return (m->data.pos < m->data.row_count) ? 1 : 0;
}
static void  mock_column(Cursor *c, int col_idx, Value *dest) {
    MockImpl *m = (MockImpl *)c;
    *dest = m->data.rows[m->data.pos][col_idx];
}
static void  mock_rowid(Cursor *c, int64_t *out) {
    MockImpl *m = (MockImpl *)c;
    *out = (int64_t)m->data.pos + 1;
}
static int   mock_insert(Cursor *c, int64_t key,
                          const Value *record, int ncols) {
    (void)c; (void)key; (void)record; (void)ncols;
    return 0;  /* no-op for read-only mock */
}
static int64_t mock_max_rowid(Cursor *c) {
    MockImpl *m = (MockImpl *)c;
    return (int64_t)m->data.row_count;
}
static void  mock_close(Cursor *c) {
    free(c);  /* MockImpl is one allocation */
}
Cursor *mock_cursor_create(Value **rows, int row_count, int col_count) {
    MockImpl *m = calloc(1, sizeof(MockImpl));
    m->iface.rewind    = mock_rewind;
    m->iface.next      = mock_next;
    m->iface.column    = mock_column;
    m->iface.rowid     = mock_rowid;
    m->iface.insert    = mock_insert;
    m->iface.max_rowid = mock_max_rowid;
    m->iface.close     = mock_close;
    m->data.rows       = rows;
    m->data.row_count  = row_count;
    m->data.col_count  = col_count;
    m->data.pos        = -1;
    return (Cursor *)m;
}
void mock_cursor_destroy(Cursor *cur) {
    if (cur) cur->close(cur);
}
```
---
## 5. Public API Contracts (`src/vdbe.h`, continued)
### 5.1 Program Lifecycle
```c
/* Allocate and zero-initialise a Program. Returns NULL on OOM (fatal in tests). */
Program *program_create(void);
/* Free all instructions and string pool entries. */
void program_free(Program *prog);
/* Print human-readable opcode listing to stdout (EXPLAIN output). */
void program_explain(const Program *prog);
```
`program_explain` output format per instruction:
```
addr  opcode          p1      p2      p3      comment
----  --------------  ------  ------  ------  ----------------------------
   0  Init             0       3       0      Start
   1  OpenRead         0       5       2      table=t
   2  Rewind           0       8       0
   3  Column           0       0       1      col=a
   4  Column           0       1       2      col=b
   5  ResultRow        1       2       0      Output row
   6  Next             0       3       0      Advance cursor
   7  Close            0       0       0
   8  Halt             0       0       0      Done
```
### 5.2 Compiler
```c
/* CompileContext: caller fills this before calling compiler_init */
typedef struct {
    const char     **table_names;
    int             *root_pages;
    int             *col_counts;
    const char    ***col_names;     /* col_names[tbl_idx][col_idx] */
    int              table_count;
} CompileContext;
/* Initialise compiler. program must already be allocated. */
void compiler_init(Compiler *c, Program *prog, const CompileContext *ctx);
/* Compile one AstNode (SELECT, INSERT, CREATE TABLE). Returns 0 on success. */
int compiler_compile(Compiler *c, const AstNode *stmt);
/* Returns pointer to static error string; empty string if no error. */
const char *compiler_error(const Compiler *c);
```
**Preconditions for `compiler_compile`:**
- `stmt` is non-NULL and its `type` is `NODE_SELECT`, `NODE_INSERT`, or `NODE_CREATE_TABLE`.
- All table names referenced in `stmt` appear in `ctx->table_names[]`.
- All column names referenced in the projection or WHERE clause appear in the corresponding `ctx->col_names[tbl_idx][]`.
**Postconditions:**
- `prog->ops[0].opcode == OP_INIT`.
- `prog->ops[prog->op_count - 1].opcode == OP_HALT`.
- Every `OP_REWIND.p2` and every comparison jump `.p2` is a valid instruction index in `[0, prog->op_count)`.
**Error return:** Returns `-1` and sets `c->had_error` on: unknown table name, unknown column name, unsupported AST node type. Returns `0` on success.
### 5.3 VM
```c
/* Initialise VM. prog must remain valid for the lifetime of the VM. */
void vm_init(VM *vm, Program *prog);
/* Set the result callback before calling vm_execute. */
void vm_set_result_callback(VM *vm,
    void (*cb)(Value *row, int ncols, void *ctx), void *ctx);
/* Attach a Cursor to slot cursor_id (called by test code that pre-populates
   cursors before execution; in M5+ integration, OP_OPEN_READ calls pager). */
void vm_attach_cursor(VM *vm, int cursor_id, Cursor *cur);
/* Execute the program until OP_HALT or error. Returns halt_code (0 = SQLITE_OK). */
int vm_execute(VM *vm);
/* Release register string memory and zero all fields. Does NOT free vm itself. */
void vm_destroy(VM *vm);
```
**`vm_execute` return codes:**
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | SQLITE_OK — clean halt |
| 1 | SQLITE_ERROR — generic runtime error |
| 19 | SQLITE_CONSTRAINT — NOT NULL violated |
| 21 | SQLITE_MISUSE — pc out of range |
---
## 6. Algorithm Specifications
### 6.1 `emit()` — Instruction Appender
```c
/* Internal to vdbe.c. Returns the instruction address (0-based index). */
static int emit(Program *prog, Opcode op, int p1, int p2, int p3,
                const char *comment_fmt, ...);
```
**Procedure:**
1. If `prog->op_count >= prog->op_capacity`, double `op_capacity` and `realloc` `prog->ops`. Initial capacity: 64.
2. Write `ops[op_count] = { op, p1, p2, p3, NULL }`.
3. If `VDBE_TRACE` is defined and `comment_fmt != NULL`, format the string via `vasprintf` and assign to `ops[op_count].comment`.
4. Increment `op_count`.
5. Return the address of the newly written instruction (`op_count - 1` after increment).
**Invariant after every `emit` call:** `ops[0..op_count-1]` are fully initialised; `ops[op_count..capacity-1]` are uninitialised but the `realloc` has provided the memory.
### 6.2 `alloc_register()` — Linear Scan Allocator
```c
static int alloc_register(Compiler *c);
```
**Procedure:**
1. `int reg = c->next_register++`.
2. Assert `reg < VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS` (debug) or set `c->had_error = 1` and return -1 (release).
3. Return `reg`.
**Design rationale:** The compiler never frees registers within a single statement compilation. Each subexpression gets a unique register. With a 256-register budget and SQL queries that rarely need more than 20 intermediate values, register pressure is never a concern. This eliminates all register clobbering bugs at the cost of a constant 6 KB register file regardless of actual usage.
### 6.3 `compile_expr()` — Post-Order Expression Emission

![Register File Memory Layout](./diagrams/tdd-diag-8.svg)

*Figure 8: Post-order AST traversal for expression `a + b * 2`. The tree is walked left-child → right-child → current-node. Leaf nodes emit OP_COLUMN or OP_INTEGER. Internal nodes emit the corresponding arithmetic opcode with a freshly allocated result register. Register assignments are annotated at each node.*
```c
/* Compile one expression subtree. Returns the register holding the result,
   or -1 on error. cursor_id is used for OP_COLUMN lookups. */
static int compile_expr(Compiler *c, const AstNode *expr,
                        int cursor_id, int table_idx);
```
**Step-by-step procedure:**
| Case | Action |
|------|--------|
| `NODE_INTEGER_LIT` | `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(OP_INTEGER, (int)expr->as.integer_lit.value, r, 0)`; return `r` |
| `NODE_REAL_LIT` | Add to `prog->reals[]`; `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(OP_REAL, pool_idx, r, 0)`; return `r` |
| `NODE_STRING_LIT` | Strip surrounding quotes; add to `prog->strings[]` via `strdup`; `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(OP_STRING8, pool_idx, r, 0)`; return `r` |
| `NODE_COLUMN_REF` | Linear search `c->table_col_names[table_idx][]` for name; error if not found; `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(OP_COLUMN, cursor_id, col_idx, r)`; return `r` |
| `NODE_STAR` | Emit `OP_COLUMN` for each column 0..n-1; return first register (caller uses count from compiler context) |
| `NODE_BINARY_OP` | Check constant folding (see below); else recurse left → `rl`, right → `rr`; `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(arithmetic_opcode, rl, rr, r)`; return `r` |
| `NODE_UNARY_OP` (negation) | Recurse operand → `ro`; `r = alloc_register(c)`; `emit(OP_INTEGER, 0, r, 0)`; `emit(OP_SUBTRACT, r, ro, r)`; return `r` |
| Unknown type | Set `c->had_error = 1`; `snprintf(c->error_msg, ...)`; return `-1` |
**Constant folding (required by M3-TS-02):**
Before recursing on a binary op, check if both children are `NODE_INTEGER_LIT`:
```c
if (left->type == NODE_INTEGER_LIT && right->type == NODE_INTEGER_LIT) {
    int64_t lv = left->as.integer_lit.value;
    int64_t rv = right->as.integer_lit.value;
    int64_t result;
    if      (strcmp(op, "+") == 0) result = lv + rv;
    else if (strcmp(op, "-") == 0) result = lv - rv;
    else if (strcmp(op, "*") == 0) result = lv * rv;
    else if (strcmp(op, "/") == 0) {
        if (rv == 0) {
            c->had_error = 1;
            snprintf(c->error_msg, sizeof(c->error_msg),
                     "Division by zero in constant expression");
            emit(prog, OP_HALT, 1, 0, 0, "div-by-zero");
            return -1;
        }
        result = lv / rv;
    }
    else if (strcmp(op, "%") == 0) {
        if (rv == 0) { c->had_error = 1; return -1; }
        result = lv % rv;
    }
    else goto no_fold;
    int r = alloc_register(c);
    emit(prog, OP_INTEGER, (int)result, r, 0, "folded constant");
    return r;
}
no_fold:;
```
Division by zero at compile time (both literals): emit `OP_HALT` with code 1, set `c->had_error`, return -1.
### 6.4 `compile_where()` — Conditional Jump Emission with Backpatching

![AST-to-Bytecode Compilation Sequence](./diagrams/tdd-diag-9.svg)

*Figure 9: Backpatching protocol for WHERE `id = 42`. Phase A emits OP_Column + OP_Integer + a placeholder jump (OP_NE with p2=0). Phase B, after emitting OP_NEXT, patches the placeholder's p2 field to point at OP_NEXT's address. The integer returned by emit() is the "patch handle" stored in the compiler for later use.*
```c
/* Compile a WHERE clause expression and emit a jump that skips the current
   row when the condition is FALSE. jump_addrs[] is filled with the addresses
   of all emitted conditional jumps (including NULL guards); *n_jumps is set
   to the count. All jumps use placeholder target 0 — caller must patch them.
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on error.                                         */
static int compile_where(Compiler *c, const AstNode *where,
                         int cursor_id, int table_idx,
                         int *jump_addrs, int *n_jumps);
```
**Procedure for a simple comparison (`NODE_BINARY_OP` with `=`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`):**
1. Recursively compile left operand → register `rl`.
2. Recursively compile right operand → register `rr`.
3. Emit NULL guards (required for three-valued logic):
   - `null_addr1 = emit(OP_ISNULL, rl, 0 /*placeholder*/, 0, "NULL guard left")`.
   - `null_addr2 = emit(OP_ISNULL, rr, 0 /*placeholder*/, 0, "NULL guard right")`.
   - Append both to `jump_addrs[]`.
4. Determine the *inverted* opcode (emit jump when condition is **FALSE** → row is skipped):
   | WHERE op | Emitted jump | Rationale |
   |----------|-------------|-----------|
   | `=` | `OP_NE` | skip row if NOT equal |
   | `!=` | `OP_EQ` | skip row if equal |
   | `<` | `OP_GE` | skip row if NOT less |
   | `<=` | `OP_GT` | skip row if NOT less-or-equal |
   | `>` | `OP_LE` | skip row if NOT greater |
   | `>=` | `OP_LT` | skip row if NOT greater-or-equal |
5. `cmp_addr = emit(prog, inverted_op, rl, 0 /*placeholder*/, rr, "WHERE filter")`.
6. Append `cmp_addr` to `jump_addrs[]`. Set `*n_jumps`.
7. Return 0.
**Procedure for `NODE_AND`:**
1. Compile left sub-condition → `n_left` jumps in `jump_addrs[0..n_left-1]`.
2. Compile right sub-condition → `n_right` jumps in `jump_addrs[n_left..n_left+n_right-1]`.
3. All jumps (from both halves) target `OP_NEXT` — patched by caller.
4. Set `*n_jumps = n_left + n_right`. Return 0.
**Procedure for `NODE_OR`:**
1. Compile left condition in *non-inverted* form → "jump to ResultRow if TRUE" (emit the non-inverted opcode that jumps when condition holds).
2. Record `or_pass_addr` = address of that jump (placeholder target = ResultRow).
3. Compile right condition in inverted form (skip-to-NEXT if FALSE).
4. Patch `or_pass_addr` to point to the instruction immediately after right's skip-jump (past the filter block, into ResultRow territory).
5. Return the skip-jump address of the right condition.
**Backpatch call site (inside `compile_select`):**
```c
static void patch_jump(Program *prog, int jump_addr, int target_addr) {
    prog->ops[jump_addr].p2 = target_addr;
}
```
### 6.5 `compile_select()` — Full Statement Emission
```c
static int compile_select(Compiler *c, const AstNode *stmt);
```
**Emitted instruction sequence for `SELECT col1, col2 FROM t WHERE condition`:**
| Addr | Opcode | Operands | Description |
|------|--------|----------|-------------|
| 0 | `OP_INIT` | 0, open_addr, 0 | Jump to OP_OPEN_READ |
| 1 | `OP_OPEN_READ` | cursor, root_page, n_cols | Open read cursor |
| 2 | `OP_REWIND` | cursor, close_addr, 0 | Go to first row; jump to OP_CLOSE if empty |
| 3 | (loop_start) WHERE opcodes | — | NULL guards + inverted comparison jump |
| k | `OP_COLUMN` × n_proj | cursor, col_idx, reg | Load projection columns |
| k+n | `OP_RESULT_ROW` | first_reg, n_proj, 0 | Emit one output row |
| k+n+1 | `OP_NEXT` | cursor, loop_start, 0 | Advance; jump back if more rows |
| k+n+2 | `OP_CLOSE` | cursor, 0, 0 | (also REWIND empty-jump target) |
| k+n+3 | `OP_HALT` | 0, 0, 0 | |
**Step-by-step procedure:**
1. `init_addr = emit(OP_INIT, 0, 0, 0, "Start")` — p2 placeholder; will be patched to `open_addr`.
2. Look up `table_idx` by scanning `c->table_names[]` for `stmt->as.select.table_name`. If not found: set `c->had_error`, return -1.
3. `cursor_id = c->next_cursor++`.
4. `open_addr = emit(OP_OPEN_READ, cursor_id, root_page, n_cols, table_name)`.
5. `patch_jump(prog, init_addr, open_addr)`.
6. `rewind_addr = emit(OP_REWIND, cursor_id, 0 /*empty-table placeholder*/, 0, "Rewind")`.
7. Record `loop_start = prog->op_count` — this is the address of the very next instruction (first WHERE opcode, or first OP_COLUMN if no WHERE).
8. If `stmt->as.select.where_clause != NULL`:
   - Declare `int where_jumps[64]; int n_where_jumps = 0;`
   - Call `compile_where(c, where_clause, cursor_id, table_idx, where_jumps, &n_where_jumps)`.
   - On error, propagate -1.
9. Determine the projection list. If `NODE_STAR`, expand to all columns 0..n_cols-1. Record `first_result_reg = c->next_register`.
10. For each projected column, `emit(OP_COLUMN, cursor_id, col_idx, alloc_register(c))`.
11. `emit(OP_RESULT_ROW, first_result_reg, n_proj_cols, 0, "Output row")`.
12. `next_addr = emit(OP_NEXT, cursor_id, loop_start, 0, "Advance cursor")`.
13. For each jump in `where_jumps[]`: `patch_jump(prog, where_jumps[i], next_addr)`.
14. `close_addr = emit(OP_CLOSE, cursor_id, 0, 0, "Close cursor")`.
15. `patch_jump(prog, rewind_addr, close_addr)` — empty table skips loop entirely.
16. `emit(OP_HALT, 0, 0, 0, "Done")`.
17. `prog->n_registers = c->next_register`.
**Critical ordering rule:** Steps 7 (record `loop_start`) and 12 (emit `OP_NEXT`) must be in this order. The WHERE jumps are patched in step 13, *after* `OP_NEXT` is emitted so `next_addr` is known. Any inversion of this order produces a WHERE jump that points into the projection block instead of `OP_NEXT`.
### 6.6 `compile_insert()` — DML Emission
**Emitted instruction sequence for `INSERT INTO t VALUES (expr1, expr2)`:**
| Addr | Opcode | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 0 | `OP_INIT` | jump to OP_OPEN_WRITE |
| 1 | `OP_OPEN_WRITE` | cursor=0, root_page, n_cols |
| 2 | `OP_NEW_ROWID` | cursor=0 → reg[r_key] |
| 3..m | compile each value expr | regs r_v0..r_vn |
| m+1 | `OP_NOT_NULL` × (per NOT NULL col) | constraint check |
| m+2 | `OP_MAKE_RECORD` | p1=r_v0, p2=n_cols, p3=r_rec |
| m+3 | `OP_INSERT` | cursor=0, record=r_rec, key=r_key |
| m+4 | `OP_CLOSE` | cursor=0 |
| m+5 | `OP_HALT` | p1=0 |
**Step-by-step procedure:**
1. `init_addr = emit(OP_INIT, 0, 0, 0, "Start")`.
2. Look up `table_idx`. If not found: error.
3. `cursor_id = c->next_cursor++`.
4. `open_addr = emit(OP_OPEN_WRITE, cursor_id, root_page, n_cols, table_name)`.
5. `patch_jump(prog, init_addr, open_addr)`.
6. `r_key = alloc_register(c)`. `emit(OP_NEW_ROWID, cursor_id, r_key, 0, "new rowid")`.
7. `first_val_reg = c->next_register`. For each value expression in `stmt->as.insert.values[]`: call `compile_expr(c, val_expr, cursor_id, table_idx)`.
8. For each column marked NOT NULL in the schema: `emit(OP_NOT_NULL, first_val_reg + col_idx, 0, 0, "constraint")`.
9. `r_rec = alloc_register(c)`. `emit(OP_MAKE_RECORD, first_val_reg, n_vals, r_rec, "pack record")`.
10. `emit(OP_INSERT, cursor_id, r_rec, r_key, "insert row")`.
11. `emit(OP_CLOSE, cursor_id, 0, 0, "close")`.
12. `emit(OP_HALT, 0, 0, 0, "done")`.
13. `prog->n_registers = c->next_register`.
### 6.7 VM Fetch-Decode-Execute Loop

![VM Execution Trace for SELECT with WHERE](./diagrams/tdd-diag-10.svg)

*Figure 10: VM execution state machine. States: UNINIT → READY (after vm_init) → RUNNING (during vm_execute) → HALTED_OK (OP_HALT p1=0) or HALTED_ERROR (OP_HALT p1≠0 or runtime fault). Each cycle: Fetch ops[pc], Decode opcode via switch, Execute handler, Increment pc (unless a jump or HALT fires). The register file (256 × Value structs) is shown as a vertical array annotated with the values populated during a SELECT example program.*
```c
int vm_execute(VM *vm) {
    while (!vm->halted && vm->pc < vm->program->op_count) {
        Instruction *op = &vm->program->ops[vm->pc];
        switch (op->opcode) {
            case OP_INIT:
                vm->pc = op->p2;
                continue;       /* skip pc++ at bottom */
            case OP_HALT:
                vm->halted    = true;
                vm->halt_code = op->p1;
                return op->p1;
            case OP_GOTO:
                vm->pc = op->p2;
                continue;
            case OP_OPEN_READ:
            case OP_OPEN_WRITE:
                /* Cursor must already be attached by caller via vm_attach_cursor.
                   In M5+ integration this opcode calls pager_fetch_btree().      */
                break;
            case OP_CLOSE:
                if (vm->cursors[op->p1]) {
                    vm->cursors[op->p1]->close(vm->cursors[op->p1]);
                    vm->cursors[op->p1] = NULL;
                }
                break;
            case OP_REWIND: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                int empty = cur->rewind(cur);
                if (empty) { vm->pc = op->p2; continue; }
                break;
            }
            case OP_NEXT: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                int has_more = cur->next(cur);
                if (has_more) { vm->pc = op->p2; continue; }
                break;
            }
            case OP_COLUMN: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                cur->column(cur, op->p2, &vm->registers[op->p3]);
                break;
            }
            case OP_ROWID: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                cur->rowid(cur, &vm->registers[op->p2].u.i);
                vm->registers[op->p2].type = VAL_INTEGER;
                break;
            }
            case OP_RESULT_ROW:
                if (vm->result_cb)
                    vm->result_cb(&vm->registers[op->p1], op->p2, vm->result_ctx);
                break;
            case OP_INTEGER:
                vm->registers[op->p2].type = VAL_INTEGER;
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.i  = (int64_t)op->p1;
                break;
            case OP_STRING8:
                vm->registers[op->p2].type       = VAL_STRING;
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.s.data   = vm->program->strings[op->p1];
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.s.len    =
                    (int)strlen(vm->program->strings[op->p1]);
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.s.owned  = 0;  /* borrowed from pool */
                break;
            case OP_NULL:
                vm->registers[op->p2].type = VAL_NULL;
                break;
            case OP_REAL:
                vm->registers[op->p2].type = VAL_REAL;
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.r  = vm->program->reals[op->p1];
                break;
            case OP_EQ: case OP_NE: case OP_LT:
            case OP_LE: case OP_GT: case OP_GE: {
                bool result = vm_compare(&vm->registers[op->p1],
                                          &vm->registers[op->p3],
                                          op->opcode);
                if (result) { vm->pc = op->p2; continue; }
                break;
            }
            case OP_ISNULL:
                if (vm->registers[op->p1].type == VAL_NULL)
                    { vm->pc = op->p2; continue; }
                break;
            case OP_NOTNULL:
                if (vm->registers[op->p1].type != VAL_NULL)
                    { vm->pc = op->p2; continue; }
                break;
            case OP_ADD: case OP_SUBTRACT:
            case OP_MULTIPLY: case OP_DIVIDE: case OP_MODULO:
                vm_arithmetic(&vm->registers[op->p1], &vm->registers[op->p2],
                               &vm->registers[op->p3], op->opcode, vm);
                if (vm->halted) return vm->halt_code;
                break;
            case OP_COPY: {
                Value *src = &vm->registers[op->p1];
                Value *dst = &vm->registers[op->p2];
                /* Free old owned string in dst */
                if (dst->type == VAL_STRING && dst->u.s.owned)
                    free(dst->u.s.data);
                *dst = *src;
                if (src->type == VAL_STRING && src->u.s.data) {
                    dst->u.s.data  = malloc(src->u.s.len + 1);
                    memcpy(dst->u.s.data, src->u.s.data, src->u.s.len + 1);
                    dst->u.s.owned = 1;
                }
                break;
            }
            case OP_SCOPY:
                vm->registers[op->p2] = vm->registers[op->p1];
                if (vm->registers[op->p2].type == VAL_STRING)
                    vm->registers[op->p2].u.s.owned = 0;  /* shallow: borrow */
                break;
            case OP_NOT_NULL:
                if (vm->registers[op->p1].type == VAL_NULL) {
                    vm->halted    = true;
                    vm->halt_code = 19;
                    snprintf(vm->error_msg, sizeof(vm->error_msg),
                             "NOT NULL constraint failed: column %d", op->p1);
                    return 19;
                }
                break;
            case OP_NEW_ROWID: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                vm->registers[op->p2].type = VAL_INTEGER;
                vm->registers[op->p2].u.i  = cur->max_rowid(cur) + 1;
                break;
            }
            case OP_MAKE_RECORD:
                /* Pack registers p1..p1+p2-1 into a BLOB record in reg[p3].
                   Simple format for M3: each cell prefixed by 1-byte type tag.
                   Full SQLite varint record format deferred to M5.            */
                vm->registers[op->p3].type = VAL_BLOB;
                /* (implementation detail: allocate a buffer and serialise) */
                break;
            case OP_INSERT: {
                Cursor *cur = vm->cursors[op->p1];
                int64_t key = vm->registers[op->p3].u.i;
                cur->insert(cur, key, &vm->registers[op->p2], 1);
                break;
            }
            default:
                snprintf(vm->error_msg, sizeof(vm->error_msg),
                         "Unknown opcode %d at pc=%d", op->opcode, vm->pc);
                vm->halted    = true;
                vm->halt_code = 1;
                return 1;
        }
        vm->pc++;
    }
    /* Fell off the end without OP_HALT — treat as SQLITE_MISUSE */
    if (!vm->halted) {
        vm->halt_code = 21;
        snprintf(vm->error_msg, sizeof(vm->error_msg),
                 "Program ended without OP_HALT at pc=%d", vm->pc);
    }
    return vm->halt_code;
}
```
**`vm_compare` helper:**
```c
static bool vm_compare(const Value *a, const Value *b, Opcode op) {
    /* NULL comparisons always return false (three-valued logic) */
    if (a->type == VAL_NULL || b->type == VAL_NULL) return false;
    int cmp = 0;
    if (a->type == VAL_INTEGER && b->type == VAL_INTEGER) {
        cmp = (a->u.i > b->u.i) - (a->u.i < b->u.i);
    } else if (a->type == VAL_REAL || b->type == VAL_REAL) {
        double da = (a->type == VAL_REAL) ? a->u.r : (double)a->u.i;
        double db = (b->type == VAL_REAL) ? b->u.r : (double)b->u.i;
        cmp = (da > db) - (da < db);
    } else if (a->type == VAL_STRING && b->type == VAL_STRING) {
        int min_len = a->u.s.len < b->u.s.len ? a->u.s.len : b->u.s.len;
        cmp = strncmp(a->u.s.data, b->u.s.data, min_len);
        if (cmp == 0) cmp = a->u.s.len - b->u.s.len;
    }
    switch (op) {
        case OP_EQ: return cmp == 0;
        case OP_NE: return cmp != 0;
        case OP_LT: return cmp <  0;
        case OP_LE: return cmp <= 0;
        case OP_GT: return cmp >  0;
        case OP_GE: return cmp >= 0;
        default:    return false;
    }
}
```
**`vm_arithmetic` helper:**
```c
static void vm_arithmetic(const Value *a, const Value *b, Value *dest,
                           Opcode op, VM *vm) {
    bool both_int = (a->type == VAL_INTEGER && b->type == VAL_INTEGER);
    double da = (a->type == VAL_REAL) ? a->u.r : (double)a->u.i;
    double db = (b->type == VAL_REAL) ? b->u.r : (double)b->u.i;
    if (op == OP_DIVIDE && db == 0.0) {
        vm->halted    = true;
        vm->halt_code = 1;
        snprintf(vm->error_msg, sizeof(vm->error_msg), "division by zero");
        return;
    }
    if (op == OP_MODULO && b->u.i == 0 && both_int) {
        vm->halted    = true;
        vm->halt_code = 1;
        snprintf(vm->error_msg, sizeof(vm->error_msg), "modulo by zero");
        return;
    }
    if (both_int && (op == OP_ADD || op == OP_SUBTRACT ||
                     op == OP_MULTIPLY || op == OP_MODULO)) {
        dest->type = VAL_INTEGER;
        switch (op) {
            case OP_ADD:      dest->u.i = a->u.i + b->u.i; break;
            case OP_SUBTRACT: dest->u.i = a->u.i - b->u.i; break;
            case OP_MULTIPLY: dest->u.i = a->u.i * b->u.i; break;
            case OP_MODULO:   dest->u.i = a->u.i % b->u.i; break;
            default: break;
        }
    } else {
        dest->type = VAL_REAL;
        switch (op) {
            case OP_ADD:      dest->u.r = da + db; break;
            case OP_SUBTRACT: dest->u.r = da - db; break;
            case OP_MULTIPLY: dest->u.r = da * db; break;
            case OP_DIVIDE:   dest->u.r = da / db; break;
            case OP_MODULO:   dest->u.r = fmod(da, db); break;
            default: break;
        }
    }
}
```
---
## 7. Error Handling Matrix
| Error | Detected By | Action | halt_code | Recovery |
|-------|-------------|--------|-----------|---------|
| Unknown table name | `compile_select` / `compile_insert` | `c->had_error = 1`; emit `OP_HALT(1)`; return -1 | 1 | Caller checks `compiler_error()` |
| Unknown column name | `compile_expr` (NODE_COLUMN_REF) | `c->had_error = 1`; return -1 | 1 | Caller checks `compiler_error()` |
| Register exhaustion | `alloc_register` | assert in debug; `c->had_error = 1` in release; return -1 | 1 | Reduce query complexity |
| Division by zero (literal) | `compile_expr` constant fold | `c->had_error = 1`; emit `OP_HALT(1)`; return -1 | — | Fix query |
| Division by zero (runtime) | `vm_arithmetic` | `vm->halted = true; halt_code = 1` | 1 | `vm->error_msg` describes location |
| NOT NULL constraint violated | `OP_NOT_NULL` handler | `halted = true; halt_code = 19` | 19 | Caller checks return value |
| Invalid register index | `reg()` guard | assert in debug; return NULL sentinel in release | — | Do not access out-of-range registers |
| Cursor not attached | any cursor opcode | null-pointer dereference in debug; `VAL_NULL` / no-op in release | — | Always call `vm_attach_cursor` before `vm_execute` |
| String pool overflow | `add_string_constant` | `c->had_error = 1`; return -1 | — | Increase `VDBE_MAX_STRINGS` |
| Instruction buffer OOM | `emit` via `realloc` | `fprintf(stderr, "OOM\n"); exit(1)` | — | Fatal — cannot recover |
| Program ends without `OP_HALT` | `vm_execute` loop exit | `halt_code = 21` (SQLITE_MISUSE) | 21 | Ensure compiler always emits `OP_HALT` |
**Register access guard (Spec 5 requirement):**
```c
static inline Value *reg(VM *vm, int idx) {
#ifdef NDEBUG
    if (idx < 0 || idx >= VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS) {
        static Value null_val = { .type = VAL_NULL };
        return &null_val;
    }
#else
    assert(idx >= 0 && idx < VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS &&
           "Register index out of bounds");
#endif
    return &vm->registers[idx];
}
```
In debug builds (`-g`, `-UNDEBUG`), out-of-range access triggers `assert` and aborts with a descriptive message. In release builds (`-DNDEBUG`), the guard returns a pointer to a static NULL-valued sentinel. The sentinel is `static` — never freed, never mutated by the caller.
---
## 8. State Machine — Program Counter Lifecycle
```
States:
  UNINIT        → vm_init() called                    → READY
  READY         → vm_execute() called                 → RUNNING
  RUNNING       → OP_HALT reached (halt_code == 0)    → HALTED_OK
  RUNNING       → OP_HALT reached (halt_code != 0)    → HALTED_ERROR
  RUNNING       → runtime div-by-zero                 → HALTED_ERROR
  RUNNING       → OP_NOT_NULL on NULL register        → HALTED_CONSTRAINT
  RUNNING       → pc falls off end of ops[]           → HALTED_MISUSE
  HALTED_*      → vm_destroy()                        → (memory released)
Illegal transitions:
  vm_execute() while RUNNING (re-entrant call)        → undefined behaviour
  vm_execute() after any HALTED state                 → returns previous halt_code immediately
  Accessing cursor after OP_CLOSE                     → NULL dereference
  Calling vm_attach_cursor after vm_execute starts    → undefined behaviour (no lock)
```
---
## 9. Implementation Sequence with Checkpoints
### Phase 1 — Opcode Enum and Instruction Struct (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `src/vdbe.h` complete with `Opcode` enum, `Instruction`, `Value`, `Program`, `Compiler`, `VM` struct definitions. All public function declarations. `src/cursor.h` with abstract `Cursor` struct. `tests/mock_cursor.c` with mock implementation.
**Implementation order:**
1. Define `Opcode` enum with all 35 values (OP_INIT=0 … OP_COUNT=35).
2. Define `Instruction` struct; add `_Static_assert(sizeof(Instruction) == 24, "Instruction size mismatch")`.
3. Define `Value` tagged union; add `_Static_assert(sizeof(Value) == 24, "Value size mismatch")`.
4. Define `Program` struct with fixed-size string/real pools and dynamic `ops` array.
5. Define `Compiler` and `VM` structs.
6. Write `program_create()` (calloc + set `op_capacity = 64`, alloc `ops`), `program_free()`, `program_explain()` stubs (empty body, returns immediately).
7. Implement `mock_cursor_create()` and `mock_cursor_destroy()` in `tests/mock_cursor.c` per the code in §4.
**Checkpoint:** The following compiles and passes with `-Wall -Wextra -std=c11 -fsanitize=address,undefined`:
```c
Program *p = program_create();
assert(p != NULL);
assert(p->op_count == 0);
assert(p->op_capacity == 64);
program_free(p);
/* Both static_asserts must compile without error */
_Static_assert(sizeof(Instruction) == 24, "ok");
_Static_assert(sizeof(Value) == 24, "ok");
```
---
### Phase 2 — Compiler: Expression Emission and Register Allocator (3 hours)
**Deliverables:** `emit()`, `alloc_register()`, `add_string_constant()`, `add_real_constant()`, `compile_expr()` (all cases from §6.3), `compiler_init()`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `emit()` with dynamic capacity doubling; initial capacity 64; use `realloc`. Return instruction address.
2. Implement `alloc_register()` with debug assert; start `next_register` at 1 (reserve reg 0).
3. Implement `add_string_constant()` — iterate `prog->strings[]` to deduplicate by pointer equality; append via `strdup` if new; return pool index.
4. Implement `add_real_constant()` — deduplicate by value (bitwise exact); append if new; return pool index.
5. Implement `compile_expr()` for: integer literal, real literal, string literal, column ref, star, binary arithmetic with constant folding, unary negation.
6. Implement `compiler_init()` — zero `Compiler`, set `next_register = 1`, copy `ctx` pointers.
**Checkpoint (validates M3-TS-02):**
```c
/* Build AST manually: a + b * 2 */
AstNode *lit2 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT);
lit2->as.integer_lit.value = 2;
AstNode *col_b = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
col_b->as.column_ref.column = "b";
col_b->as.column_ref.column_len = 1;
AstNode *mul = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
strcpy(mul->as.binary_op.op, "*");
mul->as.binary_op.left = col_b; mul->as.binary_op.right = lit2;
AstNode *col_a = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
col_a->as.column_ref.column = "a";
col_a->as.column_ref.column_len = 1;
AstNode *add = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
strcpy(add->as.binary_op.op, "+");
add->as.binary_op.left = col_a; add->as.binary_op.right = mul;
const char *cols[] = {"a", "b"};
CompileContext ctx = {
    .table_names = (const char*[]){"t"},
    .root_pages  = (int[]){1},
    .col_counts  = (int[]){2},
    .col_names   = (const char**[]){cols},
    .table_count = 1
};
Program *prog = program_create();
Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
int r = compile_expr(&c, add, 0, 0);
assert(prog->op_count == 5);
assert(prog->ops[0].opcode == OP_COLUMN);  /* b at col 1 */
assert(prog->ops[0].p2 == 1);
assert(prog->ops[1].opcode == OP_INTEGER); /* literal 2 */
assert(prog->ops[1].p1 == 2);
assert(prog->ops[2].opcode == OP_MULTIPLY);
assert(prog->ops[3].opcode == OP_COLUMN);  /* a at col 0 */
assert(prog->ops[3].p2 == 0);
assert(prog->ops[4].opcode == OP_ADD);
assert(r == 5);  /* registers 1..5 allocated */
/* Constant folding: 2 + 3 → single OP_INTEGER(5) */
Program *p2 = program_create();
Compiler c2; compiler_init(&c2, p2, &ctx);
AstNode *l2 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); l2->as.integer_lit.value = 2;
AstNode *l3 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); l3->as.integer_lit.value = 3;
AstNode *fold = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
strcpy(fold->as.binary_op.op, "+");
fold->as.binary_op.left = l2; fold->as.binary_op.right = l3;
compile_expr(&c2, fold, 0, 0);
assert(p2->op_count == 1);
assert(p2->ops[0].opcode == OP_INTEGER);
assert(p2->ops[0].p1 == 5);
```
---
### Phase 3 — Compiler: Statement Emission with Backpatching (3 hours)
**Deliverables:** `patch_jump()`, `compile_where()`, `compile_select()`, `compile_insert()`, `compiler_compile()`, full `program_explain()`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `patch_jump(prog, jump_addr, target_addr)` — one-liner: `prog->ops[jump_addr].p2 = target_addr`.
2. Implement `compile_where()` for simple binary comparisons including NULL guards (§6.4).
3. Implement `compile_where()` for `NODE_AND` (iterate, collect all jumps).
4. Implement `compile_where()` for `NODE_OR` (short-circuit, §6.4).
5. Implement `compile_select()` following the 16-step procedure in §6.5.
6. Implement `compile_insert()` following §6.6.
7. Implement `compiler_compile()` as a dispatcher: `switch(stmt->type)` → `compile_select` / `compile_insert` / `compile_create_table`.
8. Implement `program_explain()` — iterate `ops[0..op_count-1]`; print opcode name from a static name table; print p1, p2, p3; print comment if non-NULL.
**Checkpoint (validates M3-TS-01 and M3-TS-03):**
```c
/* SELECT a, b FROM t */
Program *prog = program_create();
Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
/* ... parse and compile ... */
assert(prog->ops[0].opcode == OP_INIT);
assert(prog->ops[1].opcode == OP_OPEN_READ);
assert(prog->ops[1].p2 == 5);           /* root page */
assert(prog->ops[1].p3 == 2);           /* 2 columns */
int rewind_idx = 2;
assert(prog->ops[rewind_idx].opcode == OP_REWIND);
/* Find OP_CLOSE and OP_HALT */
int close_idx = prog->op_count - 2;
int halt_idx  = prog->op_count - 1;
assert(prog->ops[close_idx].opcode == OP_CLOSE);
assert(prog->ops[halt_idx].opcode  == OP_HALT);
/* REWIND empty-jump → CLOSE */
assert(prog->ops[rewind_idx].p2 == close_idx);
/* NEXT backward-jump → loop_start */
int next_idx = prog->op_count - 3;
assert(prog->ops[next_idx].opcode == OP_NEXT);
assert(prog->ops[next_idx].p2 == 3);   /* loop_start after REWIND */
/* WHERE id = 42 */
/* ... compile "SELECT id FROM t WHERE id = 42" ... */
int ne_addr = -1, next_addr2 = -1;
for (int i = 0; i < prog2->op_count; i++) {
    if (prog2->ops[i].opcode == OP_NE)   ne_addr   = i;
    if (prog2->ops[i].opcode == OP_NEXT) next_addr2 = i;
}
assert(ne_addr >= 0 && next_addr2 >= 0);
assert(prog2->ops[ne_addr].p2 == next_addr2); /* WHERE jump → OP_NEXT */
```
---
### Phase 4 — VM Interpreter (3 hours)
**Deliverables:** `vm_init()`, `vm_execute()` (all 35 opcodes), `vm_destroy()`, `vm_compare()`, `vm_arithmetic()`, `vm_attach_cursor()`, `vm_set_result_callback()`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `vm_init()` — `memset(vm, 0, sizeof(*vm)); vm->program = prog;` (`VAL_NULL == 0` ensures zero-init is correct).
2. Implement `vm_attach_cursor()` and `vm_set_result_callback()` — one-liner assignments.
3. Implement `vm_compare()` and `vm_arithmetic()` as static helpers.
4. Implement `vm_execute()` — the full switch statement from §6.7.
5. Implement `vm_destroy()`:
   ```c
   void vm_destroy(VM *vm) {
       for (int i = 0; i < VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS; i++) {
           Value *v = &vm->registers[i];
           if ((v->type == VAL_STRING || v->type == VAL_BLOB) && v->u.s.owned)
               free(v->u.s.data);
       }
       memset(vm, 0, sizeof(*vm));
   }
   ```
**Checkpoint (validates M3-TS-04):**
```c
/* Three-row mock: (1,"alice"),(2,"bob"),(3,"carol") */
/* ... setup tc, rows, cur as per test spec ... */
VM vm; vm_init(&vm, prog);
vm_attach_cursor(&vm, 0, cur);
vm_set_result_callback(&vm, collect_cb, &tc);
int rc = vm_execute(&vm);
assert(rc == 0);
assert(tc.collected_count == 3);
assert(tc.collected[0][0].u.i == 1);
assert(strncmp(tc.collected[0][1].u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
/* Empty table: REWIND should jump to CLOSE, zero result rows */
Cursor *empty = mock_cursor_create(NULL, 0, 2);
VM vm2; vm_init(&vm2, prog2);
vm_attach_cursor(&vm2, 0, empty);
vm_set_result_callback(&vm2, collect_cb, &tc2);
assert(vm_execute(&vm2) == 0);
assert(tc2.collected_count == 0);
```
---
## 10. Test Specifications
### M3-TS-01 — SELECT Column List Opcodes
```c
void test_compile_select_column_list(void) {
    const char *col_names[] = {"a", "b"};
    CompileContext ctx = {
        .table_names = (const char*[]){"t"},
        .root_pages  = (int[]){5},
        .col_counts  = (int[]){2},
        .col_names   = (const char**[]){col_names},
        .table_count = 1
    };
    Program *prog = program_create();
    Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
    int tok_count;
    Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all("SELECT a, b FROM t", &tok_count);
    Parser p; parser_init(&p, toks, tok_count);
    AstNode *ast = parser_parse(&p);
    assert(ast != NULL);
    assert(compiler_compile(&c, ast) == 0);
    assert(!c.had_error);
    /* First instruction is OP_INIT */
    assert(prog->ops[0].opcode == OP_INIT);
    /* Second is OP_OPEN_READ on cursor 0, root page 5, 2 cols */
    assert(prog->ops[1].opcode == OP_OPEN_READ);
    assert(prog->ops[1].p1 == 0);
    assert(prog->ops[1].p2 == 5);
    assert(prog->ops[1].p3 == 2);
    /* Locate both OP_COLUMN instructions */
    int col_a_addr = -1, col_b_addr = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < prog->op_count; i++) {
        if (prog->ops[i].opcode == OP_COLUMN) {
            if (prog->ops[i].p2 == 0) col_a_addr = i;
            if (prog->ops[i].p2 == 1) col_b_addr = i;
        }
    }
    assert(col_a_addr >= 0 && col_b_addr >= 0);
    assert(col_b_addr == col_a_addr + 1);
    /* OP_RESULT_ROW immediately follows with ncols=2 */
    assert(prog->ops[col_b_addr + 1].opcode == OP_RESULT_ROW);
    assert(prog->ops[col_b_addr + 1].p2 == 2);
    /* Final instruction is OP_HALT */
    assert(prog->ops[prog->op_count - 1].opcode == OP_HALT);
    /* SELECT * expands to all columns */
    Program *prog2 = program_create();
    Compiler c2; compiler_init(&c2, prog2, &ctx);
    Token *toks2 = lexer_tokenize_all("SELECT * FROM t", &tok_count);
    Parser p2; parser_init(&p2, toks2, tok_count);
    AstNode *ast2 = parser_parse(&p2);
    compiler_compile(&c2, ast2);
    int star_col_count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < prog2->op_count; i++)
        if (prog2->ops[i].opcode == OP_COLUMN) star_col_count++;
    assert(star_col_count == 2);
    ast_free(ast); ast_free(ast2);
    program_free(prog); program_free(prog2);
    free(toks); free(toks2);
    printf("PASS: test_compile_select_column_list\n");
}
```
### M3-TS-02 — Arithmetic Expression Opcodes (Post-Order + Constant Folding)
```c
void test_compile_arithmetic_expression(void) {
    const char *col_names[] = {"a", "b"};
    CompileContext ctx = {
        .table_names = (const char*[]){"t"},
        .root_pages  = (int[]){1},
        .col_counts  = (int[]){2},
        .col_names   = (const char**[]){col_names},
        .table_count = 1
    };
    /* Case 1: post-order traversal of a + b * 2 */
```
