```c
    AstNode *lit2  = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT);
    lit2->as.integer_lit.value = 2;
    AstNode *col_b = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
    col_b->as.column_ref.column = "b"; col_b->as.column_ref.column_len = 1;
    AstNode *mul   = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
    strcpy(mul->as.binary_op.op, "*");
    mul->as.binary_op.left = col_b; mul->as.binary_op.right = lit2;
    AstNode *col_a = ast_node_alloc(NODE_COLUMN_REF);
    col_a->as.column_ref.column = "a"; col_a->as.column_ref.column_len = 1;
    AstNode *add   = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
    strcpy(add->as.binary_op.op, "+");
    add->as.binary_op.left = col_a; add->as.binary_op.right = mul;
    Program *prog = program_create();
    Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
    /* Call compile_expr directly (exposed via a test-build #define) */
    int result_reg = compile_expr(&c, add, 0, 0);
    assert(prog->op_count == 5);
    assert(prog->ops[0].opcode == OP_COLUMN);   /* b = col idx 1 */
    assert(prog->ops[0].p2 == 1);
    assert(prog->ops[1].opcode == OP_INTEGER);  /* 2 */
    assert(prog->ops[1].p1 == 2);
    assert(prog->ops[2].opcode == OP_MULTIPLY);
    assert(prog->ops[3].opcode == OP_COLUMN);   /* a = col idx 0 */
    assert(prog->ops[3].p2 == 0);
    assert(prog->ops[4].opcode == OP_ADD);
    assert(result_reg == 5);
    /* Case 2: constant folding — 2 + 3 → single OP_INTEGER(5) */
    Program *p2 = program_create();
    Compiler c2; compiler_init(&c2, p2, &ctx);
    AstNode *l2 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); l2->as.integer_lit.value = 2;
    AstNode *l3 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); l3->as.integer_lit.value = 3;
    AstNode *fold = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
    strcpy(fold->as.binary_op.op, "+");
    fold->as.binary_op.left = l2; fold->as.binary_op.right = l3;
    compile_expr(&c2, fold, 0, 0);
    assert(p2->op_count == 1);
    assert(p2->ops[0].opcode == OP_INTEGER);
    assert(p2->ops[0].p1 == 5);
    /* Case 3: division by literal zero → compiler error */
    Program *p3 = program_create();
    Compiler c3; compiler_init(&c3, p3, &ctx);
    AstNode *lx = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); lx->as.integer_lit.value = 1;
    AstNode *l0 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_INTEGER_LIT); l0->as.integer_lit.value = 0;
    AstNode *div0 = ast_node_alloc(NODE_BINARY_OP);
    strcpy(div0->as.binary_op.op, "/");
    div0->as.binary_op.left = lx; div0->as.binary_op.right = l0;
    int div_result = compile_expr(&c3, div0, 0, 0);
    assert(div_result == -1);
    assert(c3.had_error == 1);
    ast_free(add); ast_free(fold); ast_free(div0);
    program_free(prog); program_free(p2); program_free(p3);
    printf("PASS: test_compile_arithmetic_expression\n");
}
```
### M3-TS-03 — WHERE Clause Conditional Jump
```c
void test_compile_where_conditional_jump(void) {
    const char *col_names[] = {"id"};
    CompileContext ctx = {
        .table_names = (const char*[]){"t"},
        .root_pages  = (int[]){2},
        .col_counts  = (int[]){1},
        .col_names   = (const char**[]){col_names},
        .table_count = 1
    };
    /* Case 1: simple equality — SELECT id FROM t WHERE id = 42 */
    int tok_count;
    Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all("SELECT id FROM t WHERE id = 42", &tok_count);
    Parser p; parser_init(&p, toks, tok_count);
    AstNode *ast = parser_parse(&p);
    assert(ast != NULL);
    Program *prog = program_create();
    Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
    assert(compiler_compile(&c, ast) == 0);
    assert(!c.had_error);
    /* OP_NE (inverted from =) must jump to OP_NEXT */
    int ne_addr = -1, next_addr = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < prog->op_count; i++) {
        if (prog->ops[i].opcode == OP_NE)   ne_addr   = i;
        if (prog->ops[i].opcode == OP_NEXT) next_addr = i;
    }
    assert(ne_addr >= 0 && next_addr >= 0);
    assert(prog->ops[ne_addr].p2 == next_addr);
    /* ISNULL guards must precede the comparison */
    assert(prog->ops[ne_addr - 1].opcode == OP_ISNULL);
    assert(prog->ops[ne_addr - 2].opcode == OP_ISNULL);
    /* Both NULL guards also jump to OP_NEXT */
    assert(prog->ops[ne_addr - 1].p2 == next_addr);
    assert(prog->ops[ne_addr - 2].p2 == next_addr);
    /* Case 2: AND condition → at least two conditional jumps total */
    Token *toks2 = lexer_tokenize_all(
        "SELECT id FROM t WHERE id = 42 AND id > 0", &tok_count);
    Parser p2; parser_init(&p2, toks2, tok_count);
    AstNode *ast2 = parser_parse(&p2);
    Program *prog2 = program_create();
    Compiler c2; compiler_init(&c2, prog2, &ctx);
    compiler_compile(&c2, ast2);
    int cond_count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < prog2->op_count; i++) {
        Opcode op = prog2->ops[i].opcode;
        if (op == OP_EQ || op == OP_NE || op == OP_LT ||
            op == OP_LE || op == OP_GT || op == OP_GE ||
            op == OP_ISNULL)
            cond_count++;
    }
    assert(cond_count >= 2);
    ast_free(ast); ast_free(ast2);
    program_free(prog); program_free(prog2);
    free(toks); free(toks2);
    printf("PASS: test_compile_where_conditional_jump\n");
}
```
### M3-TS-04 — VM Executes on Mock Cursor
```c
typedef struct {
    Value collected[16][8];
    int   collected_count;
} ResultCollector;
static void collect_cb(Value *row, int ncols, void *ctx) {
    ResultCollector *rc = ctx;
    for (int i = 0; i < ncols; i++)
        rc->collected[rc->collected_count][i] = row[i];
    rc->collected_count++;
}
void test_vm_executes_on_mock_cursor(void) {
    /* Build 3-row dataset: (1,"alice"), (2,"bob"), (3,"carol") */
    Value rows[3][2];
    memset(rows, 0, sizeof(rows));
    rows[0][0].type = VAL_INTEGER; rows[0][0].u.i = 1;
    rows[0][1].type = VAL_STRING;
    rows[0][1].u.s.data = "alice"; rows[0][1].u.s.len = 5; rows[0][1].u.s.owned = 0;
    rows[1][0].type = VAL_INTEGER; rows[1][0].u.i = 2;
    rows[1][1].type = VAL_STRING;
    rows[1][1].u.s.data = "bob"; rows[1][1].u.s.len = 3; rows[1][1].u.s.owned = 0;
    rows[2][0].type = VAL_INTEGER; rows[2][0].u.i = 3;
    rows[2][1].type = VAL_STRING;
    rows[2][1].u.s.data = "carol"; rows[2][1].u.s.len = 5; rows[2][1].u.s.owned = 0;
    /* Cast to Value** — row pointers stored in aux array */
    Value *row_ptrs[3] = { rows[0], rows[1], rows[2] };
    Cursor *cur = mock_cursor_create(row_ptrs, 3, 2);
    const char *col_names[] = {"a", "b"};
    CompileContext ctx = {
        .table_names = (const char*[]){"t"},
        .root_pages  = (int[]){1},
        .col_counts  = (int[]){2},
        .col_names   = (const char**[]){col_names},
        .table_count = 1
    };
    Program *prog = program_create();
    Compiler c; compiler_init(&c, prog, &ctx);
    int tok_count;
    Token *toks = lexer_tokenize_all("SELECT a, b FROM t", &tok_count);
    Parser pp; parser_init(&pp, toks, tok_count);
    AstNode *ast = parser_parse(&pp);
    assert(compiler_compile(&c, ast) == 0);
    ResultCollector rc; memset(&rc, 0, sizeof(rc));
    VM vm; vm_init(&vm, prog);
    vm_attach_cursor(&vm, 0, cur);
    vm_set_result_callback(&vm, collect_cb, &rc);
    int status = vm_execute(&vm);
    assert(status == 0);
    assert(rc.collected_count == 3);
    assert(rc.collected[0][0].u.i == 1);
    assert(strncmp(rc.collected[0][1].u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
    assert(rc.collected[1][0].u.i == 2);
    assert(rc.collected[2][0].u.i == 3);
    assert(strncmp(rc.collected[2][1].u.s.data, "carol", 5) == 0);
    vm_destroy(&vm);
    mock_cursor_destroy(cur);
    /* Empty table: zero result rows, clean halt */
    Cursor *empty_cur = mock_cursor_create(NULL, 0, 2);
    Program *prog2 = program_create();
    Compiler c2; compiler_init(&c2, prog2, &ctx);
    AstNode *ast2 = parser_parse(&pp);  /* re-parse or reuse ast */
    compiler_compile(&c2, ast);
    ResultCollector rc2; memset(&rc2, 0, sizeof(rc2));
    VM vm2; vm_init(&vm2, prog2);
    vm_attach_cursor(&vm2, 0, empty_cur);
    vm_set_result_callback(&vm2, collect_cb, &rc2);
    assert(vm_execute(&vm2) == 0);
    assert(rc2.collected_count == 0);
    vm_destroy(&vm2);
    mock_cursor_destroy(empty_cur);
    ast_free(ast);
    program_free(prog); program_free(prog2);
    free(toks);
    printf("PASS: test_vm_executes_on_mock_cursor\n");
}
```
### M3-TS-05 — Register Bounds Enforcement
```c
void test_register_bounds(void) {
    Program *prog = program_create();
    VM vm; vm_init(&vm, prog);
    /* All 256 registers must be VAL_NULL after vm_init (zero-initialised) */
    for (int i = 0; i < VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS; i++) {
        Value *v = reg(&vm, i);
        assert(v != NULL);
        assert(v->type == VAL_NULL);
    }
    /* Last valid register (255) is accessible */
    Value *last = reg(&vm, VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS - 1);
    assert(last != NULL);
    assert(last->type == VAL_NULL);
#ifdef NDEBUG
    /* Release build: out-of-range returns a non-NULL VAL_NULL sentinel */
    Value *sentinel = reg(&vm, VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS);
    assert(sentinel != NULL);
    assert(sentinel->type == VAL_NULL);
    /* Far out-of-range index also returns sentinel, no crash */
    Value *far_sentinel = reg(&vm, 99999);
    assert(far_sentinel != NULL);
    assert(far_sentinel->type == VAL_NULL);
#else
    /* Debug build: assert fires on out-of-range access.
       Verified via a dedicated test binary compiled with -DVDBE_BOUNDS_TEST:
         gcc -UNDEBUG -DVDBE_BOUNDS_TEST tests/test_vdbe.c ... -o test_bounds
       Expected exit code: non-zero (SIGABRT from assert).
       Run: make test-bounds-debug && ! ./test_bounds   */
    fprintf(stderr,
        "INFO: Debug bounds check requires separate test-bounds-debug binary.\n");
#endif
    /* Compiler must refuse to allocate register VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS */
    Compiler c; const char *no_cols[] = {};
    CompileContext empty_ctx = { .table_count = 0 };
    compiler_init(&c, prog, &empty_ctx);
    c.next_register = VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS;  /* force register exhaustion */
    int r = alloc_register(&c);
#ifndef NDEBUG
    /* In debug this would assert; in release: */
    (void)r;
#else
    assert(r == -1);
    assert(c.had_error == 1);
#endif
    program_free(prog);
    vm_destroy(&vm);
    printf("PASS: test_register_bounds\n");
}
```
---
## 11. Performance Targets
| Operation | Target | Measurement Method |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| Compile simple SELECT (no WHERE) | ≤ 2 µs | `clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC)` over 100K iterations |
| Compile SELECT with 3-condition compound WHERE | ≤ 5 µs | Same |
| VM: full table scan, 10 000 rows, 3 columns | ≤ 8 ms (≤ 800 ns/row) | Time `vm_execute`; divide by row count |
| VM: single `OP_COLUMN` + `OP_INTEGER` + `OP_NE` cycle | ≤ 15 ns | `clock_gettime` in tight loop |
| `emit()` (single instruction append, amortised) | ≤ 5 ns | 1M appends; divide total |
| `alloc_register()` | ≤ 1 ns | Counter increment only |
| `program_explain()` (100-instruction program) | ≤ 200 µs | Redirect stdout to `/dev/null` |
**10 000-row scan benchmark template:**
```c
void bench_table_scan_10k(void) {
    #define N_ROWS 10000
    Value data[N_ROWS][3];
    Value *row_ptrs[N_ROWS];
    for (int i = 0; i < N_ROWS; i++) {
        data[i][0].type = VAL_INTEGER; data[i][0].u.i = i;
        data[i][1].type = VAL_INTEGER; data[i][1].u.i = i * 2;
        data[i][2].type = VAL_INTEGER; data[i][2].u.i = i * 3;
        row_ptrs[i] = data[i];
    }
    Cursor *cur = mock_cursor_create(row_ptrs, N_ROWS, 3);
    /* compile SELECT a,b,c FROM t ... */
    VM vm; vm_init(&vm, prog);
    vm_attach_cursor(&vm, 0, cur);
    int sink = 0;
    vm_set_result_callback(&vm, [](Value *r, int n, void *ctx) {
        (*(int*)ctx)++;
    }, &sink);
    struct timespec t0, t1;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);
    vm_execute(&vm);
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);
    long ns = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec) * 1000000000L +
              (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec);
    printf("10000-row scan: %ld ns total, %ld ns/row\n", ns, ns / N_ROWS);
    assert(ns < 8000000LL);  /* must complete in < 8 ms */
    assert(sink == N_ROWS);
    mock_cursor_destroy(cur);
    vm_destroy(&vm);
    #undef N_ROWS
}
```
---
## 12. Build Integration
```makefile
# Additions to existing Makefile (M1/M2 already define CC, CFLAGS baseline)
CFLAGS ?= -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -g \
           -fsanitize=address,undefined -Isrc
SRC_VDBE = src/vdbe.c src/ast.c src/parser.c src/lexer.c
tests/mock_cursor.o: tests/mock_cursor.c src/cursor.h src/vdbe.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
tests/test_vdbe: tests/test_vdbe.c tests/mock_cursor.o $(SRC_VDBE:.c=.o)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $^ -o $@ -lm
.PHONY: test-vdbe
test-vdbe: tests/test_vdbe
	@echo "==> Running VDBE tests..."
	./tests/test_vdbe
	@echo "==> All VDBE tests passed."
.PHONY: test-vdbe-trace
test-vdbe-trace: CFLAGS += -DVDBE_TRACE
test-vdbe-trace: tests/test_vdbe
	./tests/test_vdbe
.PHONY: test-bounds-debug
test-bounds-debug: CFLAGS += -DVDBE_BOUNDS_TEST -UNDEBUG
test-bounds-debug: tests/test_vdbe
	@! ./tests/test_vdbe --bounds-only && echo "Bounds assert fired correctly."
.PHONY: bench-vdbe
bench-vdbe: CFLAGS = -std=c11 -O2 -DNDEBUG -Isrc
bench-vdbe: tests/test_vdbe
	./tests/test_vdbe --bench
```
---
## 13. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------|-----|
| `OP_NEXT.p2` jumps to `OP_COLUMN` instead of loop start | `loop_start` recorded after calling `compile_where` | Record `loop_start = prog->op_count` *before* the `compile_where` call |
| WHERE jump (`OP_NE.p2`) points to `OP_RESULT_ROW` | `patch_jump` called before `OP_NEXT` is emitted | Emit `OP_NEXT` first; then patch all `where_jumps[]` |
| `OP_REWIND.p2` points to `OP_HALT` instead of `OP_CLOSE` | `patch_jump(prog, rewind_addr, halt_addr)` by mistake | Patch `rewind_addr` to `close_addr` (index `op_count - 2`) |
| `sizeof(Instruction)` = 20 not 24 | `comment` declared as `int32_t` or `char[4]` | Declare as `char *comment` (pointer-sized) |
| Constant folding emits two `OP_INTEGER` and one arithmetic opcode | Fold check placed after recursive descent | Move the `both-are-literals` guard *before* the recursive calls to `compile_expr` |
| `OP_COLUMN` reads wrong column index | `strcmp` on non-NUL-terminated column ref token | Use `strncmp(name, ref, len)` where `len = column_ref.column_len` |
| VM infinite loop on empty table | `OP_REWIND` empty-jump not patched | Ensure `patch_jump(prog, rewind_addr, close_addr)` runs after `close_addr` is known |
| String values corrupted after `vm_destroy` | Owned strings freed, but caller still holds pointer | Caller must copy string data *before* calling `vm_destroy`; or use `OP_COPY` to make an owned copy in a persistent buffer |
| Register file not zeroed | `vm_init` uses `memset` but `VAL_NULL != 0` | Ensure `VAL_NULL = 0` in enum (first entry); `memset(vm, 0, sizeof(*vm))` then works correctly |
| ASan stack-use-after-scope in tests | `col_names[]` array is a local in test helper; pointer escapes | Declare `col_names` at file scope or use `static` keyword in the test function |
| NULL guard jumps skip `OP_RESULT_ROW` then land at `OP_HALT` | NULL guards patched to `halt_addr` instead of `next_addr` | All `where_jumps[]` entries (including null guards) must be patched to `next_addr` |
| `program_explain` prints wrong opcode names | Name table index does not match enum values | Build the name table as `static const char *opcode_names[OP_COUNT] = { [OP_INIT]="Init", ... }` using designated initialisers |
| `OP_NEXT` fires even on empty table | Mock cursor `next()` returns 1 at `pos = row_count` | Guard: `return (++m->data.pos < m->data.row_count) ? 1 : 0` |
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m4 -->
# Module M4 — Buffer Pool Manager
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m4`
**Language:** C (ISO C11) | **Depends On:** `build-sqlite-m3` (VDBE — `Value` type reused for dirty tracking context)
**Difficulty:** Expert | **Estimated Effort:** 7–8 hours
---
## 1. Module Charter
The Buffer Pool Manager is the exclusive gateway between memory and the database file on disk. Every page read or written by the B-tree layer (M5), the transaction manager (M9), or the WAL subsystem (M10) passes through this module. No code outside the buffer pool may call `pread`, `pwrite`, or any file I/O syscall on the database file descriptor.
The module manages a fixed-size array of **frames** — contiguous 4096-byte buffers in user-space memory. Each frame can hold one database page. The pool tracks which page currently occupies each frame via a hash-based **page table**, enforces a **pin/unpin reference-counting discipline** that prevents eviction of actively-used pages, maintains a **doubly-linked LRU list** for eviction ordering, and performs **dirty page tracking** so that modified pages are written back to disk before their frame is reused.
**What this module does NOT do:**
- It does not interpret page contents. A page is an opaque `uint8_t[PAGE_SIZE]` buffer.
- It does not implement crash recovery, journaling, or write-ahead logging. It exposes `flush_page` and `flush_all` primitives that the transaction layer (M9/M10) orchestrates.
- It does not manage file growth policy. `new_page` extends the file; a free-page list is deferred to M5.
- It does not handle concurrency. All operations assume single-threaded access. External synchronisation is the caller's responsibility.
**Upstream dependencies:** None within the project (M4 is the lowest infrastructure layer). Uses only POSIX file I/O (`pread`, `pwrite`, `open`, `close`, `fsync`, `lseek`, `ftruncate`) and standard C library (`calloc`, `realloc`, `free`, `memset`, `memcpy`).
**Downstream consumers:** M5 (B-tree) calls `fetch_page` / `unpin_page` / `new_page` / `mark_dirty`. M9 (rollback journal) calls `flush_page` / `flush_all` and hooks into the eviction path via the transaction manager pointer. M10 (WAL) redirects the write path through `wal_write_frame` instead of `disk_write_page`.
**Invariants:**
1. A frame with `pin_count > 0` is **never** selected for eviction.
2. A dirty frame is **always** written to disk before its `data` buffer is overloaded with a different page.
3. The page table is a bijection: at most one frame maps to any given `page_id`, and at most one `page_id` maps to any given frame index.
4. After `flush_all` returns, every frame's `is_dirty` flag is `false` and `fsync(fd)` has been called.
5. `fetch_page` returns a non-NULL pointer if and only if a frame was successfully populated (from cache or disk). The returned frame is pinned (`pin_count ≥ 1`).
---
## 2. File Structure
```
build-sqlite/
├── src/
│   ├── pager.h          ← Step 1: Frame, BufferPool structs, public API
│   └── pager.c          ← Step 2: Full implementation
└── tests/
    ├── mock_disk.h      ← Step 3: Mock disk I/O interface for deterministic testing
    ├── mock_disk.c      ← Step 3: Mock implementation
    └── test_pager.c     ← Step 4: Five test groups
```
**Dependency graph:**
```
pager.h  → <stdint.h>, <stdbool.h>
pager.c  → pager.h, <unistd.h>, <fcntl.h>, <errno.h>, <string.h>, <stdlib.h>, <stdio.h>
mock_disk.h → pager.h   (uses page_id_t, PAGE_SIZE)
test_pager.c → pager.h, mock_disk.h
```
---
## 3. Complete Data Model
### 3.1 Constants
```c
/* src/pager.h */
#ifndef PAGER_H
#define PAGER_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#define PAGE_SIZE           4096
#define INVALID_PAGE_ID     ((page_id_t)0xFFFFFFFF)
typedef uint32_t page_id_t;
typedef int32_t  frame_id_t;
#define INVALID_FRAME_ID    ((frame_id_t)-1)
```
### 3.2 Frame Struct — Byte Layout
```c
typedef struct {
    page_id_t  page_id;       /* offset  0, size 4 — which page occupies this frame */
    int32_t    pin_count;     /* offset  4, size 4 — number of active users          */
    bool       is_dirty;      /* offset  8, size 1 — modified since last disk write  */
    uint8_t    _pad[3];       /* offset  9, size 3 — alignment padding               */
    int32_t    lru_prev;      /* offset 12, size 4 — index of previous frame in LRU  */
    int32_t    lru_next;      /* offset 16, size 4 — index of next frame in LRU      */
    /* padding: 4 bytes to align data pointer to 8-byte boundary on LP64 */
    uint8_t   *data;          /* offset 24, size 8 — pointer into contiguous data block */
} Frame;
/* sizeof(Frame) == 32 on LP64. 1000 frames × 32 bytes = 32 KB metadata.            */
/* The actual page data is stored in a separate contiguous allocation (§3.4).        */
```
**Field-by-field rationale:**
| Field | Why it exists |
|-------|---------------|
| `page_id` | Identifies which database page this frame currently holds. Set to `INVALID_PAGE_ID` when the frame is empty (on free list). |
| `pin_count` | Reference count. Incremented by `fetch_page`, decremented by `unpin_page`. Eviction is forbidden when `> 0`. Signed so that a double-unpin bug produces a negative value detectable by assertions rather than wrapping to `UINT32_MAX`. |
| `is_dirty` | Set by `unpin_page(pool, pid, /*dirty=*/true)` or `mark_dirty(pool, pid)`. Cleared after the frame's data is written to disk. The eviction path checks this to decide whether a write-back is needed. |
| `lru_prev` / `lru_next` | Indices into the `frames[]` array forming a doubly-linked list threaded through the frames themselves. This avoids allocating separate list nodes. `-1` means "no neighbour" (head or tail sentinel). |
| `data` | Points into the contiguous data block (§3.4). Never NULL for an initialised frame. Each frame's data pointer is `data_block + (frame_index × PAGE_SIZE)`. |
### 3.3 Page Table — Open-Addressing Hash Map
```c
#define PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY    INVALID_FRAME_ID
#define PAGE_TABLE_DELETED  ((frame_id_t)-2)
/* Stored inside BufferPool; not a separate struct */
```
The page table is a flat array of `frame_id_t` values, sized to a prime number at least `2 × pool_size + 1`. Slots contain:
- `PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY` — never occupied.
- `PAGE_TABLE_DELETED` — previously occupied, tombstoned (needed for linear probing correctness).
- A valid `frame_id_t` in `[0, pool_size)` — the page table maps `page_id` → `frame_id`.
The hash function:
```c
static inline uint32_t page_hash(page_id_t pid, int table_size) {
    /* Knuth multiplicative hash — good distribution for sequential page IDs */
    return (uint32_t)((uint64_t)pid * 2654435761ULL) % (uint32_t)table_size;
}
```
### 3.4 BufferPool Struct
```c
typedef struct {
    /* Configuration */
    int           page_size;         /* bytes per page (always PAGE_SIZE = 4096)      */
    int           pool_size;         /* number of frames                              */
    /* Storage */
    Frame        *frames;            /* heap-allocated array of pool_size Frame structs */
    uint8_t      *data_block;        /* contiguous aligned block: pool_size × PAGE_SIZE */
    int           fd;                /* file descriptor for database file              */
    /* Page table (open-addressing hash map) */
    frame_id_t   *page_table;        /* hash table: hash(page_id) → frame_id          */
    int           page_table_size;   /* prime, ≥ 2 * pool_size + 1                    */
    /* LRU list (doubly-linked through Frame.lru_prev / lru_next) */
    frame_id_t    lru_head;          /* most recently used (MRU end)                   */
    frame_id_t    lru_tail;          /* least recently used (eviction end)             */
    /* Free list (stack) */
    frame_id_t   *free_stack;        /* stack of unused frame indices                  */
    int           free_count;        /* number of frames on the free stack             */
    /* Statistics */
    uint64_t      stat_hits;         /* page found in pool (no disk read)              */
    uint64_t      stat_misses;       /* page loaded from disk                          */
    uint64_t      stat_evictions;    /* frames evicted                                 */
    uint64_t      stat_disk_reads;   /* pread calls issued                             */
    uint64_t      stat_disk_writes;  /* pwrite calls issued                            */
    /* Transaction hook (set by M9; NULL until transaction layer exists) */
    void         *txn_manager;       /* opaque pointer; buffer pool does not dereference */
    int         (*txn_journal_page)(void *txn_mgr, void *pool, page_id_t pid);
} BufferPool;
```

![Buffer Pool Frame Array Memory Layout](./diagrams/tdd-diag-11.svg)

*Figure 11: BufferPool architecture. The `frames[]` array holds metadata; the `data_block` holds PAGE_SIZE × pool_size contiguous bytes aligned to PAGE_SIZE. The page table (hash map) maps page_id → frame_id. The LRU list is doubly-linked through the frames themselves. The free stack holds indices of never-used or fully-evicted frames.*
### 3.5 Helper Primes Table
```c
/* Internal to pager.c. Used by pool_create to select page_table_size. */
static const int primes[] = {
    53, 97, 193, 389, 769, 1543, 3079, 6151, 12289,
    24593, 49157, 98317, 196613, 393241, 786433, 0 /* sentinel */
};
static int next_prime_ge(int n) {
    for (int i = 0; primes[i] != 0; i++)
        if (primes[i] >= n) return primes[i];
    return n | 1;  /* fallback: make odd */
}
```
---
## 4. Public API Contracts
### 4.1 `pool_create`
```c
BufferPool *pool_create(const char *db_path, int pool_size);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `db_path != NULL`, points to a valid filesystem path.
- `pool_size > 0` and `pool_size ≤ 100000`.
**Postconditions:**
- Returns a heap-allocated `BufferPool*` with:
  - `fd` = open file descriptor for `db_path` (created if absent, `O_RDWR | O_CREAT`, mode `0644`).
  - `frames` = `calloc(pool_size, sizeof(Frame))` — all `page_id = INVALID_PAGE_ID`, `pin_count = 0`, `is_dirty = false`, `lru_prev = lru_next = -1`.
  - `data_block` = `aligned_alloc(PAGE_SIZE, pool_size * PAGE_SIZE)` — zero-filled via `memset`.
  - Each `frames[i].data = data_block + (i * PAGE_SIZE)`.
  - `page_table` = `malloc(page_table_size * sizeof(frame_id_t))`, all slots `PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY`.
  - `page_table_size` = `next_prime_ge(2 * pool_size + 1)`.
  - `free_stack` = `malloc(pool_size * sizeof(frame_id_t))`, pre-filled with `[0, 1, ..., pool_size - 1]`.
  - `free_count = pool_size`.
  - `lru_head = lru_tail = INVALID_FRAME_ID` (empty LRU list).
  - All stats zeroed. `txn_manager = NULL`, `txn_journal_page = NULL`.
- Returns `NULL` if `open()` fails. Caller should check `errno`.
**Side effects:** Creates or opens the database file. Does not read any pages.
**Error handling:** On `open()` failure, frees all allocated memory and returns `NULL`. On `calloc` / `aligned_alloc` / `malloc` failure, calls `fprintf(stderr, ...)` and `exit(EXIT_FAILURE)`.
### 4.2 `pool_destroy`
```c
void pool_destroy(BufferPool *pool);
```
**Preconditions:** `pool != NULL`. All pages should have been flushed and unpinned. (In practice, `pool_destroy` calls `pool_flush_all` internally as a safety net.)
**Postconditions:**
- Calls `pool_flush_all(pool)`.
- Calls `close(pool->fd)`.
- Frees `pool->frames`, `pool->data_block`, `pool->page_table`, `pool->free_stack`.
- Frees `pool` itself.
- All pointers are invalid after this call.
### 4.3 `pool_fetch_page`
```c
uint8_t *pool_fetch_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t page_id);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `pool != NULL`.
- `page_id != INVALID_PAGE_ID`.
**Postconditions (on success):**
- Returns a pointer to `PAGE_SIZE` bytes of page data.
- The returned frame's `pin_count` is incremented by 1.
- The frame is moved to the MRU end of the LRU list.
- If the page was not in the pool (cache miss), `stat_misses++` and `stat_disk_reads++`.
- If the page was in the pool (cache hit), `stat_hits++`.
**Return value:**
- Non-NULL pointer into `data_block` on success.
- `NULL` if all frames are pinned and no victim can be found.
**Error recovery:** If `pread` fails (returns < `PAGE_SIZE`):
- For short reads (new pages beyond file end): zero-fill the remainder of the frame.
- For actual I/O errors (`errno != 0` and `bytes < 0`): return `NULL`.
**Algorithm (§5.1).**
### 4.4 `pool_unpin_page`
```c
void pool_unpin_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t page_id, bool is_dirty);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `pool != NULL`.
- `page_id` is currently in the pool (present in page table).
- The corresponding frame has `pin_count > 0`.
**Postconditions:**
- `pin_count` decremented by 1.
- If `is_dirty == true`, `frame->is_dirty = true`.
- No other state change. The frame remains in the LRU list at its current position.
**Error handling:**
- If `page_id` not found in page table: `fprintf(stderr, "unpin: page %u not in pool\n", page_id)` and return (no-op).
- If `pin_count` is already 0:
  - Debug build (`assert`): triggers assertion failure.
  - Release build: `fprintf(stderr, "unpin: page %u pin_count already 0\n", page_id)` and return.
### 4.5 `pool_mark_dirty`
```c
void pool_mark_dirty(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t page_id);
```
Sets `is_dirty = true` on the frame holding `page_id`. No-op if `page_id` is not in the pool.
### 4.6 `pool_flush_page`
```c
int pool_flush_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t page_id);
```
**Postconditions (on success):** If the frame holding `page_id` has `is_dirty == true`, writes the frame's data to disk at offset `page_id * PAGE_SIZE` via `pwrite`. Sets `is_dirty = false`. Increments `stat_disk_writes`. Returns `0`.
**Returns:** `0` on success, `-1` on I/O error. Does not call `fsync` — that is the caller's responsibility.
### 4.7 `pool_flush_all`
```c
int pool_flush_all(BufferPool *pool);
```
Iterates all frames. For each frame with `page_id != INVALID_PAGE_ID && is_dirty == true`, writes to disk. After all writes, calls `fsync(pool->fd)`. Returns `0` on success, `-1` if any `pwrite` or `fsync` fails.
**Ordering guarantee:** Frames are flushed in ascending `page_id` order (iterate frames, sort dirty ones, write in order). This is not strictly required for rollback-journal correctness (the journal provides the ordering guarantee), but it improves sequential I/O patterns and simplifies debugging.
### 4.8 `pool_new_page`
```c
page_id_t pool_new_page(BufferPool *pool);
```
Extends the database file by one page. Returns the newly allocated `page_id`. The page is fetched into the pool (pinned with `pin_count = 1`) and zero-filled.
**Algorithm:**
1. `file_size = lseek(pool->fd, 0, SEEK_END)`.
2. `new_page_id = (page_id_t)(file_size / PAGE_SIZE)`.
3. Write `PAGE_SIZE` zero bytes at offset `new_page_id * PAGE_SIZE` via `pwrite`.
4. Call `pool_fetch_page(pool, new_page_id)` to bring it into the pool.
5. Return `new_page_id`.
### 4.9 `pool_get_stats`
```c
typedef struct {
    uint64_t hits;
    uint64_t misses;
    uint64_t evictions;
    uint64_t disk_reads;
    uint64_t disk_writes;
    double   hit_rate;        /* hits / (hits + misses), 0.0 if no fetches */
} PoolStats;
PoolStats pool_get_stats(const BufferPool *pool);
```
Copies stat counters from pool. Computes `hit_rate` as `(double)hits / (double)(hits + misses)` (guard against division by zero).
### 4.10 Stat Reset (Test Support)
```c
void pool_reset_stats(BufferPool *pool);
```
Zeros all `stat_*` fields. Used by tests to isolate measurements.
---
## 5. Algorithm Specifications
### 5.1 `pool_fetch_page` — Full Procedure

![LRU State Machine](./diagrams/tdd-diag-12.svg)

*Figure 12: `pool_fetch_page` decision flowchart. Entry: page_id. Check page table (hit/miss). On hit: increment pin, move to MRU, return. On miss: get free frame (free stack or LRU eviction). On eviction: journal hook → write-back if dirty → remove from page table. Load page from disk. Insert into page table. Add to MRU. Return.*
```
PROCEDURE pool_fetch_page(pool, page_id):
    // Step 1: Page table lookup
    frame_id = page_table_lookup(pool, page_id)
    IF frame_id != INVALID_FRAME_ID:
        // CACHE HIT
        pool->stat_hits++
        frame = &pool->frames[frame_id]
        frame->pin_count++
        lru_move_to_head(pool, frame_id)
        RETURN frame->data
    // Step 2: CACHE MISS — acquire a free frame
    pool->stat_misses++
    frame_id = get_free_frame(pool)
    IF frame_id == INVALID_FRAME_ID:
        RETURN NULL   // all frames pinned
    // Step 3: Load page from disk
    frame = &pool->frames[frame_id]
    frame->page_id   = page_id
    frame->is_dirty   = false
    frame->pin_count  = 1
    disk_read_page(pool, page_id, frame->data)
    pool->stat_disk_reads++
    // Step 4: Register in page table and LRU
    page_table_insert(pool, page_id, frame_id)
    lru_push_head(pool, frame_id)
    RETURN frame->data
```
### 5.2 `get_free_frame` — Free Stack then LRU Eviction
```
PROCEDURE get_free_frame(pool):
    // Try free stack first
    IF pool->free_count > 0:
        pool->free_count--
        RETURN pool->free_stack[pool->free_count]
    // Free stack empty — evict from LRU tail
    candidate = pool->lru_tail
    WHILE candidate != INVALID_FRAME_ID:
        frame = &pool->frames[candidate]
        IF frame->pin_count == 0:
            evict_frame(pool, candidate)
            RETURN candidate
        candidate = frame->lru_prev    // walk toward MRU
    RETURN INVALID_FRAME_ID            // all frames pinned
```
### 5.3 `evict_frame`
```
PROCEDURE evict_frame(pool, frame_id):
    frame = &pool->frames[frame_id]
    // Transaction hook: journal original page before eviction write
    IF pool->txn_journal_page != NULL:
        pool->txn_journal_page(pool->txn_manager, pool, frame->page_id)
    // Write-back dirty page
    IF frame->is_dirty:
        disk_write_page(pool, frame->page_id, frame->data)
        pool->stat_disk_writes++
        frame->is_dirty = false
    // Remove from page table
    page_table_remove(pool, frame->page_id)
    // Remove from LRU list
    lru_remove(pool, frame_id)
    // Mark frame as empty
    frame->page_id = INVALID_PAGE_ID
    pool->stat_evictions++
```
### 5.4 LRU Doubly-Linked List Operations
All operations are O(1). The LRU list is ordered from MRU (head) to LRU (tail). On `fetch_page` (hit or miss), the accessed frame moves to head. On eviction, the scan starts from tail.
```c
/* Remove frame_id from wherever it sits in the LRU list */
static void lru_remove(BufferPool *pool, frame_id_t fid) {
    Frame *f = &pool->frames[fid];
    if (f->lru_prev != INVALID_FRAME_ID)
        pool->frames[f->lru_prev].lru_next = f->lru_next;
    else
        pool->lru_head = f->lru_next;
    if (f->lru_next != INVALID_FRAME_ID)
        pool->frames[f->lru_next].lru_prev = f->lru_prev;
    else
        pool->lru_tail = f->lru_prev;
    f->lru_prev = INVALID_FRAME_ID;
    f->lru_next = INVALID_FRAME_ID;
}
/* Push frame_id to the MRU end (head) of the LRU list */
static void lru_push_head(BufferPool *pool, frame_id_t fid) {
    Frame *f = &pool->frames[fid];
    f->lru_prev = INVALID_FRAME_ID;
    f->lru_next = pool->lru_head;
    if (pool->lru_head != INVALID_FRAME_ID)
        pool->frames[pool->lru_head].lru_prev = fid;
    pool->lru_head = fid;
    if (pool->lru_tail == INVALID_FRAME_ID)
        pool->lru_tail = fid;
}
/* Move frame_id to head (remove then push) */
static void lru_move_to_head(BufferPool *pool, frame_id_t fid) {
    if (pool->lru_head == fid) return; /* already at head */
    lru_remove(pool, fid);
    lru_push_head(pool, fid);
}
```

![fetch_page Flow (Cache Miss Path)](./diagrams/tdd-diag-13.svg)

*Figure 13: LRU list state evolution. Initial: [A←→B←→C←→D], head=A, tail=D. Access C: remove C from middle, push to head → [C←→A←→B←→D]. Evict: scan from tail → D is unpinned → evict D → [C←→A←→B]. Fetch new page E: push to head → [E←→C←→A←→B].*
### 5.5 Page Table — Open-Addressing Linear Probe
```c
/* Lookup: returns frame_id or INVALID_FRAME_ID */
static frame_id_t page_table_lookup(const BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid) {
    uint32_t idx = page_hash(pid, pool->page_table_size);
    for (int i = 0; i < pool->page_table_size; i++) {
        uint32_t slot = (idx + i) % pool->page_table_size;
        frame_id_t fid = pool->page_table[slot];
        if (fid == PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY)
            return INVALID_FRAME_ID;  /* not found */
        if (fid == PAGE_TABLE_DELETED)
            continue;                  /* skip tombstone */
        if (pool->frames[fid].page_id == pid)
            return fid;                /* found */
    }
    return INVALID_FRAME_ID;          /* table full (should never happen) */
}
/* Insert: requires slot is available (caller guarantees load factor < 0.5) */
static void page_table_insert(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid, frame_id_t fid) {
    uint32_t idx = page_hash(pid, pool->page_table_size);
    for (int i = 0; i < pool->page_table_size; i++) {
        uint32_t slot = (idx + i) % pool->page_table_size;
        frame_id_t existing = pool->page_table[slot];
        if (existing == PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY || existing == PAGE_TABLE_DELETED) {
            pool->page_table[slot] = fid;
            return;
        }
    }
    /* Should never reach here — table sized at 2× capacity */
    fprintf(stderr, "page_table_insert: hash table full\n");
    exit(EXIT_FAILURE);
}
/* Remove: tombstone the matching slot */
static void page_table_remove(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid) {
    uint32_t idx = page_hash(pid, pool->page_table_size);
    for (int i = 0; i < pool->page_table_size; i++) {
        uint32_t slot = (idx + i) % pool->page_table_size;
        frame_id_t fid = pool->page_table[slot];
        if (fid == PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY) return; /* not found */
        if (fid == PAGE_TABLE_DELETED) continue;
        if (pool->frames[fid].page_id == pid) {
            pool->page_table[slot] = PAGE_TABLE_DELETED;
            return;
        }
    }
}
```
### 5.6 Disk I/O Helpers
```c
static void disk_read_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid, uint8_t *dest) {
    off_t offset = (off_t)pid * PAGE_SIZE;
    ssize_t bytes = pread(pool->fd, dest, PAGE_SIZE, offset);
    if (bytes < PAGE_SIZE) {
        /* Page beyond current file end — zero-fill remainder */
        if (bytes < 0) bytes = 0;
        memset(dest + bytes, 0, PAGE_SIZE - bytes);
    }
}
static int disk_write_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid,
                            const uint8_t *src) {
    off_t offset = (off_t)pid * PAGE_SIZE;
    ssize_t bytes = pwrite(pool->fd, src, PAGE_SIZE, offset);
    if (bytes != PAGE_SIZE) {
        fprintf(stderr, "disk_write_page: pwrite failed for page %u: %s\n",
                pid, strerror(errno));
        return -1;
    }
    return 0;
}
```
---
## 6. Error Handling Matrix
| Error | Detected By | Recovery | User-Visible? |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------------|
| `open()` fails in `pool_create` | Return value `< 0` | Free all allocated memory, return `NULL` | Yes — caller must check return value |
| `pread` returns `< 0` | `disk_read_page` | Zero-fill frame; log to `stderr` | Transparent — page appears zeroed (acceptable for new pages; problematic for existing) |
| `pwrite` returns `!= PAGE_SIZE` | `disk_write_page` | Return `-1`; caller decides (retry or abort) | Yes — `pool_flush_page` / eviction propagates error |
| `fsync` fails | `pool_flush_all` | Return `-1` | Yes — caller must handle (transaction abort) |
| All frames pinned (pool exhaustion) | `get_free_frame` returns `INVALID_FRAME_ID` | `pool_fetch_page` returns `NULL` | Yes — caller must unpin pages or increase pool size |
| `unpin_page` on unknown `page_id` | `page_table_lookup` returns `INVALID_FRAME_ID` | Log warning, no-op return | No |
| `unpin_page` with `pin_count == 0` | Check in `pool_unpin_page` | Debug: `assert`. Release: log, no-op | Debug: abort. Release: no |
| `aligned_alloc` / `calloc` OOM | Return `NULL` | `fprintf + exit(EXIT_FAILURE)` (fatal) | Fatal — cannot recover |
| `page_id == INVALID_PAGE_ID` passed to `fetch` | Precondition check | `assert(page_id != INVALID_PAGE_ID)` | Debug: abort. Release: undefined |
---
## 7. Mock Disk Interface (Test Support)
Tests must not touch real files. A mock disk intercepts `pread` and `pwrite` and records calls.
```c
/* tests/mock_disk.h */
#ifndef MOCK_DISK_H
#define MOCK_DISK_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "pager.h"
#define MOCK_MAX_PAGES 256
typedef struct {
    uint8_t  pages[MOCK_MAX_PAGES][PAGE_SIZE]; /* stored page data        */
    bool     page_exists[MOCK_MAX_PAGES];      /* true if page was written */
    int      read_count;                        /* number of pread calls   */
    int      write_count;                       /* number of pwrite calls  */
    uint8_t  last_written_data[PAGE_SIZE];      /* data from last write    */
    page_id_t last_written_pid;                 /* page_id of last write   */
    bool     fail_next_read;                    /* inject read failure     */
    bool     fail_next_write;                   /* inject write failure    */
} MockDisk;
void mock_disk_init(MockDisk *md);
/* Creates a BufferPool that uses the mock disk instead of real file I/O.
   Internally patches the pool's fd to -1 and installs function pointers
   (via pager.c compile-time hooks or struct-of-function-pointers).
   For simplicity: compile pager.c with -DMOCK_DISK and use a global
   MockDisk* pointer. */
BufferPool *mock_pool_create(MockDisk *md, int pool_size);
#endif
```
**Implementation strategy:** Compile `pager.c` with `-DMOCK_DISK` for test builds. When `MOCK_DISK` is defined, `disk_read_page` and `disk_write_page` are replaced:
```c
#ifdef MOCK_DISK
extern MockDisk *g_mock_disk;
static void disk_read_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid, uint8_t *dest) {
    (void)pool;
    g_mock_disk->read_count++;
    if (g_mock_disk->fail_next_read) {
        g_mock_disk->fail_next_read = false;
        memset(dest, 0, PAGE_SIZE); /* simulate error: zero-fill */
        return;
    }
    if (pid < MOCK_MAX_PAGES && g_mock_disk->page_exists[pid]) {
        memcpy(dest, g_mock_disk->pages[pid], PAGE_SIZE);
    } else {
        memset(dest, 0, PAGE_SIZE);
    }
}
static int disk_write_page(BufferPool *pool, page_id_t pid,
                            const uint8_t *src) {
    (void)pool;
    g_mock_disk->write_count++;
    g_mock_disk->last_written_pid = pid;
    memcpy(g_mock_disk->last_written_data, src, PAGE_SIZE);
    if (g_mock_disk->fail_next_write) {
        g_mock_disk->fail_next_write = false;
        return -1;
    }
    if (pid < MOCK_MAX_PAGES) {
        memcpy(g_mock_disk->pages[pid], src, PAGE_SIZE);
        g_mock_disk->page_exists[pid] = true;
    }
    return 0;
}
#endif /* MOCK_DISK */
```
---
## 8. Implementation Sequence with Checkpoints
### Phase 1 — Frame Struct and Pool Array (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `src/pager.h` with all constants, `Frame`, `BufferPool`, `PoolStats` definitions, and all public function declarations.
2. `src/pager.c` with `pool_create`, `pool_destroy`, `pool_get_stats`, `pool_reset_stats`.
3. `tests/mock_disk.h` and `tests/mock_disk.c` with `MockDisk` struct and `mock_pool_create`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Define all constants and typedefs in `pager.h`.
2. Define `Frame` struct. Add `_Static_assert(sizeof(Frame) == 32, "Frame size")`.
3. Define `BufferPool` struct.
4. Implement `next_prime_ge()` using the primes table.
5. Implement `pool_create()`:
   - `calloc` frames array.
   - `aligned_alloc` data block. Assign `frames[i].data = data_block + i * PAGE_SIZE`.
   - `malloc` page table; fill with `PAGE_TABLE_EMPTY`.
   - `malloc` free stack; fill with `0, 1, ..., pool_size - 1`.
   - Set `lru_head = lru_tail = INVALID_FRAME_ID`.
   - Set all `frames[i].page_id = INVALID_PAGE_ID; lru_prev = lru_next = INVALID_FRAME_ID`.
6. Implement `pool_destroy()` (free in reverse order; `close(fd)`).
7. Implement `pool_get_stats()` and `pool_reset_stats()`.
8. Implement `mock_disk_init`, `mock_pool_create` in test files.
**Checkpoint:**
```c
MockDisk md;
mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
assert(pool != NULL);
assert(pool->pool_size == 4);
assert(pool->free_count == 4);
assert(pool->lru_head == INVALID_FRAME_ID);
assert(pool->lru_tail == INVALID_FRAME_ID);
for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
    assert(pool->frames[i].page_id == INVALID_PAGE_ID);
    assert(pool->frames[i].pin_count == 0);
    assert(pool->frames[i].data != NULL);
}
PoolStats s = pool_get_stats(pool);
assert(s.hits == 0 && s.misses == 0);
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 1 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 2 — LRU List Management (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `lru_remove`, `lru_push_head`, `lru_move_to_head` as `static` functions in `pager.c`. `page_hash`, `page_table_lookup`, `page_table_insert`, `page_table_remove`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `lru_remove` — handle all four cases (middle, head, tail, single element).
2. Implement `lru_push_head` — handle empty list and non-empty list.
3. Implement `lru_move_to_head` — short-circuit if already at head.
4. Implement `page_hash`, `page_table_lookup`, `page_table_insert`, `page_table_remove`.
**Checkpoint:** Write a standalone test that manually pushes frames 0,1,2,3 to the LRU head in order, then verifies head=3, tail=0, and the chain links are correct:
```c
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) lru_push_head(pool, i);
assert(pool->lru_head == 3);
assert(pool->lru_tail == 0);
assert(pool->frames[3].lru_next == 2);
assert(pool->frames[2].lru_next == 1);
assert(pool->frames[1].lru_next == 0);
assert(pool->frames[0].lru_next == INVALID_FRAME_ID);
/* Move tail (0) to head */
lru_move_to_head(pool, 0);
assert(pool->lru_head == 0);
assert(pool->frames[0].lru_next == 3);
assert(pool->lru_tail == 1);
printf("Phase 2 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 3 — fetch_page and unpin_page (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `disk_read_page`, `disk_write_page`, `get_free_frame`, `evict_frame`, `pool_fetch_page`, `pool_unpin_page`, `pool_mark_dirty`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `disk_read_page` and `disk_write_page` (both real-file and `#ifdef MOCK_DISK` variants).
2. Implement `evict_frame` following §5.3.
3. Implement `get_free_frame` following §5.2.
4. Implement `pool_fetch_page` following §5.1.
5. Implement `pool_unpin_page` with `pin_count` guard.
6. Implement `pool_mark_dirty`.
**Checkpoint (validates M4-TS-01 and M4-TS-02):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
memset(md.pages[5], 0xAB, PAGE_SIZE);
md.page_exists[5] = true;
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
/* Cache miss */
uint8_t *data = pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
assert(data != NULL);
assert(data[0] == 0xAB);
assert(md.read_count == 1);
assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 1); /* first free frame used */
/* Cache hit */
pool_reset_stats(pool);
uint8_t *data2 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
assert(data2 == data);  /* same frame */
assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 2);
PoolStats s = pool_get_stats(pool);
assert(s.hits == 1 && s.misses == 0 && s.disk_reads == 0);
pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 1);
pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 0);
printf("Phase 3 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 4 — flush_all, new_page, and Crash-Safety Guarantee (1 hour)
**Deliverables:** `pool_flush_page`, `pool_flush_all`, `pool_new_page`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `pool_flush_page`.
2. Implement `pool_flush_all` — iterate frames, collect dirty ones, write in `page_id` order, then `fsync` (or no-op for mock).
3. Implement `pool_new_page` — `lseek(SEEK_END)` to get file size (or track page count internally for mock), write zeros, fetch.
**Checkpoint (validates M4-TS-04):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
uint8_t *data = pool_fetch_page(pool, 2);
memset(data, 0xCC, PAGE_SIZE);
pool_unpin_page(pool, 2, true);  /* dirty */
assert(pool->frames[0].is_dirty == true);
pool_flush_all(pool);
assert(pool->frames[0].is_dirty == false);
assert(md.write_count == 1);
assert(md.last_written_pid == 2);
assert(md.last_written_data[0] == 0xCC);
printf("Phase 4 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
---
## 9. Test Specifications
### M4-TS-01 — fetch_page returns correct data for cached page
```c
void test_fetch_cached_page(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    memset(md.pages[5], 0xAB, PAGE_SIZE);
    md.page_exists[5] = true;
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 8);
    /* First fetch: cache miss */
    uint8_t *d1 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
    assert(d1 != NULL);
    assert(d1[0] == 0xAB && d1[PAGE_SIZE - 1] == 0xAB);
    assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 1);
    assert(md.read_count == 1);
    /* Second fetch: cache hit — same pointer, no disk I/O */
    pool_reset_stats(pool);
    md.read_count = 0;
    uint8_t *d2 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
    assert(d2 == d1);
    assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 2);
    assert(md.read_count == 0);
    PoolStats s = pool_get_stats(pool);
    assert(s.hits == 1 && s.misses == 0);
    /* Unpin both */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
    assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 0);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_fetch_cached_page\n");
}
```
### M4-TS-02 — fetch_page triggers disk read on cache miss
```c
void test_fetch_miss_reads_disk(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    /* Pre-populate page 7 with known pattern */
    for (int i = 0; i < PAGE_SIZE; i++)
        md.pages[7][i] = (uint8_t)(i & 0xFF);
    md.page_exists[7] = true;
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
    uint8_t *data = pool_fetch_page(pool, 7);
    assert(data != NULL);
    assert(md.read_count == 1);
    /* Verify every byte */
    for (int i = 0; i < PAGE_SIZE; i++)
        assert(data[i] == (uint8_t)(i & 0xFF));
    assert(pool->frames[0].pin_count == 1);
    /* Fetch non-existent page → zero-filled */
    uint8_t *d2 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 200);
    assert(d2 != NULL);
    for (int i = 0; i < PAGE_SIZE; i++)
        assert(d2[i] == 0);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 7, false);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 200, false);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_fetch_miss_reads_disk\n");
}
```
### M4-TS-03 — LRU eviction selects correct victim frame
```c
void test_lru_eviction_order(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
        memset(md.pages[i], (uint8_t)i, PAGE_SIZE);
        md.page_exists[i] = true;
    }
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 4);
    /* Fill all 4 frames with pages 1,2,3,4 (load order = access order) */
    for (int i = 1; i <= 4; i++) {
        uint8_t *d = pool_fetch_page(pool, i);
        assert(d != NULL);
        pool_unpin_page(pool, i, false);
    }
    /* LRU order (tail→head): 1, 2, 3, 4 */
    /* Re-access pages 1, 3, 2 → moves them to MRU end */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 1); pool_unpin_page(pool, 1, false);
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 3); pool_unpin_page(pool, 3, false);
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 2); pool_unpin_page(pool, 2, false);
    /* LRU order (tail→head): 4, 1, 3, 2 */
    /* Fetch page 5 → must evict page 4 (LRU tail, unpinned) */
    pool_reset_stats(pool);
    md.write_count = 0;
    uint8_t *d5 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
    assert(d5 != NULL);
    assert(d5[0] == 5);  /* page 5 data loaded */
    /* Page 4 should be evicted: lookup should fail */
    frame_id_t fid4 = page_table_lookup(pool, 4);
    assert(fid4 == INVALID_FRAME_ID);
    /* Page 4 was clean → no disk write */
    assert(md.write_count == 0);
    PoolStats s = pool_get_stats(pool);
    assert(s.evictions == 1);
    /* All frames pinned → fetch returns NULL */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 1); /* pin 1 */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 3); /* pin 3 */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 2); /* pin 2 */
    /* page 5 still pinned from above */
    uint8_t *fail = pool_fetch_page(pool, 99);
    assert(fail == NULL);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 1, false);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 3, false);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 2, false);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_lru_eviction_order\n");
}
```
### M4-TS-04 — Dirty page is written to disk on eviction
```c
void test_dirty_eviction_writes(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    md.page_exists[2] = true;
    memset(md.pages[2], 0x00, PAGE_SIZE);
    for (int i = 3; i <= 5; i++) {
        md.page_exists[i] = true;
        memset(md.pages[i], (uint8_t)i, PAGE_SIZE);
    }
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 3);
    /* Load page 2, modify, mark dirty */
    uint8_t *d2 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 2);
    memset(d2, 0xDD, PAGE_SIZE);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 2, true);
    assert(pool->frames[0].is_dirty == true);
    /* Load pages 3,4 to fill the pool */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 3); pool_unpin_page(pool, 3, false);
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 4); pool_unpin_page(pool, 4, false);
    /* LRU order: 2(dirty), 3, 4 */
    /* Fetch page 5 → evicts page 2 (LRU tail, dirty → write-back) */
    md.write_count = 0;
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 5);
    assert(md.write_count == 1);
    assert(md.last_written_pid == 2);
    assert(md.last_written_data[0] == 0xDD);
    /* Verify page 2 was persisted to mock disk */
    assert(md.pages[2][0] == 0xDD);
    /* Evicting a clean page produces no write */
    md.write_count = 0;
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 5, false);
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 2);  /* re-load page 2, evicts page 3 (clean) */
    assert(md.write_count == 0);  /* page 3 was clean */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 2, false);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_dirty_eviction_writes\n");
}
```
### M4-TS-05 — Unpin decrements pin count; zero pin allows eviction
```c
void test_unpin_pin_count_lifecycle(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    md.page_exists[3] = true;
    memset(md.pages[3], 0x33, PAGE_SIZE);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 2);
    /* Double fetch → pin_count = 2 */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 3);
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 3);
    frame_id_t fid = page_table_lookup(pool, 3);
    assert(fid != INVALID_FRAME_ID);
    assert(pool->frames[fid].pin_count == 2);
    /* Unpin once → pin_count = 1, NOT evictable */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 3, false);
    assert(pool->frames[fid].pin_count == 1);
    /* Fill other frame, try to evict page 3 — should fail */
    md.page_exists[10] = true;
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 10);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 10, false);
    /* Both frames used. Fetch page 20 → eviction needed.
       Page 3 has pin_count=1, page 10 has pin_count=0.
       Should evict page 10, not page 3. */
    md.page_exists[20] = true;
    uint8_t *d20 = pool_fetch_page(pool, 20);
    assert(d20 != NULL);
    /* Page 3 must still be present */
    assert(page_table_lookup(pool, 3) != INVALID_FRAME_ID);
    /* Page 10 was evicted */
    assert(page_table_lookup(pool, 10) == INVALID_FRAME_ID);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 20, false);
    /* Unpin page 3 again → pin_count = 0, now evictable */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 3, false);
    assert(pool->frames[fid].pin_count == 0);
    /* Now page 3 can be evicted */
    md.page_exists[30] = true;
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 30);
    /* One of {3, 20} was evicted (LRU determines which) */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 30, false);
    /* Verify pool_unpin on page_id not in pool → warning, no crash */
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 999, false); /* should log and return */
#ifndef NDEBUG
    /* In debug builds, unpin with pin_count == 0 should assert.
       We verify this via a separate test binary (see Makefile). */
    fprintf(stderr, "INFO: pin_count=0 assert verified in test-bounds-debug\n");
#endif
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_unpin_pin_count_lifecycle\n");
}
```

![Dirty Page Tracking and Flush Data Flow](./diagrams/tdd-diag-14.svg)

*Figure 14: Pin count lifecycle state diagram. States: FREE (pin_count=0, page_id=INVALID) → LOADED_UNPINNED (pin_count=0, page_id valid, in LRU) → PINNED (pin_count>0, NOT evictable) → back to LOADED_UNPINNED via unpin. LOADED_UNPINNED → EVICTED (during get_free_frame) → FREE. Dirty flag is orthogonal: set by unpin(dirty=true) or mark_dirty; cleared by write-back during eviction or flush. Transition PINNED → EVICTED is ILLEGAL (the invariant).*
---
## 10. Performance Targets
| Operation | Target | Measurement Method |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| `pool_fetch_page` (cache hit) | ≤ 50 ns | `clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC)` over 1M iterations |
| `pool_fetch_page` (cache miss, mock disk) | ≤ 200 ns | Same (mock eliminates real I/O latency) |
| `pool_unpin_page` | ≤ 15 ns | Same |
| `pool_mark_dirty` | ≤ 30 ns | Same (includes page table lookup) |
| LRU move-to-head | ≤ 10 ns | Isolated benchmark of `lru_move_to_head` |
| Page table lookup (load factor 0.5) | ≤ 20 ns | 1M lookups on pre-filled table |
| `pool_flush_all` (1000 dirty pages, mock disk) | ≤ 500 µs | End-to-end timing |
| Hit rate for sequential scan (10K pages, 1K frames) | ≥ 10% | `pool_get_stats().hit_rate` after scanning 0..9999 |
| Hit rate for repeated scan (100 pages, 200 frames) | 100% after first pass | Second pass: `misses == 0` |
**Benchmark template:**
```c
void bench_fetch_hit(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    md.page_exists[42] = true;
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    /* Warm: one miss */
    pool_fetch_page(pool, 42);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, 42, false);
    /* Bench: 1M hits */
    struct timespec t0, t1;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);
    for (int i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
        pool_fetch_page(pool, 42);
        pool_unpin_page(pool, 42, false);
    }
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);
    long ns = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec) * 1000000000L
            + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec);
    printf("fetch_page (hit): %ld ns/op\n", ns / 1000000);
    assert(ns / 1000000 <= 50);
    pool_destroy(pool);
}
```
---
## 11. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------|-----|
| `pool_fetch_page` always returns the same frame (frame 0) | Free stack pops are LIFO but free stack was filled 0..n-1; all pages map to frame 0 because `page_table_insert` finds first empty slot | Verify `free_stack` pops from `free_count - 1` (highest index first) — this is correct LIFO behaviour. The real issue is likely page_table_remove not tombstoning correctly, so re-fetches always insert into frame 0 |
| Page data corrupted after eviction and re-fetch | `data` pointer reassigned instead of being stable per frame | `frames[i].data` is assigned once in `pool_create` and never changed. Eviction overwrites data contents but not the pointer. Verify `evict_frame` does not touch `frame->data` |
| LRU list corrupted: infinite loop in eviction scan | `lru_remove` does not clear both `lru_prev` and `lru_next` on the removed node | After removing, set `f->lru_prev = f->lru_next = INVALID_FRAME_ID` |
| Page table lookup returns wrong frame after many evictions | Tombstones (`PAGE_TABLE_DELETED`) accumulate, degrading probe chain | Implement periodic rehash (not required for correctness but helps performance); or ensure `page_table_size` ≥ 4× `pool_size` |
| `pool_flush_all` misses a dirty page | Flush loop checks `is_dirty` but the frame was evicted (and written) before the loop reached it | `pool_flush_all` iterates the `frames` array, not the page table. Evicted frames have `page_id == INVALID_PAGE_ID` and are skipped. This is correct — eviction already wrote the data |
| `pool_unpin_page` called with wrong `page_id` after frame was evicted and reloaded | Caller cached a stale `page_id` | Always pass the `page_id` you received from the original `pool_fetch_page` call. The page table enforces the mapping |
| `aligned_alloc` returns NULL on some platforms | `aligned_alloc(PAGE_SIZE, total)` requires `total` to be a multiple of `PAGE_SIZE` and `PAGE_SIZE` to be a power of 2 | `total = pool_size * PAGE_SIZE` is always a multiple. Verify `PAGE_SIZE` is 4096 (power of 2) |
| Dirty page lost on process crash | `pool_flush_all` not called before exit | This is expected in M4 — crash recovery is M9's responsibility. Document that `pool_destroy` calls `pool_flush_all` as a safety net |
| `stat_disk_reads` wrong after eviction + re-fetch of same page | Eviction removes page from pool; re-fetch triggers a new `disk_read_page` but `stat_disk_reads` was reset between calls | `pool_reset_stats` zeroes counters; ensure test expectations account for this |
---
## 12. Build Integration
```makefile
# Additions to existing Makefile
PAGER_SRC = src/pager.c
PAGER_OBJ = src/pager.o
# Normal build
src/pager.o: src/pager.c src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
# Mock-disk build for tests
tests/pager_mock.o: src/pager.c src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK -c $< -o $@
tests/mock_disk.o: tests/mock_disk.c tests/mock_disk.h src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK -c $< -o $@
tests/test_pager: tests/test_pager.c tests/pager_mock.o tests/mock_disk.o
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK $^ -o $@
.PHONY: test-pager
test-pager: tests/test_pager
	@echo "==> Running Buffer Pool tests..."
	./tests/test_pager
	@echo "==> All Buffer Pool tests passed."
.PHONY: bench-pager
bench-pager: CFLAGS = -std=c11 -O2 -DNDEBUG -DMOCK_DISK -Isrc
bench-pager: tests/test_pager
	./tests/test_pager --bench
```
---
## 13. Concurrency Notes (Future — Not Required for M4)
The buffer pool is single-threaded in M4. When multi-threaded access is introduced (M10 WAL readers):
- Each frame needs a `pthread_rwlock_t` or equivalent.
- The page table needs a global mutex (or a concurrent hash map).
- The LRU list needs a mutex protecting head/tail/link modifications.
- `pin_count` becomes an `_Atomic int32_t` with `atomic_fetch_add` / `atomic_fetch_sub`.
These are documented here for awareness. M4 implementation must not use atomics or locks — they add complexity and hinder testing.
---
## 14. Integration with M9 (Transaction Manager Hook)
The buffer pool provides a **transaction hook** that the rollback journal (M9) uses to intercept eviction writes. The hook is called in `evict_frame` before `disk_write_page`:
```c
/* In evict_frame: */
if (pool->txn_journal_page != NULL) {
    pool->txn_journal_page(pool->txn_manager, pool, frame->page_id);
}
```
M9 sets this hook during `txn_begin`:
```c
pool->txn_manager = txn;
pool->txn_journal_page = txn_journal_page_hook;
```
The hook reads the **original** page data from the database file (not from the buffer pool frame, which may already be modified) and writes it to the journal. This is critical: the journal must contain pre-modification data for crash recovery.
**M4 does not implement this hook.** It only provides the function pointer slots. Tests verify that eviction works correctly with `txn_journal_page == NULL`.
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m5 -->
# Module M5 — B-tree Page Format & Table Storage
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m5`
**Language:** C (ISO C11) | **Depends On:** `build-sqlite-m4` (Buffer Pool Manager)
**Difficulty:** Expert | **Estimated Effort:** 12 hours
---
## 1. Module Charter
The B-tree Page Format & Table Storage module defines the on-disk binary layout of every page in the database file and implements the core data structure that stores table rows. It sits directly above the buffer pool (M4), consuming raw `uint8_t[4096]` page buffers and imposing structure on them: a page header, a sorted cell pointer array growing downward from the header, and variable-length cells growing upward from the bottom of the page. Every `INSERT`, `SELECT`, `UPDATE`, and `DELETE` in the entire database eventually resolves to operations on this module's page structures.
**What this module does:**
- Defines the slotted-page binary format for both leaf and internal B-tree pages.
- Implements variable-length integer (varint) encoding and decoding for compact key/size storage.
- Implements the SQLite record serialization format (serial type header + value body).
- Provides cell insertion into a page with sorted pointer array maintenance and overflow detection.
- Implements the B-tree node split algorithm that creates a new page and promotes a separator key to the parent.
- Implements a cursor abstraction (first, next, seek) that replaces the mock cursor from M3.
- Manages the system catalog (`sqlite_master` equivalent) on page 1.
**What this module does NOT do:**
- It does not handle transactions, crash recovery, journaling, or WAL. Those are M9/M10.
- It does not handle secondary indexes (B+tree variant). That is M7.
- It does not interpret SQL or compile bytecode. It is called by the VM cursor opcodes (M3/M6).
- It does not manage buffer pool eviction or disk I/O. It calls `pool_fetch_page` / `pool_unpin_page` from M4.
- It does not handle `DELETE` with node merging. Deleted cells leave fragments; compaction reclaims space.
**Upstream dependency:** `pool_fetch_page`, `pool_unpin_page`, `pool_mark_dirty`, `pool_new_page` from M4.
**Downstream consumers:** M3 VM replaces mock cursor with the real `BTreeCursor` from this module. M6 (DML) calls `btree_insert`, `btree_search`. M7 (indexes) extends the page format for B+tree leaf linking. M9 (transactions) relies on the buffer pool hook for journaling page modifications initiated by B-tree operations.
**Invariants:**
1. Every page's `cell_content_area_start` is always ≥ `header_size + cell_count × 2`. The pointer array and cell content area never overlap.
2. Cell pointers within a page are sorted in ascending key order. The cell data they point to may be in any physical order within the content area.
3. After a node split, every key in the left page is strictly less than the separator key, and every key in the right page is ≥ the separator key.
4. The B-tree is always balanced: every root-to-leaf path has the same depth.
5. All multi-byte integers stored on disk are big-endian.
---
## 2. File Structure
```
build-sqlite/
├── src/
│   ├── pager.h           ← M4 (do not modify)
│   ├── pager.c           ← M4 (do not modify)
│   ├── btree.h           ← Step 1: Page constants, PageHeader, cell format structs, public API
│   ├── btree.c           ← Step 2: Page operations, varint, record format, insert, split
│   ├── btree_cursor.h    ← Step 3: BTreeCursor struct, cursor public API
│   ├── btree_cursor.c    ← Step 4: Cursor implementation (first, next, seek, column)
│   └── catalog.h / .c    ← Step 5: System catalog (sqlite_master) operations
└── tests/
    └── test_btree.c      ← Step 6: Five test groups
```
**Dependency graph:**
```
btree.h         → pager.h
btree.c         → btree.h, pager.h
btree_cursor.h  → btree.h
btree_cursor.c  → btree_cursor.h, btree.h, pager.h
catalog.h       → btree.h
test_btree.c    → btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h
```
---
## 3. Complete Data Model
### 3.1 Page-Level Constants
```c
/* src/btree.h */
#ifndef BTREE_H
#define BTREE_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "pager.h"  /* PAGE_SIZE, page_id_t, BufferPool */
/* Page type bytes — match SQLite's actual values */
#define PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF     0x0D   /* 13 */
#define PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_INTERNAL 0x05   /*  5 */
#define PAGE_TYPE_INDEX_LEAF     0x0A   /* 10 — reserved for M7 */
#define PAGE_TYPE_INDEX_INTERNAL 0x02   /*  2 — reserved for M7 */
/* Header sizes in bytes */
#define LEAF_HEADER_SIZE         8
#define INTERNAL_HEADER_SIZE    12   /* 8 + 4-byte right_child pointer */
/* Cell pointer size */
#define CELL_PTR_SIZE            2    /* uint16_t, big-endian */
/* Maximum usable space per page (PAGE_SIZE - header) */
#define LEAF_USABLE_SPACE     (PAGE_SIZE - LEAF_HEADER_SIZE)
#define INTERNAL_USABLE_SPACE (PAGE_SIZE - INTERNAL_HEADER_SIZE)
```
### 3.2 Page Header — Byte Layout
```
Offset  Size  Field                   Description
------  ----  ----------------------  -----------------------------------------------
0x00    1     page_type               0x0D=table leaf, 0x05=table internal
0x01    2     first_freeblock         Offset to first free block (0=none)
0x03    2     cell_count              Number of cells on this page
0x05    2     cell_content_start      Offset to start of cell content area
0x07    1     fragmented_free_bytes   Total fragmented free bytes in content area
0x08    4     right_child             (Internal pages only) rightmost child page ID
```
Total: 8 bytes for leaf pages, 12 bytes for internal pages.

![B-tree Leaf Page Binary Layout](./diagrams/tdd-diag-15.svg)

*Figure 15: Slotted page binary layout. The header occupies bytes 0x00–0x07 (leaf) or 0x00–0x0B (internal). Cell pointers grow downward from offset header_size in 2-byte increments. Cell content grows upward from PAGE_SIZE (0x1000) toward the pointers. Free space is the gap between the last pointer and cell_content_start.*
```c
typedef struct {
    uint8_t   page_type;              /* offset 0x00, 1 byte  */
    uint16_t  first_freeblock;        /* offset 0x01, 2 bytes — big-endian */
    uint16_t  cell_count;             /* offset 0x03, 2 bytes — big-endian */
    uint16_t  cell_content_start;     /* offset 0x05, 2 bytes — big-endian */
    uint8_t   fragmented_free_bytes;  /* offset 0x07, 1 byte  */
    uint32_t  right_child;            /* offset 0x08, 4 bytes — big-endian
                                         (valid only for internal pages)   */
} PageHeader;
```
**Byte-level access functions (all big-endian):**
```c
static inline uint16_t read_be16(const uint8_t *p) {
    return (uint16_t)((p[0] << 8) | p[1]);
}
static inline uint32_t read_be32(const uint8_t *p) {
    return ((uint32_t)p[0] << 24) | ((uint32_t)p[1] << 16) |
           ((uint32_t)p[2] << 8)  | (uint32_t)p[3];
}
static inline void write_be16(uint8_t *p, uint16_t v) {
    p[0] = (uint8_t)(v >> 8);
    p[1] = (uint8_t)(v & 0xFF);
}
static inline void write_be32(uint8_t *p, uint32_t v) {
    p[0] = (uint8_t)(v >> 24);
    p[1] = (uint8_t)(v >> 16);
    p[2] = (uint8_t)(v >> 8);
    p[3] = (uint8_t)(v & 0xFF);
}
```
### 3.3 Page Header Read/Write
```c
void page_header_read(const uint8_t *page, PageHeader *hdr) {
    hdr->page_type             = page[0];
    hdr->first_freeblock       = read_be16(page + 1);
    hdr->cell_count            = read_be16(page + 3);
    hdr->cell_content_start    = read_be16(page + 5);
    hdr->fragmented_free_bytes = page[7];
    if (hdr->page_type == PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_INTERNAL ||
        hdr->page_type == PAGE_TYPE_INDEX_INTERNAL) {
        hdr->right_child = read_be32(page + 8);
    } else {
        hdr->right_child = 0;
    }
}
void page_header_write(uint8_t *page, const PageHeader *hdr) {
    page[0] = hdr->page_type;
    write_be16(page + 1, hdr->first_freeblock);
    write_be16(page + 3, hdr->cell_count);
    write_be16(page + 5, hdr->cell_content_start);
    page[7] = hdr->fragmented_free_bytes;
    if (hdr->page_type == PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_INTERNAL ||
        hdr->page_type == PAGE_TYPE_INDEX_INTERNAL) {
        write_be32(page + 8, hdr->right_child);
    }
}
```
### 3.4 Varint Encoding — Byte Layout
SQLite varints use 1–9 bytes. Each byte's high bit is a continuation flag (1 = more bytes follow, 0 = last byte). The remaining 7 bits per byte carry payload. The 9th byte (if reached) uses all 8 bits — no continuation flag needed. Total capacity: 7×8 + 8 = 64 bits.
| Value Range        | Bytes | Example: 300 (binary: 100101100) |
|--------------------|-------|----------------------------------|
| 0 – 127            | 1     | `0x7F` → 127                    |
| 128 – 16 383       | 2     | `0x82 0x2C` → 300               |
| 16 384 – 2 097 151 | 3     |                                  |
| …                  | …     |                                  |
| Up to 2⁶³ − 1      | 1–9   |                                  |
Encoding for value 300:
```
300 = 0b100101100
Split into 7-bit groups (from LSB): 0101100, 0000010
Encode MSB-first with continuation bits:
  byte 0: 1_0000010 = 0x82  (continuation=1, payload=2)
  byte 1: 0_0101100 = 0x2C  (continuation=0, payload=44)
Check: (2 << 7) | 44 = 256 + 44 = 300 ✓
```
```c
/* Returns number of bytes written (1–9). */
int varint_encode(uint64_t value, uint8_t *buf);
/* Returns number of bytes consumed (1–9). Decoded value written to *out. */
int varint_decode(const uint8_t *buf, uint64_t *out);
```
### 3.5 Record Serialization Format — Byte Layout
A record serializes a row's column values into a self-describing binary format:
```
[header_size (varint)] [serial_type_0 (varint)] ... [serial_type_N (varint)]
[value_0 bytes] [value_1 bytes] ... [value_N bytes]
```
`header_size` includes itself (the varint encoding of header_size) and all serial type varints.
**Serial type codes:**
| Code | Meaning            | Value Size (bytes) |
|------|--------------------|--------------------|
| 0    | NULL               | 0                  |
| 1    | 8-bit signed int   | 1                  |
| 2    | 16-bit BE int      | 2                  |
| 3    | 24-bit BE int      | 3                  |
| 4    | 32-bit BE int      | 4                  |
| 5    | 48-bit BE int      | 6                  |
| 6    | 64-bit BE int      | 8                  |
| 7    | IEEE 754 double BE | 8                  |
| 8    | Integer constant 0 | 0                  |
| 9    | Integer constant 1 | 0                  |
| N≥12, even | BLOB, size (N-12)/2 | (N-12)/2    |
| N≥13, odd  | TEXT, size (N-13)/2 | (N-13)/2    |
```c
typedef enum {
    COL_NULL = 0,
    COL_INTEGER,
    COL_REAL,
    COL_TEXT,
    COL_BLOB
} ColumnType;
typedef struct {
    ColumnType type;
    union {
        int64_t  i;
        double   r;
        struct {
            uint8_t *data;
            int      length;
        } s;    /* text or blob */
    } u;
} ColumnValue;
/* Determine serial type code for a column value. */
uint64_t serial_type_for(const ColumnValue *val);
/* Byte size of a value with the given serial type. */
int serial_type_size(uint64_t serial_type);
/* Serialize col_count columns into buf. Returns total bytes written. */
int record_serialize(const ColumnValue *cols, int col_count,
                     uint8_t *buf, int buf_size);
/* Deserialize a record. Returns bytes consumed. */
int record_deserialize(const uint8_t *buf, int buf_len,
                       ColumnValue *cols, int max_cols, int *col_count);
/* Read a single column by index without full deserialization. */
int record_read_column(const uint8_t *record, int record_len,
                       int col_idx, ColumnValue *dest);
```
### 3.6 Cell Formats — Byte Layout
**Table B-tree Leaf Cell:**
```
Offset  Size       Field
------  ---------  ------------------
0       varint     payload_size (byte count of the record that follows the rowid)
+n      varint     rowid
+m      payload_size bytes  record data (serial type header + values)
```
Total cell size = `varint_size(payload_size) + varint_size(rowid) + payload_size`.

![Cell Binary Format with Varint Key](./diagrams/tdd-diag-16.svg)

*Figure 16: Table leaf cell binary layout. The payload_size varint encodes the byte count of the record body. The rowid varint encodes the row's integer primary key. The record body follows the serial-type-header + values format from §3.5.*
**Table B-tree Internal Cell:**
```
Offset  Size     Field
------  -------  ------------------
0       4        left_child_page (big-endian uint32)
4       varint   rowid (separator key)
```
Total cell size = `4 + varint_size(rowid)`.
```c
/* Serialize a table leaf cell into buf. Returns bytes written. */
int cell_serialize_table_leaf(uint8_t *buf, int buf_size,
                              int64_t rowid,
                              const uint8_t *record, int record_size);
/* Serialize a table internal cell into buf. Returns bytes written. */
int cell_serialize_table_internal(uint8_t *buf, int buf_size,
                                  uint32_t left_child, int64_t rowid);
/* Read the key (rowid) from a cell at the given page offset. */
int64_t cell_read_key(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                      uint8_t page_type);
/* Compute total byte size of a cell at the given page offset. */
int cell_compute_size(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                      uint8_t page_type);
```
### 3.7 Cell Pointer Array
Cell pointers are stored as 2-byte big-endian offsets immediately after the page header. The `i`-th pointer is at byte offset `header_size + i * 2`. Pointers are maintained in ascending key order.
```c
static inline uint16_t get_cell_pointer(const uint8_t *page,
                                        int header_size, int i) {
    int offset = header_size + i * CELL_PTR_SIZE;
    return read_be16(page + offset);
}
static inline void set_cell_pointer(uint8_t *page, int header_size,
                                    int i, uint16_t ptr) {
    int offset = header_size + i * CELL_PTR_SIZE;
    write_be16(page + offset, ptr);
}
```
### 3.8 BTree Handle
```c
typedef struct {
    BufferPool *pool;
    page_id_t   root_page;
} BTree;
```
### 3.9 BTreeCursor
```c
/* src/btree_cursor.h */
#ifndef BTREE_CURSOR_H
#define BTREE_CURSOR_H
#include "btree.h"
#define MAX_BTREE_DEPTH 20
typedef struct {
    BTree     *tree;
    page_id_t  page_id;        /* current leaf page */
    int        cell_index;     /* current cell within leaf (-1 = before first) */
    bool       is_eof;         /* past last cell */
    /* Parent chain for traversal (replaces leaf-linking) */
    struct {
        page_id_t page_id;
        int       cell_index;  /* which child slot we descended through */
    } stack[MAX_BTREE_DEPTH];
    int stack_depth;
} BTreeCursor;
/* Lifecycle */
BTreeCursor *btree_cursor_open(BTree *tree);
void         btree_cursor_close(BTreeCursor *cur);
/* Positioning */
int  btree_cursor_first(BTreeCursor *cur);    /* 0=ok, 1=empty */
int  btree_cursor_next(BTreeCursor *cur);     /* 0=ok, 1=eof */
int  btree_cursor_seek(BTreeCursor *cur, int64_t key); /* 0=found, 1=not found, positions at ≥key */
/* Data access */
int64_t btree_cursor_rowid(BTreeCursor *cur);
void    btree_cursor_column(BTreeCursor *cur, int col_idx, ColumnValue *dest);
#endif /* BTREE_CURSOR_H */
```
---
## 4. Interface Contracts
### 4.1 `page_init`
```c
void page_init(uint8_t *page, uint8_t page_type);
```
**Preconditions:** `page` points to PAGE_SIZE bytes of writable memory. `page_type` is one of the four `PAGE_TYPE_*` constants.
**Postconditions:**
- `page[0] = page_type`.
- `first_freeblock = 0` (no free blocks).
- `cell_count = 0`.
- `cell_content_start = PAGE_SIZE` (0x1000). Content area is empty — grows upward from the end.
- `fragmented_free_bytes = 0`.
- For internal pages: `right_child = 0`.
- All remaining bytes are zeroed.
### 4.2 `page_insert_cell`
```c
int page_insert_cell(uint8_t *page, const uint8_t *cell_data,
                     int cell_size, int64_t key);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `page` is a valid initialized page.
- `cell_data` points to `cell_size` bytes of a serialized cell.
- `key` is the rowid for sort ordering.
**Postconditions (on success, returns 0):**
- `cell_count` incremented by 1.
- `cell_content_start` decremented by `cell_size`.
- Cell bytes written at the new `cell_content_start` offset.
- A 2-byte pointer to the new cell is inserted into the sorted pointer array at the correct position. Existing pointers at or after the insertion point are shifted right by 2 bytes.
- Page header updated.
**Returns `-1` (overflow):** When the free space between the pointer array end and `cell_content_start` is less than `cell_size + CELL_PTR_SIZE`. The caller must trigger a node split.
**Free space calculation:**
```
header_size = (page_type is internal) ? INTERNAL_HEADER_SIZE : LEAF_HEADER_SIZE
ptr_array_end = header_size + cell_count * CELL_PTR_SIZE
free_space = cell_content_start - ptr_array_end
needed = cell_size + CELL_PTR_SIZE
if (needed > free_space) return -1;
```
**Insertion point:** Binary search on the pointer array: for each pointer `i`, read the key from the cell at that offset. Find the first `i` where the existing key > `key`. Insert at position `i`.
### 4.3 `btree_insert`
```c
int btree_insert(BTree *tree, int64_t rowid,
                 const ColumnValue *cols, int col_count);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `tree->pool` is valid. `tree->root_page` points to an initialized page.
- `rowid` is unique within the tree (duplicate detection is deferred to M6 for UNIQUE constraint).
**Postconditions:**
- On success (returns 0): the row is stored in a leaf cell keyed by `rowid`.
