```c
page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
/* Insert 5 rows: rowid 1-5 */
for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
    ColumnValue c = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = i * 100 };
    btree_insert(&tree, i, &c, 1);
}
/* DELETE rowid 3 */
assert(table_delete(&tree, pool, 3) == 0);
/* DELETE non-existent rowid 99 → returns 1 */
assert(table_delete(&tree, pool, 99) == 1);
/* Scan: expect 4 rows [1,2,4,5] */
BTreeCursor *cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
btree_cursor_first(cur);
int64_t expected[] = {1, 2, 4, 5};
int idx = 0;
do {
    assert(btree_cursor_rowid(cur) == expected[idx]);
    idx++;
} while (btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
assert(idx == 4);
btree_cursor_close(cur);
/* UPDATE rowid 2: change value from 200 to 999 */
ColumnValue new_val = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 999 };
assert(table_update(&tree, pool, 2, &new_val, 1) == 0);
/* Verify */
cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
btree_cursor_seek(cur, 2);
ColumnValue read_val;
btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &read_val);
assert(read_val.type == COL_INTEGER && read_val.u.i == 999);
btree_cursor_close(cur);
/* Page defragmentation: manually verify fragmented_free_bytes is reduced */
/* (Implicitly tested: after delete + inserts, page remains functional) */
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 4 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
---
## 9. Test Specifications
### M6-TS-01 — Full Table Scan Returns All Rows in Rowid Order
```c
void test_full_table_scan(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    TableSchema schema = { .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false,
                           .column_count = 2 };
    /* Insert 5 rows with (id, name) */
    const char *names[] = {"alice", "bob", "carol", "dave", "eve"};
    for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[2];
        cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER;
        cols[0].u.i = (i + 1) * 10;
        cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
        cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t *)names[i];
        cols[1].u.s.length = (int)strlen(names[i]);
        table_insert(&tree, pool, &schema, -1, cols, 2);
    }
    /* Scan via TableCursor */
    Cursor *cur = table_cursor_create(&tree, pool, 2);
    int empty = cur->rewind(cur);
    assert(empty == 0);
    int count = 0;
    int64_t prev_rid = 0;
    do {
        count++;
        int64_t rid;
        cur->rowid(cur, &rid);
        assert(rid > prev_rid);  /* ascending rowid order */
        prev_rid = rid;
        Value v0, v1;
        cur->column(cur, 0, &v0);
        cur->column(cur, 1, &v1);
        assert(v0.type == VAL_INTEGER);
        assert(v0.u.i == count * 10);
        assert(v1.type == VAL_STRING);
        assert(v1.u.s.len == (int)strlen(names[count - 1]));
    } while (cur->next(cur) == 1);
    assert(count == 5);
    cur->close(cur);
    /* Empty table: 0 rows */
    page_id_t root2 = pool_new_page(pool);
    rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root2);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root2, true);
    BTree tree2 = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root2 };
    Cursor *cur2 = table_cursor_create(&tree2, pool, 1);
    assert(cur2->rewind(cur2) == 1);  /* empty */
    cur2->close(cur2);
    /* Single-row table */
    page_id_t root3 = pool_new_page(pool);
    rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root3);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root3, true);
    BTree tree3 = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root3 };
    TableSchema schema3 = { .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false };
    ColumnValue one_col = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 42 };
    table_insert(&tree3, pool, &schema3, -1, &one_col, 1);
    Cursor *cur3 = table_cursor_create(&tree3, pool, 1);
    assert(cur3->rewind(cur3) == 0);
    Value v;
    cur3->column(cur3, 0, &v);
    assert(v.u.i == 42);
    assert(cur3->next(cur3) == 0);  /* eof after 1 row */
    cur3->close(cur3);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_full_table_scan\n");
}
```
### M6-TS-02 — WHERE rowid=N Uses Point Lookup, Not Full Scan
```c
void test_point_lookup(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    TableSchema schema = { .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false };
    /* Insert 1000 rows */
    for (int i = 1; i <= 1000; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[1];
        cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER;
        cols[0].u.i = i * 7;
        btree_insert(&tree, (int64_t)i, cols, 1);
    }
    /* Point lookup rowid=500 */
    Cursor *cur = table_cursor_create(&tree, pool, 1);
    TableCursor *tc = (TableCursor *)cur;
    int found = table_cursor_seek(tc, 500);
    assert(found == 0);
    assert(tc->seek_count == 1);
    int64_t rid;
    cur->rowid(cur, &rid);
    assert(rid == 500);
    Value v;
    cur->column(cur, 0, &v);
    assert(v.type == VAL_INTEGER);
    assert(v.u.i == 500 * 7);
    /* No sequential scans occurred */
    assert(tc->next_count == 0);
    /* Seek non-existent rowid */
    found = table_cursor_seek(tc, 99999);
    assert(found == 1);  /* not found */
    assert(tc->seek_count == 2);
    /* Edge: first row */
    found = table_cursor_seek(tc, 1);
    assert(found == 0);
    cur->rowid(cur, &rid);
    assert(rid == 1);
    /* Edge: last row */
    found = table_cursor_seek(tc, 1000);
    assert(found == 0);
    cur->rowid(cur, &rid);
    assert(rid == 1000);
    cur->close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_point_lookup\n");
}
```
### M6-TS-03 — INSERT Auto-Assigns Rowid and Persists Cell
```c
void test_insert_auto_rowid(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    TableSchema schema = { .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false };
    /* INSERT with auto-assign */
    ColumnValue c1 = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"a", .length = 1 }};
    int64_t r1 = table_insert(&tree, pool, &schema, -1, &c1, 1);
    assert(r1 == 1);
    ColumnValue c2 = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"b", .length = 1 }};
    int64_t r2 = table_insert(&tree, pool, &schema, -1, &c2, 1);
    assert(r2 == 2);
    /* Flush to "disk" and verify persistence */
    pool_flush_all(pool);
    /* Re-scan from the B-tree (simulates re-open) */
    BTreeCursor *cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    assert(btree_cursor_first(cur) == 0);
    assert(btree_cursor_rowid(cur) == 1);
    ColumnValue cv;
    btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
    assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT && cv.u.s.length == 1);
    assert(cv.u.s.data[0] == 'a');
    assert(btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    assert(btree_cursor_rowid(cur) == 2);
    btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
    assert(cv.u.s.data[0] == 'b');
    assert(btree_cursor_next(cur) == 1);  /* eof */
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    /* INSERT with explicit rowid */
    ColumnValue c3 = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"c", .length = 1 }};
    int64_t r3 = table_insert(&tree, pool, &schema, 100, &c3, 1);
    assert(r3 == 100);
    /* Next auto-assign after explicit 100 should be 101 */
    ColumnValue c4 = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"d", .length = 1 }};
    int64_t r4 = table_insert(&tree, pool, &schema, -1, &c4, 1);
    assert(r4 == 101);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_insert_auto_rowid\n");
}
```
### M6-TS-04 — DELETE Removes Cell and UPDATE Overwrites Cell Payload
```c
void test_delete_and_update(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    /* Insert rows 1-5 */
    for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
        ColumnValue c = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = i * 100 };
        btree_insert(&tree, (int64_t)i, &c, 1);
    }
    /* DELETE rowid=3 */
    assert(table_delete(&tree, pool, 3) == 0);
    /* Scan: expect [1,2,4,5] */
    BTreeCursor *cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_first(cur);
    int64_t expected_after_del[] = {1, 2, 4, 5};
    int idx = 0;
    do {
        assert(btree_cursor_rowid(cur) == expected_after_del[idx]);
        idx++;
    } while (btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    assert(idx == 4);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    /* DELETE non-existent rowid → 0 rows affected */
    assert(table_delete(&tree, pool, 99) == 1);
    /* UPDATE rowid=2 → value 999 */
    ColumnValue new_val = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 999 };
    assert(table_update(&tree, pool, 2, &new_val, 1) == 0);
    /* Verify update */
    cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_seek(cur, 2);
    ColumnValue cv;
    btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
    assert(cv.type == COL_INTEGER && cv.u.i == 999);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    /* Full scan after all operations: [1(100), 2(999), 4(400), 5(500)] */
    cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_first(cur);
    int64_t exp_keys[] = {1, 2, 4, 5};
    int64_t exp_vals[] = {100, 999, 400, 500};
    idx = 0;
    do {
        assert(btree_cursor_rowid(cur) == exp_keys[idx]);
        btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
        assert(cv.u.i == exp_vals[idx]);
        idx++;
    } while (btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    assert(idx == 4);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    /* UPDATE non-existent rowid → no-op */
    assert(table_update(&tree, pool, 99, &new_val, 1) == 1);
    /* UPDATE that causes record size increase (tests cell replacement) */
    ColumnValue big_val = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"this_is_a_much_longer_string_value",
                 .length = 34 }};
    assert(table_update(&tree, pool, 1, &big_val, 1) == 0);
    cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_seek(cur, 1);
    btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
    assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT && cv.u.s.length == 34);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_delete_and_update\n");
}
```
### M6-TS-05 — Three-Valued Logic: NULL Comparisons Return NULL, Not False

![Three-Valued Logic Truth Table](./diagrams/tdd-diag-22.svg)

*Figure 22: Three-valued logic test scenarios. Top: `WHERE col = NULL` on table {1, NULL, 3}. The comparison NULL = NULL yields TRI_NULL, which is falsy — the ISNULL guard jumps to NEXT for both the left operand (col when it's NULL) and the right operand (the literal NULL). Result: 0 rows. Bottom: `WHERE col IS NULL` uses OP_ISNULL which directly tests type == VAL_NULL. Row with col=NULL passes. Result: 1 row.*
```c
void test_three_valued_logic(void) {
    /* TriBool truth table verification */
    assert(tri_and(TRI_TRUE,  TRI_TRUE)  == TRI_TRUE);
    assert(tri_and(TRI_TRUE,  TRI_FALSE) == TRI_FALSE);
    assert(tri_and(TRI_TRUE,  TRI_NULL)  == TRI_NULL);
    assert(tri_and(TRI_FALSE, TRI_NULL)  == TRI_FALSE);
    assert(tri_and(TRI_NULL,  TRI_NULL)  == TRI_NULL);
    assert(tri_or(TRI_TRUE,  TRI_NULL)  == TRI_TRUE);
    assert(tri_or(TRI_FALSE, TRI_NULL)  == TRI_NULL);
    assert(tri_or(TRI_NULL,  TRI_NULL)  == TRI_NULL);
    assert(tri_or(TRI_FALSE, TRI_FALSE) == TRI_FALSE);
    assert(tri_not(TRI_TRUE)  == TRI_FALSE);
    assert(tri_not(TRI_FALSE) == TRI_TRUE);
    assert(tri_not(TRI_NULL)  == TRI_NULL);
    /* vm_compare_tri: NULL operand always yields TRI_NULL */
    Value null_val = { .type = VAL_NULL };
    Value int_val  = { .type = VAL_INTEGER, .u.i = 42 };
    Value null_val2 = { .type = VAL_NULL };
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&null_val, &int_val,   OP_EQ) == TRI_NULL);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&int_val,  &null_val,  OP_EQ) == TRI_NULL);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&null_val, &null_val2, OP_EQ) == TRI_NULL);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&null_val, &int_val,   OP_GT) == TRI_NULL);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&null_val, &int_val,   OP_LT) == TRI_NULL);
    /* Non-NULL comparisons work normally */
    Value int5 = { .type = VAL_INTEGER, .u.i = 5 };
    Value int10 = { .type = VAL_INTEGER, .u.i = 10 };
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&int5, &int10, OP_LT) == TRI_TRUE);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&int5, &int10, OP_GT) == TRI_FALSE);
    assert(vm_compare_tri(&int5, &int5,  OP_EQ) == TRI_TRUE);
    /* Integration: WHERE col = NULL on table with NULL row */
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    /* Insert: rowid=1 val=10, rowid=2 val=NULL, rowid=3 val=30 */
    ColumnValue c1 = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 10 };
    btree_insert(&tree, 1, &c1, 1);
    ColumnValue c2 = { .type = COL_NULL };
    btree_insert(&tree, 2, &c2, 1);
    ColumnValue c3 = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 30 };
    btree_insert(&tree, 3, &c3, 1);
    /* Simulate WHERE col = NULL via cursor scan + TriBool check */
    BTreeCursor *cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_first(cur);
    int match_eq_null = 0;
    int match_is_null = 0;
    do {
        ColumnValue cv;
        btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
        Value v;
        column_value_to_vm_value(&cv, &v);
        Value null_literal = { .type = VAL_NULL };
        /* WHERE col = NULL → should match 0 rows */
        TriBool eq_result = vm_compare_tri(&v, &null_literal, OP_EQ);
        if (eq_result == TRI_TRUE) match_eq_null++;
        /* WHERE col IS NULL → should match 1 row (rowid=2) */
        if (v.type == VAL_NULL) match_is_null++;
    } while (btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    assert(match_eq_null == 0);   /* NULL = NULL is NULL, not TRUE */
    assert(match_is_null == 1);   /* IS NULL checks type tag directly */
    /* WHERE col > 5: should return rows 1 and 3 (skip NULL row 2) */
    cur = btree_cursor_open(&tree);
    btree_cursor_first(cur);
    int match_gt5 = 0;
    do {
        ColumnValue cv;
        btree_cursor_column(cur, 0, &cv);
        Value v;
        column_value_to_vm_value(&cv, &v);
        Value five = { .type = VAL_INTEGER, .u.i = 5 };
        TriBool gt = vm_compare_tri(&v, &five, OP_GT);
        if (gt == TRI_TRUE) match_gt5++;
    } while (btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    btree_cursor_close(cur);
    assert(match_gt5 == 2);  /* rows 1 (val=10) and 3 (val=30) */
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_three_valued_logic\n");
}
```
---
## 10. Performance Targets
| Operation | Target | Measurement Method |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| `table_cursor_create` | ≤ 500 ns | `clock_gettime` over 100K iterations |
| `cur->next()` (sequential scan, per row) | ≤ 800 ns | 10K-row scan, total / row_count |
| `table_cursor_seek` (1K-row tree) | ≤ 1 µs | 10K random seeks, average |
| `table_insert` (sequential, 10K rows) | ≤ 5 µs/row | Total / 10K; includes splits |
| `table_delete` (point delete) | ≤ 2 µs | 1K deletes on 10K-row table |
| `table_update` (point update) | ≤ 7 µs | 1K updates on 10K-row table (delete + insert) |
| `column_value_to_vm_value` | ≤ 5 ns | 10M conversions |
| `page_defragment` | ≤ 10 µs | On a page with 50% fragmentation |
| Full scan 10K rows, 3 columns | ≤ 8 ms total | End-to-end via cursor |
**Benchmark template:**
```c
void bench_full_scan_10k(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 500);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    /* Insert 10K rows */
    for (int i = 1; i <= 10000; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[3] = {
            { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = i },
            { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = i * 2 },
            { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = i * 3 }
        };
        btree_insert(&tree, (int64_t)i, cols, 3);
    }
    Cursor *cur = table_cursor_create(&tree, pool, 3);
    cur->rewind(cur);
    int count = 0;
    struct timespec t0, t1;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);
    do {
        Value v0, v1, v2;
        cur->column(cur, 0, &v0);
        cur->column(cur, 1, &v1);
        cur->column(cur, 2, &v2);
        count++;
    } while (cur->next(cur) == 1);
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);
    long ns = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec) * 1000000000L
            + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec);
    printf("10K-row full scan: %ld ns total, %ld ns/row\n", ns, ns / 10000);
    assert(count == 10000);
    assert(ns < 8000000LL);  /* < 8 ms */
    cur->close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
}
```
---
## 11. Build Integration
```makefile
# Additions to existing Makefile
DML_SRC = src/table_cursor.c src/dml.c
DML_OBJ = $(DML_SRC:.c=.o)
src/table_cursor.o: src/table_cursor.c src/table_cursor.h src/btree_cursor.h \
                    src/cursor.h src/pager.h src/vdbe.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
src/dml.o: src/dml.c src/dml.h src/btree.h src/btree_cursor.h src/pager.h src/vdbe.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
tests/test_dml: tests/test_dml.c $(DML_OBJ) $(BTREE_OBJ) tests/pager_mock.o \
                tests/mock_disk.o
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK $^ -o $@ -lm
.PHONY: test-dml
test-dml: tests/test_dml
	@echo "==> Running DML tests..."
	./tests/test_dml
	@echo "==> All DML tests passed."
.PHONY: bench-dml
bench-dml: CFLAGS = -std=c11 -O2 -DNDEBUG -DMOCK_DISK -Isrc
bench-dml: tests/test_dml
	./tests/test_dml --bench
```
---
## 12. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------|-----|
| `cur->next()` returns wrong direction (1=more vs 0=more) | Cursor interface convention (M3) vs BTreeCursor convention (M5) are inverted | `tc_next` adapter: `btree_cursor_next` returns 0=ok, 1=eof; Cursor.next returns 1=has_more, 0=eof. The adapter inverts |
| String data corrupted after cursor advance | `column_value_to_vm_value` borrows pointer from page buffer; page evicted on next fetch | Copy string data via `OP_COPY` if value must persist across cursor movements. For single-row read→output, borrowing is safe |
| `table_delete` doesn't update `max_rowid` cache | DELETE of the max-rowid row leaves cache stale | After deleting the max rowid, invalidate cache: `schema->max_rowid_valid = false`. Next INSERT recomputes via `btree_max_rowid` |
| `table_update` loses data when new record overflows page | `btree_insert` triggers split but the old cell was already deleted | This is correct behavior — the split handles the insertion transparently. No fix needed |
| `page_defragment` corrupts cell pointers | Pointer update writes to wrong offset | Recalculate pointer offset as `header_size + i * CELL_PTR_SIZE` for each pointer `i` |
| Two-pass UPDATE deletes a row that another concurrent writer inserted between passes | Single-threaded constraint not enforced | M6 is single-threaded. Document that multi-connection support requires M9/M10 locks |
| `OP_SEEK_ROWID` segfaults when cursor is NULL | VM handler doesn't check `vm->cursors[cursor_id] != NULL` | Add null check before cast |
| `btree_cursor_delete_current` leaves cursor at wrong position | After shifting pointer array, `cell_index` points to the next cell automatically (pointers shifted left) | Verify `cell_index` stays the same — it now points to what was the next cell. If `cell_index >= new_cell_count`, call `btree_cursor_advance_to_next_leaf` |
| `btree_max_rowid` returns wrong value after split | Split creates new right page; rightmost key is in new page, not original | `btree_max_rowid` follows `right_child` at every internal level — this naturally reaches the rightmost leaf regardless of splits |
| `OP_NOT_NULL` fires on column that has a value | Register index off-by-one between compiler and VM | Compiler must emit `OP_NOT_NULL` with the register that holds the specific column value, not the base register |
| NULL comparison returns TRUE instead of NULL | Using `vm_compare` (bool) instead of `vm_compare_tri` (TriBool) | WHERE compilation must emit `OP_ISNULL` guards that jump to NEXT for NULL operands before the comparison opcode fires |
---
## 13. State Machine — Cursor Lifecycle
```
States:
  CREATED          table_cursor_create() called; btcur exists but not positioned
  REWOUND          rewind() called successfully; positioned at first row
  POSITIONED       next() or seek() positioned at a valid row
  EOF              next() returned 0 (no more rows), or rewind() on empty table
  CLOSED           close() called; all memory freed
Transitions:
  CREATED    → REWOUND       via rewind() when table is non-empty
  CREATED    → EOF            via rewind() when table is empty
  REWOUND    → POSITIONED    via next() returning 1 (has more)
  REWOUND    → EOF            via next() returning 0 (single-row table)
  POSITIONED → POSITIONED    via next() returning 1
  POSITIONED → EOF            via next() returning 0
  POSITIONED → POSITIONED    via seek() finding exact match
  POSITIONED → EOF            via seek() past all keys
  ANY        → CLOSED         via close()
Illegal transitions:
  column() / rowid() in EOF state     → undefined (reads garbage cell index)
  delete_current() in EOF state       → assert failure (no cell to delete)
  Any operation after CLOSED          → use-after-free
```
---
## 14. Dirty Page Tracking Verification
Every write operation must mark the affected page(s) dirty. Verification checklist:
| Operation | Pages Modified | Dirty Marking |
|-----------|---------------|---------------|
| `btree_insert` (no split) | 1 leaf page | `pool_unpin_page(pool, leaf_pid, true)` |
| `btree_insert` (leaf split) | 1 old leaf + 1 new leaf + 1 parent | All three unpinned with `dirty=true` |
| `btree_insert` (root split) | 1 old root + 1 new sibling + 1 new root | All three dirty |
| `btree_cursor_delete_current` | 1 leaf page | `pool_unpin_page(pool, leaf_pid, true)` |
| `page_defragment` | 1 page | `pool_unpin_page(pool, pid, true)` |
| `table_update` | DELETE marks 1 page + INSERT marks 1+ pages | All pages dirty |
The mock disk's `write_count` field in tests validates that `pool_flush_all` writes the expected number of dirty pages.
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m7 -->
<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m7 -->
# Module M7 — Secondary Indexes
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m7`
**Language:** C (ISO C11) | **Depends On:** `build-sqlite-m6` (DML), `build-sqlite-m5` (B-tree), `build-sqlite-m4` (Buffer Pool)
**Difficulty:** Expert | **Estimated Effort:** 8 hours
---
## 1. Module Charter
The Secondary Indexes module introduces B+tree index structures that map arbitrary column values to rowids, enabling the query engine to locate rows without a full table scan. Each index is a standalone B-tree whose cells contain a composite key `(indexed_column_value, rowid)` with an empty payload. The rowid suffix is mandatory: it breaks ties when multiple rows share the same indexed value and ensures every index cell key is globally unique, which simplifies the split algorithm.
**What this module does:**
- Defines the index cell binary format: a record containing `(indexed_value, rowid)` serialized using the same serial-type encoding from M5.
- Implements `compare_index_keys` — a comparator respecting SQLite type-affinity ordering: `NULL < INTEGER/REAL < TEXT < BLOB`, with rowid as tiebreaker.
- Implements `IndexCursor` with `seek`, `next`, `current_entry`, and `scan_equal` operations over the index B-tree.
- Implements `create_index` — scans an existing table, extracts the indexed column from every row, and bulk-inserts `(value, rowid)` entries into a fresh B-tree.
- Hooks into M6's `table_insert`, `table_update`, and `table_delete` to maintain all associated indexes synchronously.
- Registers indexes in the system catalog (`sqlite_master`) with `type='index'`.
- Implements UNIQUE index constraint enforcement on INSERT and UPDATE.
**What this module does NOT do:**
- It does not implement the query planner's decision logic for choosing index scan vs table scan (M8).
- It does not implement multi-column (composite) indexes. Single-column only.
- It does not implement covering index optimization (index-only scans). All lookups perform a double-lookup (index → rowid → table B-tree).
- It does not implement B+tree leaf-page linking. Range scans use the parent-chain traversal from M5's `BTreeCursor`.
- It does not implement DROP INDEX.
**Upstream dependencies:** `BTree`, `BTreeCursor`, `btree_insert`, `btree_search`, `btree_cursor_open/first/next/seek/close`, `btree_cursor_delete_current`, `page_init`, `pool_fetch_page`, `pool_unpin_page`, `pool_new_page`, `pool_mark_dirty` from M4/M5. `table_insert`, `table_delete`, `table_update` from M6. `ColumnValue`, `record_serialize`, `record_deserialize`, `record_read_column` from M5. `TableSchema`, system catalog from M5.
**Downstream consumers:** M8 (query planner) calls `index_cursor_seek` and `index_scan_equal` to evaluate index scan cost and execute index-based access paths. M6's DML functions are modified in-place to call index maintenance hooks.
**Invariants:**
1. Every `(indexed_value, rowid)` pair in an index B-tree corresponds to exactly one live row in the table B-tree. No stale entries. No missing entries.
2. Index cells are sorted first by `indexed_value` (type-affinity order), then by `rowid` (ascending integer).
3. A UNIQUE index rejects any INSERT or UPDATE that would create a second non-NULL entry with the same `indexed_value`. Multiple NULL entries are permitted.
4. After `create_index` completes, the index entry count equals the table row count.
5. `compare_index_keys` is a strict total order on non-NULL values and a strict weak order when NULLs are involved (all NULLs compare equal to each other, and all NULLs sort before every non-NULL).
---
## 2. File Structure
```
build-sqlite/
├── src/
│   ├── index.h            ← Step 1: IndexSchema, index key encoding, comparator, IndexCursor API
│   ├── index.c            ← Step 2: Encoding, comparator, IndexCursor, create_index, UNIQUE check
│   ├── index_maint.h      ← Step 3: Index maintenance hook declarations
│   ├── index_maint.c      ← Step 4: Hook implementations (insert/delete/update index entries)
│   ├── dml.c              ← (M6 — modify to call index maintenance hooks)
│   ├── btree.h            ← (M5 — no changes)
│   ├── btree.c            ← (M5 — no changes)
│   ├── btree_cursor.h     ← (M5 — no changes)
│   ├── btree_cursor.c     ← (M5 — no changes)
│   ├── pager.h            ← (M4 — no changes)
│   └── catalog.h / .c     ← (M5 — extend for index metadata)
└── tests/
    └── test_index.c       ← Step 5: Five test groups
```
**Dependency graph:**
```
index.h       → btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h, vdbe.h (Value type)
index.c       → index.h, btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h
index_maint.h → index.h, btree.h
index_maint.c → index_maint.h, index.h, btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h, dml.h
test_index.c  → index.h, index_maint.h, btree.h, pager.h, dml.h
```
---
## 3. Complete Data Model
### 3.1 IndexSchema
```c
/* src/index.h */
#ifndef INDEX_H
#define INDEX_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "btree.h"
#include "btree_cursor.h"
#include "pager.h"
#define MAX_INDEXES_PER_TABLE 16
typedef struct {
    char        name[64];           /* index name, NUL-terminated                */
    char        table_name[64];     /* owning table name                         */
    char        column_name[64];    /* single indexed column name                */
    int         column_idx;         /* column index in the table schema (0-based)*/
    page_id_t   root_page;          /* root page of the index B-tree             */
    bool        is_unique;          /* UNIQUE constraint enforced?               */
} IndexSchema;
```
**`sizeof(IndexSchema)` on LP64:** 64 + 64 + 64 + 4 + 4 + 1 + 3 padding = 204 bytes.
### 3.2 Index Cell Binary Format — Byte Layout
An index cell stores a composite key `(indexed_value, rowid)` serialized as a standard SQLite record. The cell structure within a **table B-tree leaf page** is:
```
Offset   Size            Field
------   --------------- ----------------------------------------
0        varint          payload_size  (byte count of record body)
+n       varint          rowid_key     (NOT used for ordering — the record
                                        itself contains the rowid as the
                                        second field; this outer rowid is
                                        set to a synthetic value = the
                                        table rowid for compatibility with
                                        the existing B-tree split logic)
+m       payload_size    record body:
                           [header_size (varint)]
                           [serial_type_col (varint)]
                           [serial_type_rowid (varint)]
                           [col_value bytes]
                           [rowid bytes (8-byte int)]
```
**Design decision — index stored in table-type B-tree, not B+tree:** SQLite's actual implementation uses a B-tree (not B+tree) for indexes with the index key as the cell key and an empty payload. For this module, we reuse the existing M5 table B-tree machinery by encoding the composite key as a record and using the table rowid as the cell's outer key. This avoids implementing a separate B+tree page format. The tradeoff: no leaf-page linking (range scans use parent-chain traversal, which is already implemented in M5). The benefit: zero new page format code.
The **outer rowid** of each index cell is set to the table rowid. This means the index B-tree's cells are physically sorted by table rowid (the B-tree's native sort key), NOT by indexed column value. To achieve correct sorted-by-column-value ordering, we use a **comparison function override** during index operations: `compare_index_keys` decodes the record body from both cells and compares by `(column_value, rowid)`.
**Alternative approach (recommended):** Use the `column_value_compare` result as the basis for a **synthetic integer key** that encodes sort order. This is complex for TEXT values. The simplest correct approach for M7:
> **Encode the index key as the cell's outer varint rowid using a deterministic mapping.**
Actually, the cleanest approach — and the one this spec mandates — is:
**The index B-tree stores cells where the outer rowid is the TABLE rowid, and the record payload contains `(indexed_column_value, table_rowid)`.** To maintain sorted order by `(column_value, rowid)`, we insert cells into the correct sorted position using `page_insert_cell_with_comparator` — a new variant of `page_insert_cell` that accepts a comparison function instead of relying on the integer key.
### 3.3 Index Key Comparison
```c
/* Type affinity sort order — matches SQLite collation sequence */
typedef enum {
    TYPE_ORDER_NULL    = 0,   /* NULL sorts first                          */
    TYPE_ORDER_NUMERIC = 1,   /* INTEGER and REAL (compared as doubles)    */
    TYPE_ORDER_TEXT    = 2,   /* TEXT (compared via memcmp / collation)    */
    TYPE_ORDER_BLOB    = 3    /* BLOB (compared via memcmp)               */
} TypeOrder;
/* Compare two ColumnValue structs using SQLite type affinity rules.
   Returns: negative if a < b, 0 if equal, positive if a > b.
   NULL sorts before all non-NULL values.
   NULL == NULL returns 0 (for grouping purposes; WHERE uses TriBool). */
int column_value_compare(const ColumnValue *a, const ColumnValue *b);
/* Compare two complete index keys: (column_value, rowid) pairs.
   First compares column values via column_value_compare.
   If equal, compares rowids as integers (ascending).
   Returns: negative if a < b, 0 if equal, positive if a > b. */
int compare_index_keys(const ColumnValue *val_a, int64_t rowid_a,
                       const ColumnValue *val_b, int64_t rowid_b);
```
{{DIAGRAM:tdd-diag-23}}
*Figure 23: Index key comparison decision tree. Entry: two keys (val_a, rid_a) and (val_b, rid_b). Step 1: map each value to TypeOrder (NULL=0, NUMERIC=1, TEXT=2, BLOB=3). If TypeOrder differs, return difference. Step 2 (same TypeOrder): compare values within type (integer comparison, double comparison, memcmp for text/blob). Step 3: if values equal, compare rowids as integers. Output: negative, zero, or positive.*
### 3.4 `column_value_compare` Implementation
```c
static TypeOrder type_order_of(const ColumnValue *v) {
    switch (v->type) {
        case COL_NULL:    return TYPE_ORDER_NULL;
        case COL_INTEGER: return TYPE_ORDER_NUMERIC;
        case COL_REAL:    return TYPE_ORDER_NUMERIC;
        case COL_TEXT:    return TYPE_ORDER_TEXT;
        case COL_BLOB:    return TYPE_ORDER_BLOB;
        default:          return TYPE_ORDER_NULL;
    }
}
int column_value_compare(const ColumnValue *a, const ColumnValue *b) {
    /* NULL handling: NULL < everything; NULL == NULL */
    if (a->type == COL_NULL && b->type == COL_NULL) return 0;
    if (a->type == COL_NULL) return -1;
    if (b->type == COL_NULL) return  1;
    TypeOrder oa = type_order_of(a);
    TypeOrder ob = type_order_of(b);
    if (oa != ob) return (oa < ob) ? -1 : 1;
    /* Same type group — compare within group */
    if (oa == TYPE_ORDER_NUMERIC) {
        double da = (a->type == COL_REAL) ? a->u.r : (double)a->u.i;
        double db = (b->type == COL_REAL) ? b->u.r : (double)b->u.i;
        if (da < db) return -1;
        if (da > db) return  1;
        return 0;
    }
    if (oa == TYPE_ORDER_TEXT || oa == TYPE_ORDER_BLOB) {
        int min_len = (a->u.s.length < b->u.s.length) ?
                       a->u.s.length : b->u.s.length;
        int cmp = memcmp(a->u.s.data, b->u.s.data, min_len);
        if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
        if (a->u.s.length < b->u.s.length) return -1;
        if (a->u.s.length > b->u.s.length) return  1;
        return 0;
    }
    return 0;
}
int compare_index_keys(const ColumnValue *val_a, int64_t rowid_a,
                       const ColumnValue *val_b, int64_t rowid_b) {
    int cmp = column_value_compare(val_a, val_b);
    if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
    /* Tiebreaker: rowid ascending */
    if (rowid_a < rowid_b) return -1;
    if (rowid_a > rowid_b) return  1;
    return 0;
}
```
### 3.5 Index Cell Serialization
```c
/* Serialize an index entry into a leaf cell buffer.
   The cell format is: [varint payload_size] [varint rowid] [record body]
   where record body = serialize(indexed_value, rowid_as_integer).
   Returns total cell bytes written. */
int index_cell_serialize(uint8_t *buf, int buf_size,
                         const ColumnValue *indexed_value,
                         int64_t rowid);
/* Deserialize an index entry from a cell at the given page offset.
   Reads the record body and extracts (indexed_value, rowid).
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on error. */
int index_cell_deserialize(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                           ColumnValue *out_value, int64_t *out_rowid);
```
**Implementation of `index_cell_serialize`:**
```c
int index_cell_serialize(uint8_t *buf, int buf_size,
                         const ColumnValue *indexed_value,
                         int64_t rowid) {
    /* Build a 2-column record: (indexed_value, rowid) */
    ColumnValue fields[2];
    fields[0] = *indexed_value;
    fields[1].type = COL_INTEGER;
    fields[1].u.i = rowid;
    uint8_t record_buf[4096];
    int record_size = record_serialize(fields, 2, record_buf, sizeof(record_buf));
    if (record_size <= 0) return -1;
    /* Cell format: [varint(payload_size)] [varint(rowid)] [record bytes] */
    int pos = 0;
    pos += varint_encode((uint64_t)record_size, buf + pos);
    pos += varint_encode((uint64_t)rowid, buf + pos);   /* outer key = table rowid */
    if (pos + record_size > buf_size) return -1;
    memcpy(buf + pos, record_buf, record_size);
    pos += record_size;
    return pos;
}
```
**Implementation of `index_cell_deserialize`:**
```c
int index_cell_deserialize(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                           ColumnValue *out_value, int64_t *out_rowid) {
    int pos = cell_offset;
    uint64_t payload_size, outer_rowid;
    pos += varint_decode(page + pos, &payload_size);
    pos += varint_decode(page + pos, &outer_rowid);
    /* Deserialize the 2-column record */
    ColumnValue fields[2];
    int col_count;
    record_deserialize(page + pos, (int)payload_size, fields, 2, &col_count);
    if (col_count < 2) return -1;
    *out_value = fields[0];
    *out_rowid = fields[1].u.i;
    return 0;
}
```
### 3.6 Page Insert with Custom Comparator
The existing `page_insert_cell` from M5 uses `cell_read_key` to extract an integer key and performs integer comparison for sorted insertion. Index pages need comparison by `(column_value, rowid)`. We add a new function:
```c
/* Insert a cell into a page using a custom comparison function.
   cmp_ctx is passed through to cmp_fn.
   cmp_fn(page, cell_offset_a, ctx_b, ctx) returns negative/zero/positive.
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on overflow. */
typedef int (*cell_compare_fn)(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                                const void *new_key_ctx, void *cmp_ctx);
int page_insert_cell_cmp(uint8_t *page, const uint8_t *cell_data,
                         int cell_size, cell_compare_fn cmp_fn,
                         const void *new_key_ctx, void *cmp_ctx);
```
**Implementation:**
```c
int page_insert_cell_cmp(uint8_t *page, const uint8_t *cell_data,
                         int cell_size, cell_compare_fn cmp_fn,
                         const void *new_key_ctx, void *cmp_ctx) {
    PageHeader hdr;
    page_header_read(page, &hdr);
    int header_size = (hdr.page_type == PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_INTERNAL ||
                       hdr.page_type == PAGE_TYPE_INDEX_INTERNAL)
                      ? INTERNAL_HEADER_SIZE : LEAF_HEADER_SIZE;
    int ptr_array_end = header_size + hdr.cell_count * CELL_PTR_SIZE;
    int free_space = hdr.cell_content_start - ptr_array_end;
    if (cell_size + CELL_PTR_SIZE > free_space) return -1;
    /* Find insertion position via linear scan with custom comparator */
    int insert_pos = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < hdr.cell_count; i++) {
        uint16_t cp = get_cell_pointer(page, header_size, i);
        int cmp = cmp_fn(page, cp, new_key_ctx, cmp_ctx);
        if (cmp > 0) break;   /* existing > new → insert here */
        insert_pos = i + 1;
    }
    /* Write cell data into content area */
    uint16_t new_start = hdr.cell_content_start - (uint16_t)cell_size;
    memcpy(page + new_start, cell_data, cell_size);
    /* Shift pointers right */
    for (int i = hdr.cell_count; i > insert_pos; i--) {
        uint16_t prev = get_cell_pointer(page, header_size, i - 1);
        set_cell_pointer(page, header_size, i, prev);
    }
    set_cell_pointer(page, header_size, insert_pos, new_start);
    /* Update header */
    hdr.cell_count++;
    hdr.cell_content_start = new_start;
    page_header_write(page, &hdr);
    return 0;
}
```
The comparator for index cells:
```c
typedef struct {
    ColumnValue value;
    int64_t     rowid;
} IndexKeyCtx;
static int index_cell_cmp(const uint8_t *page, uint16_t cell_offset,
                           const void *new_key_ctx, void *cmp_ctx) {
    (void)cmp_ctx;
    const IndexKeyCtx *new_key = (const IndexKeyCtx *)new_key_ctx;
    ColumnValue existing_val;
    int64_t existing_rowid;
    index_cell_deserialize(page, cell_offset, &existing_val, &existing_rowid);
    return compare_index_keys(&existing_val, existing_rowid,
                              &new_key->value, new_key->rowid);
}
```
### 3.7 IndexCursor
```c
typedef struct {
    BTree       *index_tree;     /* the index B-tree                */
    BTreeCursor *btcur;          /* underlying B-tree cursor        */
    BufferPool  *pool;
    bool         is_eof;
} IndexCursor;
/* Create a cursor over an index B-tree. */
IndexCursor *index_cursor_open(BTree *index_tree, BufferPool *pool);
/* Close and free the cursor. */
void index_cursor_close(IndexCursor *cur);
/* Position at the first entry with indexed_value >= key.
   Returns 0 if positioned, 1 if no such entry (EOF). */
int index_cursor_seek_ge(IndexCursor *cur, const ColumnValue *key);
/* Position at the very first entry in the index. */
int index_cursor_first(IndexCursor *cur);
/* Advance to next entry. Returns 0 if valid, 1 if EOF. */
int index_cursor_next(IndexCursor *cur);
/* Read the current entry's (indexed_value, rowid). */
int index_cursor_current(IndexCursor *cur,
                         ColumnValue *out_value, int64_t *out_rowid);
/* Collect all rowids where indexed_value == key.
   Writes rowids into out_rowids[0..max_rowids-1].
   Returns the count of collected rowids. */
int index_scan_equal(IndexCursor *cur, const ColumnValue *key,
                     int64_t *out_rowids, int max_rowids);
/* Check if a specific value exists in the index (for UNIQUE check).
   Returns 1 if found, 0 if not. */
int index_contains_value(BTree *index_tree, BufferPool *pool,
                         const ColumnValue *value);
```

![Index Lookup Double-Seek Sequence](./diagrams/tdd-diag-24.svg)

*Figure 24: Index lookup and double-lookup sequence. (1) IndexCursor.seek_ge(key='bob') descends the index B-tree to the first leaf cell where value ≥ 'bob'. (2) index_cursor_current() reads (value='bob', rowid=2). (3) scan_equal collects rowid=2 (and any duplicates). (4) Double-lookup: table_cursor_seek(rowid=2) descends the table B-tree to fetch the full row. The total I/O is: h_index pages for index descent + 1 index leaf page + h_table pages for table descent.*
### 3.8 Index Maintenance Hooks
```c
/* src/index_maint.h */
#ifndef INDEX_MAINT_H
#define INDEX_MAINT_H
#include "index.h"
#include "btree.h"
#include "pager.h"
/* Global index registry (simplification for M7; a real DB reads from catalog) */
#define MAX_TOTAL_INDEXES 64
typedef struct {
    IndexSchema  indexes[MAX_TOTAL_INDEXES];
    BTree        index_trees[MAX_TOTAL_INDEXES];
    int          count;
} IndexRegistry;
/* Insert an entry into all indexes on the given table.
   Called after a successful btree_insert into the table B-tree. */
int index_maint_after_insert(IndexRegistry *reg, BufferPool *pool,
                              const char *table_name,
                              int64_t rowid,
                              const ColumnValue *cols, int col_count);
/* Remove entries from all indexes on the given table.
   Called before btree_cursor_delete_current on the table B-tree.
   old_cols must contain the current column values of the row being deleted. */
int index_maint_before_delete(IndexRegistry *reg, BufferPool *pool,
                               const char *table_name,
                               int64_t rowid,
                               const ColumnValue *old_cols, int col_count);
/* Update index entries. For each index whose column changed,
   removes old entry and inserts new entry.
   Returns 0 on success, 19 (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT) on UNIQUE violation. */
int index_maint_on_update(IndexRegistry *reg, BufferPool *pool,
                           const char *table_name,
                           int64_t rowid,
                           const ColumnValue *old_cols,
                           const ColumnValue *new_cols,
                           int col_count);
/* Check UNIQUE constraint for all unique indexes on insert.
   Returns 0 if OK, 19 if violation. */
int index_check_unique_on_insert(IndexRegistry *reg, BufferPool *pool,
                                  const char *table_name,
                                  const ColumnValue *cols, int col_count);
#endif /* INDEX_MAINT_H */
```
### 3.9 Index B-tree Insert
```c
/* Insert a (value, rowid) entry into an index B-tree.
   Uses page_insert_cell_cmp with the index comparator.
   Handles page splits via a modified btree_split that uses the
   custom comparator for cell redistribution.
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on error. */
int index_btree_insert(BTree *index_tree, BufferPool *pool,
                       const ColumnValue *value, int64_t rowid);
```
**Algorithm:**
```
PROCEDURE index_btree_insert(index_tree, pool, value, rowid):
    // Step 1: Serialize cell
    cell_buf[4096]
    cell_size = index_cell_serialize(cell_buf, 4096, value, rowid)
    IF cell_size <= 0: RETURN -1
    // Step 2: Descend to the correct leaf page
    //   Use a modified search that compares (value, rowid)
    //   instead of integer keys.
    page_id = find_index_leaf(index_tree, pool, value, rowid)
    // Step 3: Attempt insertion with custom comparator
    page = pool_fetch_page(pool, page_id)
    IndexKeyCtx new_key = { .value = *value, .rowid = rowid }
    rc = page_insert_cell_cmp(page, cell_buf, cell_size,
                               index_cell_cmp, &new_key, NULL)
    IF rc == 0:
        pool_unpin_page(pool, page_id, true)
        RETURN 0
    // Step 4: Page full — split
    pool_unpin_page(pool, page_id, false)
    // Use the index-aware split that redistributes cells using
    // the custom comparator. Promoted key = first key of right page.
    index_btree_split_and_insert(index_tree, pool, page_id,
                                  cell_buf, cell_size, value, rowid)
    RETURN 0
```
### 3.10 Index B-tree Delete
```c
/* Remove a specific (value, rowid) entry from an index B-tree.
   Returns 0 on success, 1 if not found. */
int index_btree_delete(BTree *index_tree, BufferPool *pool,
                       const ColumnValue *value, int64_t rowid);
```
**Algorithm:**
```
PROCEDURE index_btree_delete(index_tree, pool, value, rowid):
    // Descend to find the exact cell matching (value, rowid)
    IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(index_tree, pool)
    index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, value)
    WHILE NOT cur->is_eof:
        ColumnValue cv
        int64_t rid
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid)
        cmp = compare_index_keys(&cv, rid, value, rowid)
        IF cmp == 0:
            // Found exact match — delete it
            btree_cursor_delete_current(cur->btcur)
            index_cursor_close(cur)
            RETURN 0
        IF cmp > 0:
            BREAK   // Past the target — not found
        index_cursor_next(cur)
    index_cursor_close(cur)
    RETURN 1   // not found
```
### 3.11 CREATE INDEX
```c
/* Create a new index on a single column.
   Allocates a new B-tree root page, scans the table, populates
   the index, and registers it in the IndexRegistry.
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on error,
   19 (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT) on UNIQUE violation during population. */
int create_index(IndexRegistry *reg, BufferPool *pool,
                 BTree *table_tree, const TableSchema *schema,
                 const char *index_name, const char *column_name,
                 bool is_unique);
```
---
## 4. Error Handling Matrix
| Error | Detected By | Recovery | User-Visible? |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------------|
| Column not found in table schema | `create_index` column lookup | Return -1 with error message | Yes |
| UNIQUE violation on CREATE INDEX | `index_check_unique_on_insert` during population | Abort index creation, delete allocated page | Yes (halt_code=19) |
| UNIQUE violation on INSERT | `index_check_unique_on_insert` | Abort INSERT, roll back table insert | Yes (halt_code=19) |
| UNIQUE violation on UPDATE | `index_maint_on_update` | Abort UPDATE, keep old row | Yes (halt_code=19) |
| Index B-tree page overflow | `page_insert_cell_cmp` returns -1 | Transparent split | No |
| Buffer pool exhausted | `pool_fetch_page` returns NULL | Propagate error to caller | Yes |
| `index_btree_delete` entry not found | Scan exhausts matching range | Return 1 (no-op; log warning in debug) | No |
| Record too large for index cell | `index_cell_serialize` returns -1 | Return -1 (column value too long for index) | Yes |
| `pool_new_page` fails (disk full) | Return from `pool_new_page` | `create_index` returns -1 | Yes |
| Stale index entry after crash | Not detected at M7 (deferred to M9) | Transaction rollback restores consistency | No (at M7) |
---
## 5. Algorithm Specifications
### 5.1 `index_cursor_seek_ge`
```
PROCEDURE index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, key):
    // Use the underlying BTreeCursor's seek to position at the
    // first cell with outer-rowid ≥ 0 (start of tree),
    // then linear-scan forward comparing decoded index values.
    //
    // Optimization: since outer rowid = table rowid and cells
    // are physically sorted by table rowid, a seek_ge on the
    // index value requires scanning from the start.
    //
    // ACTUAL APPROACH: Since we store cells sorted by
    // (column_value, rowid) via page_insert_cell_cmp, and the
    // B-tree maintains sorted order, we can descend the tree
    // comparing record bodies at each internal node.
    //
    // For M7 simplicity: cursor_first(), then advance while
    // current_value < key.
    btree_cursor_first(cur->btcur)
    IF cur->btcur->is_eof:
        cur->is_eof = true
        RETURN 1
    WHILE NOT cur->btcur->is_eof:
        ColumnValue cv
        int64_t rid
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid)
        cmp = column_value_compare(&cv, key)
        IF cmp >= 0:
            cur->is_eof = false
            RETURN 0   // positioned at first entry ≥ key
        btree_cursor_next(cur->btcur)
    cur->is_eof = true
    RETURN 1   // no entry ≥ key
```
**Performance note:** This linear scan is O(n) in the worst case. For M7, this is acceptable. M8's query planner will only use the index when selectivity makes it worthwhile. A future optimization would store index cells sorted by column value (using a dedicated B+tree page format) for O(log n) seek.
### 5.2 `index_scan_equal`
```
PROCEDURE index_scan_equal(cur, key, out_rowids, max_rowids):
    rc = index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, key)
    IF rc != 0: RETURN 0   // empty or no match
    count = 0
    WHILE NOT cur->is_eof AND count < max_rowids:
        ColumnValue cv
        int64_t rid
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid)
        cmp = column_value_compare(&cv, key)
        IF cmp != 0: BREAK   // past matching range
        out_rowids[count++] = rid
        index_cursor_next(cur)
    RETURN count
```
### 5.3 `create_index`

![Index Maintenance on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE](./diagrams/tdd-diag-25.svg)

*Figure 25: CREATE INDEX execution flow. (1) Allocate new B-tree root page via pool_new_page. (2) Initialize as PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF. (3) Open table cursor. (4) For each row: extract column value, check UNIQUE if applicable, serialize index cell, insert via index_btree_insert. (5) Register IndexSchema in IndexRegistry. (6) Persist to catalog (sqlite_master row with type='index').*
```
PROCEDURE create_index(reg, pool, table_tree, schema, index_name,
                       column_name, is_unique):
    // Step 1: Find column index
    col_idx = -1
    FOR i = 0 TO schema->column_count - 1:
        IF strcasecmp(schema->column_names[i], column_name) == 0:
            col_idx = i
            BREAK
    IF col_idx == -1: RETURN -1   // column not found
    // Step 2: Allocate index B-tree root
    root_page = pool_new_page(pool)
    page = pool_fetch_page(pool, root_page)
    page_init(page, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF)
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root_page, true)
    // Step 3: Create BTree handle
    BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root_page }
    // Step 4: Scan table and populate index
    BTreeCursor *tcur = btree_cursor_open(table_tree)
    IF btree_cursor_first(tcur) == 0:   // non-empty table
        DO:
            int64_t rowid = btree_cursor_rowid(tcur)
            ColumnValue col_val
            btree_cursor_column(tcur, col_idx, &col_val)
            // UNIQUE check: reject if non-NULL value already in index
            IF is_unique AND col_val.type != COL_NULL:
                IF index_contains_value(&idx_tree, pool, &col_val):
                    btree_cursor_close(tcur)
                    // TODO: deallocate root_page
                    RETURN 19   // SQLITE_CONSTRAINT
            index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &col_val, rowid)
        WHILE btree_cursor_next(tcur) == 0
    btree_cursor_close(tcur)
    // Step 5: Register in IndexRegistry
    IndexSchema *idx = &reg->indexes[reg->count]
    strncpy(idx->name, index_name, 63)
    strncpy(idx->table_name, schema->name, 63)
    strncpy(idx->column_name, column_name, 63)
    idx->column_idx = col_idx
    idx->root_page = root_page
    idx->is_unique = is_unique
    reg->index_trees[reg->count] = idx_tree
    reg->count++
    RETURN 0
```
### 5.4 `index_maint_after_insert`
```
PROCEDURE index_maint_after_insert(reg, pool, table_name, rowid, cols, col_count):
    FOR i = 0 TO reg->count - 1:
        IF strcasecmp(reg->indexes[i].table_name, table_name) != 0:
            CONTINUE
        idx = &reg->indexes[i]
        col_val = cols[idx->column_idx]
        // UNIQUE check
        IF idx->is_unique AND col_val.type != COL_NULL:
            IF index_contains_value(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &col_val):
                RETURN 19   // SQLITE_CONSTRAINT
        index_btree_insert(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &col_val, rowid)
    RETURN 0
```
### 5.5 `index_maint_before_delete`
```
PROCEDURE index_maint_before_delete(reg, pool, table_name, rowid,
                                     old_cols, col_count):
    FOR i = 0 TO reg->count - 1:
        IF strcasecmp(reg->indexes[i].table_name, table_name) != 0:
            CONTINUE
        idx = &reg->indexes[i]
        old_val = old_cols[idx->column_idx]
        index_btree_delete(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &old_val, rowid)
    RETURN 0
```
### 5.6 `index_maint_on_update`
```
PROCEDURE index_maint_on_update(reg, pool, table_name, rowid,
                                 old_cols, new_cols, col_count):
    FOR i = 0 TO reg->count - 1:
        IF strcasecmp(reg->indexes[i].table_name, table_name) != 0:
            CONTINUE
        idx = &reg->indexes[i]
        old_val = old_cols[idx->column_idx]
        new_val = new_cols[idx->column_idx]
        // Skip if indexed column didn't change
        IF column_value_compare(&old_val, &new_val) == 0:
            CONTINUE
        // UNIQUE check on new value
        IF idx->is_unique AND new_val.type != COL_NULL:
            IF index_contains_value(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &new_val):
                RETURN 19   // SQLITE_CONSTRAINT
        // Remove old, insert new
        index_btree_delete(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &old_val, rowid)
        index_btree_insert(&reg->index_trees[i], pool, &new_val, rowid)
    RETURN 0
```
---
## 6. Implementation Sequence with Checkpoints
### Phase 1 — Index Key Encoding and Comparison (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `src/index.h` with `IndexSchema`, `IndexKeyCtx`, `TypeOrder`, all function declarations.
2. `src/index.c` with `column_value_compare`, `compare_index_keys`, `index_cell_serialize`, `index_cell_deserialize`, `page_insert_cell_cmp`, `index_cell_cmp`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `type_order_of` and `column_value_compare`.
2. Implement `compare_index_keys`.
3. Implement `index_cell_serialize` using `record_serialize`.
4. Implement `index_cell_deserialize` using `record_deserialize`.
5. Implement `page_insert_cell_cmp` — copy of `page_insert_cell` but using comparator function.
6. Implement `index_cell_cmp` static comparator.
**Checkpoint:**
```c
/* Type ordering */
ColumnValue null_v = { .type = COL_NULL };
ColumnValue int_v  = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 42 };
ColumnValue txt_v  = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"abc", .length = 3 }};
assert(column_value_compare(&null_v, &int_v) < 0);   /* NULL < INTEGER */
assert(column_value_compare(&int_v, &txt_v) < 0);    /* INTEGER < TEXT */
assert(column_value_compare(&null_v, &null_v) == 0);  /* NULL == NULL */
/* String ordering */
ColumnValue alice = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"alice", .length = 5 }};
ColumnValue bob   = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"bob", .length = 3 }};
assert(column_value_compare(&alice, &bob) < 0);
/* Tiebreaker */
assert(compare_index_keys(&alice, 1, &alice, 5) < 0);  /* same value, rowid 1 < 5 */
assert(compare_index_keys(&alice, 5, &alice, 1) > 0);
/* Cell round-trip */
uint8_t cell_buf[256];
int cs = index_cell_serialize(cell_buf, 256, &bob, 42);
assert(cs > 0);
ColumnValue dec_val;
int64_t dec_rid;
/* Build a page with the cell to test deserialization */
uint8_t page[PAGE_SIZE];
page_init(page, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
IndexKeyCtx key_ctx = { .value = bob, .rowid = 42 };
assert(page_insert_cell_cmp(page, cell_buf, cs, index_cell_cmp, &key_ctx, NULL) == 0);
uint16_t cp = get_cell_pointer(page, LEAF_HEADER_SIZE, 0);
assert(index_cell_deserialize(page, cp, &dec_val, &dec_rid) == 0);
assert(dec_rid == 42);
assert(dec_val.type == COL_TEXT && dec_val.u.s.length == 3);
assert(memcmp(dec_val.u.s.data, "bob", 3) == 0);
printf("Phase 1 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 2 — IndexCursor: Seek, Scan, and Range (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `IndexCursor` struct and all cursor functions in `index.c`: `index_cursor_open`, `index_cursor_close`, `index_cursor_first`, `index_cursor_seek_ge`, `index_cursor_next`, `index_cursor_current`, `index_scan_equal`, `index_contains_value`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `index_cursor_open` — allocate `IndexCursor`, open `BTreeCursor`.
2. Implement `index_cursor_first` — delegates to `btree_cursor_first`.
3. Implement `index_cursor_next` — delegates to `btree_cursor_next`.
4. Implement `index_cursor_current` — calls `index_cell_deserialize` on current position.
5. Implement `index_cursor_seek_ge` per §5.1.
6. Implement `index_scan_equal` per §5.2.
7. Implement `index_contains_value` — wraps `index_scan_equal`, returns count > 0.
**Checkpoint (validates M7-TS-01 and M7-TS-02):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
page_id_t idx_root = pool_new_page(pool);
uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, idx_root);
page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, idx_root, true);
BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = idx_root };
/* Insert ('bob',2), ('alice',1), ('carol',3) */
ColumnValue bob   = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"bob",   .length = 3 }};
ColumnValue alice = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"alice", .length = 5 }};
ColumnValue carol = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"carol", .length = 5 }};
index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &bob,   2);
index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &alice, 1);
index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &carol, 3);
/* Traverse — expect sorted: alice(1), bob(2), carol(3) */
IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 0);
ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
assert(rid == 1 && memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
assert(rid == 2 && memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "bob", 3) == 0);
assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
assert(rid == 3 && memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "carol", 5) == 0);
assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 1);  /* EOF */
index_cursor_close(cur);
/* Scan equal for 'bob' → rowid=2 */
cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
int64_t rowids[16];
int found = index_scan_equal(cur, &bob, rowids, 16);
assert(found == 1);
assert(rowids[0] == 2);
index_cursor_close(cur);
/* Seek non-existent key → 0 results */
ColumnValue zz = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"zzz", .length = 3 }};
cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
found = index_scan_equal(cur, &zz, rowids, 16);
assert(found == 0);
index_cursor_close(cur);
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 2 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 3 — Index Maintenance in DML (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `src/index_maint.h` and `src/index_maint.c` with all maintenance functions.
2. Modification to `src/dml.c`: `table_insert`, `table_delete`, `table_update` now accept an `IndexRegistry*` parameter and call maintenance hooks.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `index_maint_after_insert` per §5.4.
2. Implement `index_maint_before_delete` per §5.5.
3. Implement `index_maint_on_update` per §5.6.
4. Implement `index_check_unique_on_insert`.
5. Modify `table_insert` signature: add `IndexRegistry *reg` parameter. After `btree_insert`, call `index_maint_after_insert`.
6. Modify `table_delete`: before `btree_cursor_delete_current`, read current row's columns, call `index_maint_before_delete`.
7. Modify `table_update`: call `index_maint_on_update` with old and new column values.
**Checkpoint (validates M7-TS-04):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
/* Create table tree */
page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
uint8_t *tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
TableSchema schema = { .column_count = 1, .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false };
strncpy(schema.name, "t", 63);
strncpy(schema.column_names[0], "name", 63);
/* Create index */
IndexRegistry reg = { .count = 0 };
assert(create_index(&reg, pool, &table_tree, &schema,
                    "idx_name", "name", false) == 0);
assert(reg.count == 1);
/* INSERT ('dave', rowid=1) */
ColumnValue dave = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"dave", .length = 4 }};
int64_t rid = table_insert(&table_tree, pool, &schema, -1, &dave, 1);
assert(rid > 0);
assert(index_maint_after_insert(&reg, pool, "t", rid, &dave, 1) == 0);
/* Verify index contains ('dave', rid) */
assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &dave) == 1);
/* UPDATE rowid=rid SET name='david' */
ColumnValue david = { .type = COL_TEXT, .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"david", .length = 5 }};
assert(index_maint_on_update(&reg, pool, "t", rid, &dave, &david, 1) == 0);
/* Old value gone, new value present */
assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &dave) == 0);
assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &david) == 1);
/* DELETE rowid=rid */
assert(index_maint_before_delete(&reg, pool, "t", rid, &david, 1) == 0);
table_delete(&table_tree, pool, rid);
assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &david) == 0);
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 3 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 4 — CREATE INDEX Implementation (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `create_index` function fully implemented in `index.c`.
2. UNIQUE index constraint enforcement during creation and INSERT.
3. Integration with catalog (register in `IndexRegistry` and optionally in `sqlite_master`).
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `create_index` per §5.3.
2. Add UNIQUE check in `index_check_unique_on_insert`.
3. Test UNIQUE violation: create table with duplicates, attempt CREATE UNIQUE INDEX → expect error.
4. Test CREATE INDEX on empty table → empty but valid index B-tree.
**Checkpoint (validates M7-TS-03):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
uint8_t *tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
TableSchema schema = { .column_count = 2, .max_rowid = 0, .max_rowid_valid = false };
strncpy(schema.name, "t", 63);
strncpy(schema.column_names[0], "id", 63);
strncpy(schema.column_names[1], "name", 63);
/* Insert 10 rows */
const char *names[] = {"eve","dave","carol","bob","alice",
                       "frank","grace","heidi","ivan","judy"};
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
    ColumnValue cols[2];
    cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER; cols[0].u.i = i + 1;
    cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
    cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t*)names[i];
    cols[1].u.s.length = (int)strlen(names[i]);
    btree_insert(&table_tree, (int64_t)(i + 1), cols, 2);
    schema.max_rowid = i + 1;
    schema.max_rowid_valid = true;
}
/* CREATE INDEX */
IndexRegistry reg = { .count = 0 };
assert(create_index(&reg, pool, &table_tree, &schema,
                    "idx_name", "name", false) == 0);
/* Verify 10 entries */
IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&reg.index_trees[0], pool);
assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 0);
int count = 0;
const char *sorted_names[] = {"alice","bob","carol","dave","eve",
                               "frank","grace","heidi","ivan","judy"};
do {
    ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT);
    assert(cv.u.s.length == (int)strlen(sorted_names[count]));
    assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, sorted_names[count], cv.u.s.length) == 0);
    count++;
} while (index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
assert(count == 10);
index_cursor_close(cur);
/* Empty table → empty index */
page_id_t empty_root = pool_new_page(pool);
tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, empty_root);
page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, empty_root, true);
BTree empty_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = empty_root };
TableSchema empty_schema = { .column_count = 1 };
strncpy(empty_schema.name, "empty", 63);
strncpy(empty_schema.column_names[0], "val", 63);
assert(create_index(&reg, pool, &empty_tree, &empty_schema,
                    "idx_empty", "val", false) == 0);
cur = index_cursor_open(&reg.index_trees[reg.count - 1], pool);
assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 1);  /* empty */
index_cursor_close(cur);
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 4 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
---
## 7. Test Specifications
### M7-TS-01 — Index B-tree Stores (key, rowid) Cells in Sorted Order
```c
void test_index_sorted_order(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 100);
    page_id_t idx_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, idx_root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, idx_root, true);
    BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = idx_root };
    /* Insert out of order: ('bob',2), ('alice',1), ('carol',3) */
    ColumnValue bob   = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"bob",   .length = 3 }};
    ColumnValue alice = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"alice", .length = 5 }};
    ColumnValue carol = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"carol", .length = 5 }};
    index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &bob,   2);
    index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &alice, 1);
    index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &carol, 3);
    /* Traverse: expect alice(1), bob(2), carol(3) */
    IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 0);
    ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 1);
    assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT && cv.u.s.length == 5);
    assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
    assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 2);
    assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "bob", 3) == 0);
    assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 3);
    assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "carol", 5) == 0);
    assert(index_cursor_next(cur) == 1);  /* EOF */
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* Edge case: duplicate key values */
    ColumnValue alice2 = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"alice", .length = 5 }};
    index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &alice2, 5);
    /* Expect: alice(1), alice(5), bob(2), carol(3) */
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    index_cursor_first(cur);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 1 && memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
    index_cursor_next(cur);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 5 && memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "alice", 5) == 0);
    index_cursor_next(cur);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(rid == 2);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* Edge case: NULL sorts before all */
    ColumnValue null_v = { .type = COL_NULL };
    index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &null_v, 10);
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    index_cursor_first(cur);
    index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
    assert(cv.type == COL_NULL && rid == 10);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_index_sorted_order\n");
}
```
### M7-TS-02 — Index Lookup Returns Correct Rowid(s) for Exact Key Match
```c
void test_index_exact_lookup(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    /* Create table + index */
    page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
    BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
    page_id_t idx_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, idx_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, idx_root, true);
    BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = idx_root };
    /* Insert 4 table rows and corresponding index entries */
    const char *names[] = {"alice", "bob", "carol", "bob"};
    int64_t rowids[]    = {1, 2, 3, 4};
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[1] = {{ .type = COL_TEXT,
            .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)names[i],
                     .length = (int)strlen(names[i]) }}};
        btree_insert(&table_tree, rowids[i], cols, 1);
        index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &cols[0], rowids[i]);
    }
    /* Lookup 'bob' → expect rowids {2, 4} */
    ColumnValue bob = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"bob", .length = 3 }};
    IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    int64_t out_rids[16];
    int found = index_scan_equal(cur, &bob, out_rids, 16);
    assert(found == 2);
    assert(out_rids[0] == 2);
    assert(out_rids[1] == 4);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* Double-lookup: verify table rows */
    for (int i = 0; i < found; i++) {
        BTreeCursor *tcur = btree_cursor_open(&table_tree);
        assert(btree_cursor_seek(tcur, out_rids[i]) == 0);
        ColumnValue cv;
        btree_cursor_column(tcur, 0, &cv);
        assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT);
        assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, "bob", 3) == 0);
        btree_cursor_close(tcur);
    }
    /* Key not in index → 0 results */
    ColumnValue zack = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"zack", .length = 4 }};
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    found = index_scan_equal(cur, &zack, out_rids, 16);
    assert(found == 0);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* Single match */
    ColumnValue alice = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"alice", .length = 5 }};
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    found = index_scan_equal(cur, &alice, out_rids, 16);
    assert(found == 1);
    assert(out_rids[0] == 1);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_index_exact_lookup\n");
}
```
### M7-TS-03 — CREATE INDEX Populates Index from Existing Table Data
```c
void test_create_index_from_table(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
    BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
    TableSchema schema = { .column_count = 2, .max_rowid = 0,
                           .max_rowid_valid = false };
    strncpy(schema.name, "people", 63);
    strncpy(schema.column_names[0], "id", 63);
    strncpy(schema.column_names[1], "name", 63);
    /* Insert 10 rows */
    const char *names[] = {"judy","ivan","heidi","grace","frank",
                           "eve","dave","carol","bob","alice"};
    for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[2];
        cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER; cols[0].u.i = i + 1;
        cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
        cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t*)names[i];
        cols[1].u.s.length = (int)strlen(names[i]);
        btree_insert(&table_tree, (int64_t)(i + 1), cols, 2);
        schema.max_rowid = i + 1;
        schema.max_rowid_valid = true;
    }
    /* CREATE INDEX */
    IndexRegistry reg = { .count = 0 };
    int rc = create_index(&reg, pool, &table_tree, &schema,
                          "idx_name", "name", false);
    assert(rc == 0);
    assert(reg.count == 1);
    assert(reg.indexes[0].root_page > 0);
    assert(reg.indexes[0].column_idx == 1);
    /* Traverse index — expect 10 entries in sorted name order */
    IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&reg.index_trees[0], pool);
    assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 0);
    int count = 0;
    ColumnValue prev = { .type = COL_NULL };
    do {
        ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
        assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT);
        /* Each entry's value ≥ previous (sorted order) */
        if (prev.type != COL_NULL) {
            assert(column_value_compare(&prev, &cv) <= 0);
        }
        prev = cv;
        count++;
    } while (index_cursor_next(cur) == 0);
    assert(count == 10);
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* CREATE INDEX on empty table */
    page_id_t empty_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, empty_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, empty_root, true);
    BTree empty_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = empty_root };
    TableSchema empty_sch = { .column_count = 1 };
    strncpy(empty_sch.name, "empty_t", 63);
    strncpy(empty_sch.column_names[0], "x", 63);
    rc = create_index(&reg, pool, &empty_tree, &empty_sch,
                      "idx_empty_x", "x", false);
    assert(rc == 0);
    cur = index_cursor_open(&reg.index_trees[reg.count - 1], pool);
    assert(index_cursor_first(cur) == 1);  /* empty index */
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    /* UNIQUE constraint violation: table with duplicate names */
    page_id_t dup_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, dup_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, dup_root, true);
    BTree dup_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = dup_root };
    TableSchema dup_sch = { .column_count = 1, .max_rowid = 0 };
    strncpy(dup_sch.name, "dup_t", 63);
    strncpy(dup_sch.column_names[0], "name", 63);
    ColumnValue d1 = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"same", .length = 4 }};
    btree_insert(&dup_tree, 1, &d1, 1);
    btree_insert(&dup_tree, 2, &d1, 1);
    rc = create_index(&reg, pool, &dup_tree, &dup_sch,
                      "idx_dup_name", "name", true);  /* UNIQUE! */
    assert(rc == 19);  /* SQLITE_CONSTRAINT */
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_create_index_from_table\n");
}
```
### M7-TS-04 — DML Operations Maintain Index Consistency
```c
void test_dml_index_maintenance(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *tp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
    page_init(tp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
    BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
    TableSchema schema = { .column_count = 2, .max_rowid = 0,
                           .max_rowid_valid = false };
    strncpy(schema.name, "t", 63);
    strncpy(schema.column_names[0], "id", 63);
    strncpy(schema.column_names[1], "name", 63);
    /* Create index on 'name' */
    IndexRegistry reg = { .count = 0 };
    create_index(&reg, pool, &table_tree, &schema, "idx", "name", false);
    /* INSERT → index updated */
    ColumnValue cols[2];
    cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER; cols[0].u.i = 10;
    cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
    cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t*)"dave"; cols[1].u.s.length = 4;
    int64_t rid = table_insert(&table_tree, pool, &schema, -1, cols, 2);
    assert(rid > 0);
    assert(index_maint_after_insert(&reg, pool, "t", rid, cols, 2) == 0);
    ColumnValue dave = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"dave", .length = 4 }};
    assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &dave) == 1);
    /* UPDATE name='david' → old entry removed, new entry added */
    ColumnValue old_cols[2];
    old_cols[0] = cols[0];
    old_cols[1] = dave;
    ColumnValue new_cols[2];
    new_cols[0] = cols[0];
    new_cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
    new_cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t*)"david"; new_cols[1].u.s.length = 5;
    assert(index_maint_on_update(&reg, pool, "t", rid,
                                  old_cols, new_cols, 2) == 0);
    assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &dave) == 0);
    ColumnValue david = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"david", .length = 5 }};
    assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &david) == 1);
    /* UPDATE of non-indexed column (id) → index unchanged */
    ColumnValue upd_old[2] = { new_cols[0], new_cols[1] };
    ColumnValue upd_new[2] = { { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 99 }, new_cols[1] };
    assert(index_maint_on_update(&reg, pool, "t", rid,
                                  upd_old, upd_new, 2) == 0);
    assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &david) == 1);
    /* UPDATE indexed column with same value → no-op */
    assert(index_maint_on_update(&reg, pool, "t", rid,
                                  upd_new, upd_new, 2) == 0);
    /* DELETE → index entry removed */
    assert(index_maint_before_delete(&reg, pool, "t", rid,
                                      upd_new, 2) == 0);
    assert(index_contains_value(&reg.index_trees[0], pool, &david) == 0);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_dml_index_maintenance\n");
}
```
### M7-TS-05 — Index Scan with Range Predicate
```c
void test_index_range_scan(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    page_id_t idx_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, idx_root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, idx_root, true);
    BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = idx_root };
    /* Also create table for double-lookup verification */
    page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
    rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
    BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
    /* Insert alice(1), bob(2), carol(3), dave(4) */
    const char *names[] = {"alice", "bob", "carol", "dave"};
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
        ColumnValue col = { .type = COL_TEXT,
            .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)names[i],
                     .length = (int)strlen(names[i]) }};
        int64_t rid = (int64_t)(i + 1);
        btree_insert(&table_tree, rid, &col, 1);
        index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &col, rid);
    }
    /* Range: name > 'b' → seek_ge('b'), skip exact 'b' matches (none here),
       collect while cursor valid:
       Seek to first ≥ 'b': that's 'bob'.
       We want name > 'b', so we need entries strictly greater than 'b'.
       'bob' > 'b' is true (memcmp "bob" vs "b" → 'o' vs '\0', positive).
       Collect: bob(2), carol(3), dave(4) → 3 results. */
    ColumnValue b = { .type = COL_TEXT,
```
