```c
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"b", .length = 1 }};
    IndexCursor *cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    int rc = index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, &b);
    assert(rc == 0);
    int64_t range_rids[16];
    int range_count = 0;
    while (!cur->is_eof && range_count < 16) {
        ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
        /* Check: cv > 'b' (strictly greater) */
        int cmp = column_value_compare(&cv, &b);
        if (cmp <= 0) {
            /* Seek positioned at 'b' or before — skip */
            index_cursor_next(cur);
            continue;
        }
        range_rids[range_count++] = rid;
        index_cursor_next(cur);
    }
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    assert(range_count == 3);
    /* Verify via double-lookup */
    const char *expected[] = {"bob", "carol", "dave"};
    for (int i = 0; i < range_count; i++) {
        BTreeCursor *tcur = btree_cursor_open(&table_tree);
        assert(btree_cursor_seek(tcur, range_rids[i]) == 0);
        ColumnValue cv;
        btree_cursor_column(tcur, 0, &cv);
        assert(cv.type == COL_TEXT);
        assert(cv.u.s.length == (int)strlen(expected[i]));
        assert(memcmp(cv.u.s.data, expected[i], cv.u.s.length) == 0);
        btree_cursor_close(tcur);
    }
    /* Range with upper bound: name >= 'bob' AND name < 'dave'
       → bob(2), carol(3) */
    ColumnValue bob_lb = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"bob", .length = 3 }};
    ColumnValue dave_ub = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"dave", .length = 4 }};
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, &bob_lb);
    range_count = 0;
    while (!cur->is_eof && range_count < 16) {
        ColumnValue cv; int64_t rid;
        index_cursor_current(cur, &cv, &rid);
        if (column_value_compare(&cv, &dave_ub) >= 0) break;
        range_rids[range_count++] = rid;
        index_cursor_next(cur);
    }
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    assert(range_count == 2);
    assert(range_rids[0] == 2);  /* bob */
    assert(range_rids[1] == 3);  /* carol */
    /* Empty range: name > 'zzz' → 0 results */
    ColumnValue zzz = { .type = COL_TEXT,
        .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"zzz", .length = 3 }};
    cur = index_cursor_open(&idx_tree, pool);
    rc = index_cursor_seek_ge(cur, &zzz);
    assert(rc == 1 || cur->is_eof);  /* no entries ≥ 'zzz' */
    index_cursor_close(cur);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_index_range_scan\n");
}
```
---
## 8. Performance Targets
| Operation | Target | Measurement Method |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| `column_value_compare` (integer pair) | ≤ 5 ns | `clock_gettime` over 10M iterations |
| `column_value_compare` (10-char text pair) | ≤ 15 ns | Same |
| `index_cell_serialize` | ≤ 80 ns | 1M serializations |
| `index_cell_deserialize` | ≤ 60 ns | 1M deserializations |
| `index_btree_insert` (1K entries) | ≤ 8 µs/entry | Total / 1K |
| `index_scan_equal` (1K-entry index, 1 match) | ≤ 50 µs | Average over 1K lookups; includes linear seek |
| `create_index` (10K-row table) | ≤ 100 ms | End-to-end including scan + insert |
| `index_maint_after_insert` (3 indexes on table) | ≤ 30 µs | Per INSERT including all 3 index updates |
| `index_btree_delete` (exact entry, 1K-entry index) | ≤ 40 µs | Average over 1K deletes |
| `index_contains_value` | ≤ 50 µs | 1K lookups on 1K-entry index |
**Benchmark template:**
```c
void bench_index_insert_1k(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 500);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree idx_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    struct timespec t0, t1;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);
    for (int i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
        char name[16];
        snprintf(name, sizeof(name), "name_%04d", i);
        ColumnValue cv = { .type = COL_TEXT,
            .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)name, .length = (int)strlen(name) }};
        index_btree_insert(&idx_tree, pool, &cv, (int64_t)(i + 1));
    }
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);
    long ns = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec) * 1000000000L
            + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec);
    printf("1K index inserts: %ld ns total, %ld ns/entry\n", ns, ns / 1000);
    assert(ns / 1000 <= 8000);  /* ≤ 8 µs/entry */
    pool_destroy(pool);
}
```
---
## 9. Build Integration
```makefile
# Additions to existing Makefile
INDEX_SRC = src/index.c src/index_maint.c
INDEX_OBJ = $(INDEX_SRC:.c=.o)
src/index.o: src/index.c src/index.h src/btree.h src/btree_cursor.h src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
src/index_maint.o: src/index_maint.c src/index_maint.h src/index.h src/btree.h \
                   src/btree_cursor.h src/pager.h src/dml.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
tests/test_index: tests/test_index.c $(INDEX_OBJ) $(DML_OBJ) $(BTREE_OBJ) \
                  tests/pager_mock.o tests/mock_disk.o
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK $^ -o $@ -lm
.PHONY: test-index
test-index: tests/test_index
	@echo "==> Running Index tests..."
	./tests/test_index
	@echo "==> All Index tests passed."
.PHONY: bench-index
bench-index: CFLAGS = -std=c11 -O2 -DNDEBUG -DMOCK_DISK -Isrc
bench-index: tests/test_index
	./tests/test_index --bench
```
---
## 10. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------|-----|
| Index entries not in sorted order after insert | `page_insert_cell_cmp` comparator has wrong sign convention | Comparator must return `positive` when existing > new (meaning: insert BEFORE this existing cell). Verify: `cmp_fn(page, existing_offset, new_key_ctx, NULL)` returns `> 0` when existing cell should come after the new cell |
| `index_scan_equal` returns 0 for a key that exists | `index_cursor_seek_ge` overshoots due to `column_value_compare` returning wrong sign for TEXT | Verify `column_value_compare("alice", "bob")` returns negative. Check `memcmp` is comparing with correct lengths |
| UNIQUE check passes when it should fail | `index_contains_value` compares entire `(value, rowid)` instead of just `value` | `index_contains_value` must use `index_scan_equal` which compares only the column value portion, ignoring rowid |
| `index_btree_delete` never finds the target entry | `compare_index_keys` tiebreaker has wrong sign | Verify: `compare_index_keys(&val, 5, &val, 10)` returns negative (5 < 10) |
| Index maintenance skips an index on the table | `strcasecmp` comparison against table name fails due to NUL-termination | Ensure `IndexSchema.table_name` is always NUL-terminated. Use `strncpy(dest, src, 63); dest[63] = '\0';` |
| `create_index` misses rows at page boundaries | `btree_cursor_next` returns 1 (EOF) incorrectly after page transition | Verify M5 cursor handles multi-page trees correctly (Phase 5 checkpoint from M5) |
| Page overflow during index insertion | Index cells are larger than expected (varint overhead for TEXT serial type) | For TEXT of length L, serial type = 2*L+13, which takes 1-2 varint bytes. Record header adds another varint. Total overhead: ~5-10 bytes per cell. Ensure page capacity calculation accounts for this |
| `index_maint_on_update` deletes wrong entry | Old column values not read before modification | `table_update` in M6 must read old row values BEFORE calling `btree_cursor_delete_current`. Pass old values to `index_maint_on_update` |
| Memory leak: `ColumnValue.u.s.data` from `index_cell_deserialize` | `record_deserialize` allocates for TEXT/BLOB data | Caller must free `ColumnValue.u.s.data` after use, or ensure it points into a pinned page buffer (borrowed). Clarify ownership in `index_cell_deserialize`: borrow from page buffer (page must be pinned during access) |
| Crash during `create_index` leaves orphan pages | Index root page allocated but not registered in catalog; crash before catalog update | Deferred to M9 (transactions). At M7, document that `create_index` is not crash-safe |
| Integer index values sort incorrectly with TEXT values | Missing type affinity check | `column_value_compare` checks `TypeOrder` first. INTEGER (TypeOrder=1) always sorts before TEXT (TypeOrder=2) regardless of value content |
---
## 11. State Machine — Index Lifecycle
```
States:
  UNREGISTERED     Index does not exist. No B-tree, no catalog entry.
  BUILDING         create_index in progress. Root page allocated, scan underway.
                   NOT crash-safe at M7 (no transaction support).
  ACTIVE           Index registered in IndexRegistry, B-tree populated.
                   All DML operations maintain this index.
  STALE            (Future: after crash during DML without transaction)
                   Index entries may not match table rows.
                   Requires REINDEX to repair.
Transitions:
  UNREGISTERED → BUILDING     create_index() called
  BUILDING     → ACTIVE       create_index() completes successfully
  BUILDING     → UNREGISTERED create_index() fails (UNIQUE violation)
  ACTIVE       → ACTIVE       INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE maintain consistency
  ACTIVE       → STALE        crash during DML (handled by M9 transactions)
  STALE        → ACTIVE       REINDEX (not implemented in M7)
Illegal transitions:
  ACTIVE → UNREGISTERED       DROP INDEX not implemented in M7
  BUILDING during active DML  must not modify table during CREATE INDEX
```
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m8 -->
# Module M8 — Query Planner & Statistics
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m8`
**Language:** C (ISO C11) | **Depends On:** `build-sqlite-m7` (Secondary Indexes), `build-sqlite-m6` (DML), `build-sqlite-m5` (B-tree), `build-sqlite-m4` (Buffer Pool), `build-sqlite-m3` (VDBE)
**Difficulty:** Expert | **Estimated Effort:** 11 hours
---
## 1. Module Charter
The Query Planner & Statistics module sits between the parser (M2) and the bytecode compiler (M3). It examines the AST produced by the parser, consults collected statistics about table sizes and column value distributions, enumerates candidate execution plans (table scan vs. index scan for each table, join orderings for multi-table queries), assigns a numeric cost to each candidate, and selects the cheapest plan. The selected plan is then passed to the compiler, which emits the corresponding bytecode sequence.
**What this module does:**
- Defines `ColumnStats` and `TableStats` structs that record per-column min, max, distinct count, null count, and per-table row count and page count.
- Implements the `ANALYZE` command, which performs a full table scan to collect statistics and persists them in a `sqlite_stat1` table.
- Implements selectivity estimation for equality predicates (`1 / distinct_count`), range predicates (`(max - value) / (max - min)`), `IS NULL` (`null_count / row_count`), and compound predicates (`AND` = product, `OR` = inclusion-exclusion).
- Implements a cost model: `cost_table_scan = page_count`, `cost_index_scan = index_height + selectivity × row_count × RANDOM_IO_FACTOR`.
- Implements `choose_access_path` that compares costs and returns a `PlanNode` (either `PLAN_TABLE_SCAN` or `PLAN_INDEX_SCAN`).
- Implements dynamic-programming join ordering for N tables (N ≤ 10) using bitmask subset enumeration.
- Extends `EXPLAIN` output to show the chosen plan, estimated rows, selectivity, and cost before the bytecode listing.
**What this module does NOT do:**
- It does not implement hash join or sort-merge join algorithms. All joins use nested-loop (M11).
- It does not implement histograms or Most Common Values (MCV) lists. Selectivity estimation assumes uniform distribution.
- It does not implement adaptive query execution (re-planning mid-execution).
- It does not implement multi-column index selection or covering index detection.
- It does not modify the bytecode compiler or VM — it produces a `PlanNode` tree that the compiler consumes.
**Upstream dependencies:** `IndexRegistry`, `IndexSchema` from M7. `BTree`, `BTreeCursor`, `btree_cursor_open/first/next/close`, `btree_cursor_column`, `btree_cursor_rowid` from M5. `BufferPool`, `pool_fetch_page`, `pool_unpin_page` from M4. `AstNode` from M2. `CompileContext` from M3.
**Downstream consumers:** M3's `compiler_compile` is modified to accept a `PlanNode*` and emit bytecode accordingly (index scan opcodes vs. table scan opcodes). M6 (DML) benefits from plan-driven access paths. M11 (aggregates/JOIN) uses the join ordering output.
**Invariants:**
1. `estimate_selectivity` always returns a value in `[0.001, 1.0]`. Never zero (would imply "impossible query") and never above 1.0.
2. The cost model never returns a negative cost.
3. `choose_access_path` always returns a valid `PlanNode`. If no index is applicable, it returns `PLAN_TABLE_SCAN`.
4. DP join ordering considers only pairs connected by a join predicate. Cartesian products are never considered unless explicitly requested.
5. After `execute_analyze`, every column of the analyzed table has a `ColumnStats` entry with `distinct_count ≥ 0` and `row_count` matching the table's actual row count.
6. Statistics default values are used when `ANALYZE` has not been run: `row_count = 1000000`, `distinct_count = 100`, `page_count = 10000`.
---
## 2. File Structure
```
build-sqlite/
├── src/
│   ├── planner.h         ← Step 1: PlanNode, PlanType, AccessPlan, ColumnStats,
│   │                        TableStats, Statistics, JoinPlanner structs, all public API
│   ├── planner.c         ← Step 2: Selectivity estimation, cost model, choose_access_path,
│   │                        DP join ordering, EXPLAIN plan output
│   ├── analyze.h         ← Step 3: execute_analyze declaration
│   ├── analyze.c         ← Step 4: ANALYZE implementation (scan + collect + persist)
│   ├── vdbe.h            ← (M3 — extend Compiler to accept PlanNode*)
│   ├── vdbe.c            ← (M3 — modify compile_select to use PlanNode*)
│   └── ... (M1–M7 files unchanged unless noted)
└── tests/
    └── test_planner.c    ← Step 5: Five test groups
```
**Dependency graph:**
```
planner.h  → ast.h, index.h, btree.h, pager.h, vdbe.h
planner.c  → planner.h, index.h, btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h
analyze.h  → planner.h, btree.h, pager.h
analyze.c  → analyze.h, planner.h, btree.h, btree_cursor.h, pager.h
test_planner.c → planner.h, analyze.h, index.h, btree.h, pager.h, dml.h
```
---
## 3. Complete Data Model
### 3.1 ColumnStats
```c
/* src/planner.h */
#ifndef PLANNER_H
#define PLANNER_H
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "btree.h"       /* ColumnValue, ColumnType */
#include "pager.h"       /* page_id_t, BufferPool */
#include "index.h"       /* IndexSchema, IndexRegistry */
#include "ast.h"         /* AstNode, NodeType */
typedef struct {
    char         column_name[64]; /* NUL-terminated                          */
    int          column_idx;      /* 0-based position in table schema        */
    ColumnValue  min_value;       /* minimum non-NULL value observed         */
    ColumnValue  max_value;       /* maximum non-NULL value observed         */
    int64_t      distinct_count;  /* number of distinct non-NULL values      */
    int64_t      null_count;      /* number of rows where this column is NULL*/
    bool         has_values;      /* false if column was all-NULL or table empty */
} ColumnStats;
/* sizeof(ColumnStats) ≈ 64 + 4 + (sizeof(ColumnValue)×2) + 8 + 8 + 1 + padding */
```
**Field-by-field rationale:**
| Field | Why |
|-------|-----|
| `column_name` | Human-readable; used for lookup by predicate column name |
| `column_idx` | Direct array indexing into schema's column list |
| `min_value` / `max_value` | Drive range predicate selectivity: `(max - value) / (max - min)` |
| `distinct_count` | Drive equality selectivity: `1.0 / distinct_count` |
| `null_count` | Drive `IS NULL` selectivity: `null_count / row_count` |
| `has_values` | Guards against division-by-zero when all values are NULL or table is empty |
### 3.2 TableStats
```c
#define MAX_STAT_COLUMNS 32
typedef struct {
    char          table_name[64];
    int64_t       row_count;                      /* total rows in table */
    int           page_count;                     /* leaf pages in table B-tree */
    ColumnStats   columns[MAX_STAT_COLUMNS];
    int           column_count;                   /* populated column slots */
} TableStats;
```
### 3.3 Statistics — Global Stats Store
```c
#define MAX_STAT_TABLES 64
typedef struct {
    TableStats  tables[MAX_STAT_TABLES];
    int         table_count;
} Statistics;
```
### 3.4 Default Statistics Constants
```c
#define DEFAULT_ROW_COUNT       1000000
#define DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT  100
#define DEFAULT_PAGE_COUNT      10000
#define DEFAULT_NULL_FRACTION   0.01
/* Selectivity clamp bounds */
#define MIN_SELECTIVITY         0.001
#define MAX_SELECTIVITY         1.0
/* Cost model tuning parameters */
#define RANDOM_IO_FACTOR        4.0
#define MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY   0.20  /* never use index if > 20% rows match */
```
### 3.5 PlanNode — Query Plan Representation
```c
typedef enum {
    PLAN_TABLE_SCAN  = 1,
    PLAN_INDEX_SCAN  = 2,
    PLAN_NESTED_LOOP = 3
} PlanType;
typedef struct PlanNode {
    PlanType     type;
    double       cost;               /* estimated total I/O cost               */
    int64_t      estimated_rows;     /* estimated result cardinality           */
    double       selectivity;        /* fraction of rows matching predicates   */
    /* PLAN_TABLE_SCAN / PLAN_INDEX_SCAN fields */
    char         table_name[64];
    int          root_page;
    int          table_col_count;
    /* PLAN_INDEX_SCAN additional fields */
    char         index_name[64];     /* name of the chosen index              */
    int          index_root_page;
    int          index_col_idx;      /* column index the index covers         */
    /* PLAN_NESTED_LOOP fields */
    struct PlanNode *outer;          /* left / driving table plan             */
    struct PlanNode *inner;          /* right / inner table plan              */
    int          join_outer_col;     /* column index for join predicate (outer)*/
    int          join_inner_col;     /* column index for join predicate (inner)*/
} PlanNode;
/* sizeof(PlanNode) ≈ 240 bytes on LP64 */
```

![Cost Model: TableScan vs IndexScan Comparison](./diagrams/tdd-diag-26.svg)

*Figure 26: PlanNode tree structures. Left: single-table PLAN_TABLE_SCAN node with cost, estimated_rows, and table metadata. Middle: single-table PLAN_INDEX_SCAN node with additional index metadata. Right: PLAN_NESTED_LOOP node whose outer and inner children are each PLAN_TABLE_SCAN or PLAN_INDEX_SCAN nodes. The cost of PLAN_NESTED_LOOP = outer.cost + outer.estimated_rows × inner.cost_per_probe.*
### 3.6 AccessPlan — Single-Table Plan Result (intermediate)
```c
typedef struct {
    PlanType    type;
    double      cost;
    double      selectivity;
    int64_t     estimated_rows;
    char        index_name[64];      /* empty string if table scan */
    int         index_root_page;
    int         index_col_idx;
} AccessPlan;
```
### 3.7 JoinPlanner — DP State for Multi-Table Joins
```c
#define MAX_JOIN_TABLES 10
typedef struct {
    /* Bitmask-indexed DP table. Entry dp[mask] holds the best plan for
       joining the set of tables whose bits are set in mask. */
    struct {
        double      cost;
        int64_t     estimated_rows;
        uint16_t    left_mask;       /* optimal left subset */
        uint16_t    right_mask;      /* optimal right subset */
    } dp[1 << MAX_JOIN_TABLES];
    int n_tables;
    /* Per-table metadata */
    struct {
        char      name[64];
        int64_t   row_count;
        int       page_count;
        double    filter_selectivity;  /* selectivity of WHERE on this table alone */
        int64_t   filtered_rows;       /* row_count × filter_selectivity */
    } tables[MAX_JOIN_TABLES];
    /* Join conditions: (table_a, table_b, col_a_idx, col_b_idx, ndv_a, ndv_b) */
    struct {
        int     table_a;
        int     table_b;
        int     col_a_idx;
        int     col_b_idx;
        int64_t ndv_a;           /* distinct value count for join column A */
        int64_t ndv_b;           /* distinct value count for join column B */
    } joins[MAX_JOIN_TABLES * MAX_JOIN_TABLES];
    int n_joins;
} JoinPlanner;
```
### 3.8 Stale Statistics Tracker
```c
typedef struct {
    char      table_name[64];
    int64_t   rows_at_analyze;   /* row_count when ANALYZE last ran      */
    int64_t   rows_current;      /* current row_count (updated on INSERT/DELETE) */
} StaleTracker;
#define MAX_STALE_TRACKERS 64
```
---
## 4. Interface Contracts
### 4.1 Statistics Lookup
```c
/* Find TableStats for a given table name. Returns NULL if no stats collected. */
TableStats *stats_find_table(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name);
/* Find ColumnStats within a TableStats. Returns NULL if column not found. */
ColumnStats *stats_find_column(TableStats *ts, const char *column_name);
/* Get row count for a table, using defaults if no stats. */
int64_t stats_get_row_count(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name);
/* Get page count for a table, using defaults if no stats. */
int stats_get_page_count(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name);
/* Get distinct count for a column, using defaults if no stats. */
int64_t stats_get_distinct(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name,
                           const char *column_name);
```
**Default behavior:** When `stats_find_table` returns NULL (ANALYZE not run), the `stats_get_*` functions return the corresponding `DEFAULT_*` constant. This ensures the planner always has numbers to work with, even if they are deliberately pessimistic.
### 4.2 Selectivity Estimation
```c
/* Estimate the selectivity of a WHERE clause expression.
   Returns a value in [MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY].
   stats may be NULL (defaults used).
   table_name identifies which table's stats to consult.
   row_count is the pre-filter row count. */
double estimate_selectivity(const AstNode *expr,
                            Statistics *stats,
                            const char *table_name,
                            int64_t row_count);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `expr` is a valid expression AST node (binary comparison, AND, OR, NOT, IS NULL check).
- `table_name` is a NUL-terminated string.
- `row_count > 0`.
**Postconditions:**
- Returns a `double` clamped to `[MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY]`.
**Dispatch rules by node type:**
| Node Type | Formula | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `NODE_BINARY_OP` with `op = "="` | `1.0 / ndv` where `ndv = stats_get_distinct(...)` | Uniform assumption. If `ndv <= 0`, use 0.01 |
| `NODE_BINARY_OP` with `op = "!="` | `1.0 - (1.0 / ndv)` | |
| `NODE_BINARY_OP` with `op ∈ {">",">=","<","<="}` | Range formula (§5.2) if min/max available, else `0.33` | |
| `NODE_AND` | `sel_left × sel_right` (independence assumption) | Clamp result |
| `NODE_OR` | `sel_left + sel_right - sel_left × sel_right` (inclusion-exclusion) | Clamp result |
| `NODE_NOT` | `1.0 - sel_inner` | Clamp result |
| `NODE_UNARY_OP` with `op = "NOT"` | Same as `NODE_NOT` | |
| IS NULL (detected by inspecting AST for `NULL` keyword comparison) | `null_count / row_count` if stats available, else `DEFAULT_NULL_FRACTION` | |
| Unknown expression type | `0.5` (conservative default) | |
**Error handling:** Never returns NaN or Inf. Division by zero guarded by `ndv <= 0` → use 0.01 default.
### 4.3 Cost Model
```c
/* Cost of a full table scan: all leaf pages read sequentially. */
double cost_table_scan(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name);
/* Cost of an index scan with the given selectivity.
   index_height estimated via estimate_tree_height().
   Includes random I/O factor for double-lookup. */
double cost_index_scan(Statistics *stats, const char *table_name,
                       double selectivity, int64_t row_count);
/* Estimate B-tree height given row_count and fanout. */
int estimate_tree_height(int64_t row_count, int fanout);
```
**`cost_table_scan`:**
```
cost = stats_get_page_count(stats, table_name)
```
Sequential reads; no multiplier.
**`cost_index_scan`:**
```
estimated_rows = selectivity × row_count
IF estimated_rows < 1: estimated_rows = 1
index_height = estimate_tree_height(row_count, 340)  // ~340 keys per internal page
index_leaf_pages = estimated_rows / 200 + 1           // ~200 entries per index leaf
table_pages_touched = MIN(estimated_rows, page_count)
cost = (double)index_height
     + (double)index_leaf_pages
     + (double)table_pages_touched × RANDOM_IO_FACTOR
```
**`estimate_tree_height`:**
```c
int estimate_tree_height(int64_t row_count, int fanout) {
    if (row_count <= 0) return 1;
    int height = 1;
    int64_t capacity = fanout;
    while (capacity < row_count) {
        capacity *= fanout;
        height++;
    }
    return height;
}
```
### 4.4 Plan Selection
```c
/* Choose the cheapest access path for a single table with optional WHERE.
   Consults IndexRegistry for available indexes.
   Returns a heap-allocated PlanNode* (caller frees). */
PlanNode *choose_access_path(Statistics *stats,
                             const char *table_name,
                             int root_page,
                             int col_count,
                             const AstNode *where_clause,
                             IndexRegistry *idx_reg);
```
**Preconditions:**
- `table_name` is NUL-terminated.
- `root_page > 0`.
- `where_clause` may be NULL (no filter → selectivity = 1.0).
- `idx_reg` may be NULL (no indexes → table scan only).
**Postconditions:**
- Returns a non-NULL `PlanNode*` with:
  - `type` = `PLAN_TABLE_SCAN` or `PLAN_INDEX_SCAN`.
  - `cost` > 0.
  - `estimated_rows` ≥ 1.
  - `selectivity` ∈ [MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY].
  - `table_name`, `root_page`, `table_col_count` populated.
  - For `PLAN_INDEX_SCAN`: `index_name`, `index_root_page`, `index_col_idx` populated.
**Algorithm:**
```
PROCEDURE choose_access_path(stats, table_name, root_page, col_count,
                              where_clause, idx_reg):
    row_count = stats_get_row_count(stats, table_name)
    page_count = stats_get_page_count(stats, table_name)
    // Baseline: table scan
    scan_cost = cost_table_scan(stats, table_name)
    best = alloc_plan_node(PLAN_TABLE_SCAN)
    best->cost = scan_cost
    best->estimated_rows = row_count
    best->selectivity = 1.0
    best->table_name = table_name
    best->root_page = root_page
    best->table_col_count = col_count
    IF where_clause == NULL OR idx_reg == NULL:
        RETURN best
    // Evaluate each index on this table
    FOR i = 0 TO idx_reg->count - 1:
        idx = &idx_reg->indexes[i]
        IF strcasecmp(idx->table_name, table_name) != 0: CONTINUE
        // Check if this index's column appears in the WHERE clause
        sel = estimate_index_selectivity(where_clause, stats, table_name,
                                          idx->column_name, row_count)
        IF sel < 0: CONTINUE    // index not applicable to this WHERE
        // Selectivity threshold: skip if too many rows match
        IF sel > MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY: CONTINUE
        idx_cost = cost_index_scan(stats, table_name, sel, row_count)
        IF idx_cost < best->cost:
            best->type = PLAN_INDEX_SCAN
            best->cost = idx_cost
            best->selectivity = sel
            best->estimated_rows = MAX(1, (int64_t)(sel * row_count))
            strncpy(best->index_name, idx->name, 63)
            best->index_root_page = idx->root_page
            best->index_col_idx = idx->column_idx
    RETURN best
```
**`estimate_index_selectivity` helper:**
Walks the WHERE AST looking for a comparison node where the left child is an identifier matching the index's column name. If found, returns the selectivity of that comparison. For compound predicates (`AND`/`OR`), recursively checks children. Returns `-1.0` if the index column does not appear in the predicate.
```c
static double estimate_index_selectivity(const AstNode *expr,
                                          Statistics *stats,
                                          const char *table_name,
                                          const char *index_col_name,
                                          int64_t row_count);
```
### 4.5 DP Join Ordering
```c
/* Initialize the JoinPlanner with table metadata and join conditions.
   tables[] and joins[] must be pre-populated by the caller. */
void join_planner_init(JoinPlanner *jp);
/* Run DP join ordering. After this call, jp->dp[(1<<n_tables)-1]
   contains the optimal plan for joining all tables. */
void join_planner_solve(JoinPlanner *jp);
/* Extract the optimal PlanNode tree from the DP result.
   Recursively constructs PLAN_NESTED_LOOP nodes.
   Returns a heap-allocated PlanNode* (caller frees). */
PlanNode *join_planner_extract_plan(JoinPlanner *jp,
                                     Statistics *stats,
                                     IndexRegistry *idx_reg);
```
**`join_planner_solve` algorithm:**

![Selectivity Estimation Decision Tree](./diagrams/tdd-diag-27.svg)

*Figure 27: DP join ordering state space for 3 tables {A, B, C}. Base cases: dp[{A}], dp[{B}], dp[{C}] = single-table access costs. Level 2: dp[{A,B}] = min(dp[{A}] + join(B), dp[{B}] + join(A)). dp[{A,C}], dp[{B,C}] similarly. Level 3: dp[{A,B,C}] = min over all binary partitions of {A,B,C} that have a join edge. Arrow annotations show cost = left_cost + right_cost + (left_rows × right_rows / max_ndv). The winning plan is the partition with minimum total cost.*
```
PROCEDURE join_planner_solve(jp):
    n = jp->n_tables
    total = 1 << n
    // Phase 1: base cases — single tables
    FOR i = 0 TO n - 1:
        mask = 1 << i
        jp->dp[mask].cost = (double)jp->tables[i].page_count
                            * jp->tables[i].filter_selectivity
        jp->dp[mask].estimated_rows = jp->tables[i].filtered_rows
        jp->dp[mask].left_mask = 0
        jp->dp[mask].right_mask = 0
    // Phase 2: enumerate subsets of increasing size
    FOR size = 2 TO n:
        FOR mask = 0 TO total - 1:
            IF popcount(mask) != size: CONTINUE
            jp->dp[mask].cost = 1e18  // infinity
            // Try all non-empty proper subsets of mask
            FOR left = (mask - 1) & mask; left > 0; left = (left - 1) & mask:
                right = mask & ~left
                IF right == 0: CONTINUE
                IF left > right: CONTINUE   // avoid duplicate pairs
                // Must have a join predicate connecting left and right
                IF NOT has_join_condition(jp, left, right): CONTINUE
                // Cost = cost to produce left + cost to produce right
                //      + nested-loop join cost
                left_entry  = &jp->dp[left]
                right_entry = &jp->dp[right]
                IF left_entry->cost >= 1e18 OR right_entry->cost >= 1e18: CONTINUE
                join_card = estimate_join_cardinality(jp, left, right,
                                                      left_entry->estimated_rows,
                                                      right_entry->estimated_rows)
                // Nested loop cost: outer scans fully, inner probed per outer row
                // If inner has an index on join column, cost_per_probe = log(inner_rows)
                // Otherwise cost_per_probe = inner_page_count
                inner_cost_per_probe = estimate_inner_probe_cost(jp, right, left)
                total_cost = left_entry->cost
                           + right_entry->cost
                           + (double)left_entry->estimated_rows * inner_cost_per_probe
                IF total_cost < jp->dp[mask].cost:
                    jp->dp[mask].cost = total_cost
                    jp->dp[mask].estimated_rows = join_card
                    jp->dp[mask].left_mask = left
                    jp->dp[mask].right_mask = right
```
**`has_join_condition`:**
```c
static bool has_join_condition(JoinPlanner *jp, uint16_t left, uint16_t right) {
    for (int j = 0; j < jp->n_joins; j++) {
        int a_bit = 1 << jp->joins[j].table_a;
        int b_bit = 1 << jp->joins[j].table_b;
        if ((left & a_bit) && (right & b_bit)) return true;
        if ((left & b_bit) && (right & a_bit)) return true;
    }
    return false;
}
```
**`estimate_join_cardinality`:**
```c
static int64_t estimate_join_cardinality(JoinPlanner *jp,
                                          uint16_t left_mask, uint16_t right_mask,
                                          int64_t left_rows, int64_t right_rows) {
    /* Find the join predicate connecting these subsets */
    for (int j = 0; j < jp->n_joins; j++) {
        int a_bit = 1 << jp->joins[j].table_a;
        int b_bit = 1 << jp->joins[j].table_b;
        bool connects = ((left_mask & a_bit) && (right_mask & b_bit)) ||
                        ((left_mask & b_bit) && (right_mask & a_bit));
        if (connects) {
            int64_t max_ndv = jp->joins[j].ndv_a > jp->joins[j].ndv_b
                            ? jp->joins[j].ndv_a : jp->joins[j].ndv_b;
            if (max_ndv <= 0) max_ndv = 1;
            int64_t result = (left_rows * right_rows) / max_ndv;
            if (result < 1) result = 1;
            return result;
        }
    }
    /* No connecting predicate — Cartesian product (should be filtered by has_join_condition) */
    return left_rows * right_rows;
}
```
**`estimate_inner_probe_cost`:**
```c
static double estimate_inner_probe_cost(JoinPlanner *jp,
                                         uint16_t inner_mask,
                                         uint16_t outer_mask) {
    /* For each table in inner_mask, sum page counts */
    double cost = 0.0;
    for (int i = 0; i < jp->n_tables; i++) {
        if (inner_mask & (1 << i)) {
            /* TODO: if index exists on join column of this table, use log cost */
            cost += (double)jp->tables[i].page_count;
        }
    }
    return cost;
}
```
### 4.6 ANALYZE Command
```c
/* src/analyze.h */
#ifndef ANALYZE_H
#define ANALYZE_H
#include "planner.h"
#include "btree.h"
#include "btree_cursor.h"
#include "pager.h"
/* Execute ANALYZE for one table. Scans all rows, collects stats.
   Writes results into the Statistics store.
   Returns 0 on success, -1 on error. */
int execute_analyze_table(Statistics *stats, BTree *table_tree,
                          BufferPool *pool, const char *table_name,
                          const char **col_names, int col_count);
/* Execute ANALYZE for all tables. */
int execute_analyze_all(Statistics *stats, /* array of BTree + schema info */
                        BufferPool *pool,
                        const char **table_names,
                        BTree *table_trees,
                        const char ***col_names_per_table,
                        int *col_counts,
                        int table_count);
#endif /* ANALYZE_H */
```
### 4.7 Plan Display (EXPLAIN extension)
```c
/* Print the plan summary to stdout before the bytecode listing. */
void plan_explain(const PlanNode *plan);
```
Output format:
```
QUERY PLAN
  SEARCH USING INDEX idx_id (estimated 1 rows, selectivity 0.0001, cost 17.3)
```
or:
```
QUERY PLAN
  SCAN TABLE users (estimated 10000 rows, cost 143.0)
```
or (nested loop):
```
QUERY PLAN
  NESTED LOOP JOIN (estimated 10000 rows, cost 3500.0)
    SCAN TABLE customers (estimated 500 rows, cost 10.0)
    SEARCH USING INDEX idx_cust_id (estimated 20 rows per probe, cost 7.0)
```
### 4.8 Plan Node Memory Management
```c
PlanNode *plan_node_alloc(PlanType type);
void plan_node_free(PlanNode *node);  /* recursive: frees outer/inner children */
```
---
## 5. Algorithm Specifications
### 5.1 Equality Selectivity
```
PROCEDURE estimate_equality_selectivity(stats, table_name, column_name, row_count):
    cs = stats_find_column(stats_find_table(stats, table_name), column_name)
    IF cs == NULL OR cs->distinct_count <= 0:
        RETURN 1.0 / DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT   // = 0.01
    sel = 1.0 / (double)cs->distinct_count
    RETURN CLAMP(sel, MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)
```
### 5.2 Range Selectivity

![Dynamic Programming Join Ordering](./diagrams/tdd-diag-28.svg)

*Figure 28: Range selectivity estimation on a numeric column. The column has min=10, max=100, and the predicate is `col > 60`. Selectivity = (max - value) / (max - min) = (100 - 60) / (100 - 10) = 40 / 90 ≈ 0.44. For `col < 60`: selectivity = (60 - 10) / (100 - 10) = 50/90 ≈ 0.56. For `col >= 60`: same as `col > 60` (uniform assumption: single-point mass is negligible). When min/max are not available (TEXT columns, no stats), default to 0.33.*
```
PROCEDURE estimate_range_selectivity(stats, table_name, column_name,
                                      bound_value, op, row_count):
    cs = stats_find_column(stats_find_table(stats, table_name), column_name)
    IF cs == NULL OR !cs->has_values:
        RETURN 0.33   // default for range
    // Only handle numeric ranges directly
    IF cs->min_value.type != COL_INTEGER AND cs->min_value.type != COL_REAL:
        RETURN 0.33
    IF bound_value->type != COL_INTEGER AND bound_value->type != COL_REAL:
        RETURN 0.33
    min_d = to_double(&cs->min_value)
    max_d = to_double(&cs->max_value)
    val_d = to_double(bound_value)
    range = max_d - min_d
    IF range <= 0.0: RETURN 0.5
    SWITCH op:
        CASE ">": CASE ">=":
            sel = (max_d - val_d) / range
        CASE "<": CASE "<=":
            sel = (val_d - min_d) / range
        DEFAULT:
            sel = 0.33
    RETURN CLAMP(sel, MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)
```
### 5.3 Compound Selectivity
```
PROCEDURE estimate_selectivity(expr, stats, table_name, row_count):
    IF expr == NULL: RETURN 1.0
    IF expr->type == NODE_AND:
        left_sel  = estimate_selectivity(expr->as.binary_op.left, ...)
        right_sel = estimate_selectivity(expr->as.binary_op.right, ...)
        RETURN CLAMP(left_sel * right_sel, MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)
    IF expr->type == NODE_OR:
        left_sel  = estimate_selectivity(expr->as.binary_op.left, ...)
        right_sel = estimate_selectivity(expr->as.binary_op.right, ...)
        RETURN CLAMP(left_sel + right_sel - left_sel * right_sel,
                     MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)
    IF expr->type == NODE_NOT:
        inner_sel = estimate_selectivity(expr->as.unary_op.operand, ...)
        RETURN CLAMP(1.0 - inner_sel, MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)
    IF expr->type == NODE_BINARY_OP:
        // Extract column name from left child (must be NODE_COLUMN_REF or NODE_IDENTIFIER)
        // Extract operator string
        SWITCH op:
            CASE "=":  RETURN estimate_equality_selectivity(...)
            CASE "!=": RETURN CLAMP(1.0 - estimate_equality_selectivity(...), ...)
            CASE "<": CASE "<=": CASE ">": CASE ">=":
                RETURN estimate_range_selectivity(...)
            DEFAULT: RETURN 0.5
    RETURN 0.5   // unknown expression
```
### 5.4 ANALYZE — Statistics Collection
```
PROCEDURE execute_analyze_table(stats, table_tree, pool, table_name,
                                 col_names, col_count):
    // Allocate or find existing TableStats entry
    ts = stats_find_or_create_table(stats, table_name)
    ts->row_count = 0
    ts->page_count = 0
    ts->column_count = col_count
    // Initialize per-column tracking
    FOR c = 0 TO col_count - 1:
        cs = &ts->columns[c]
        strncpy(cs->column_name, col_names[c], 63)
        cs->column_idx = c
        cs->distinct_count = 0
        cs->null_count = 0
        cs->has_values = false
        cs->min_value.type = COL_NULL
        cs->max_value.type = COL_NULL
    // Hash set per column for distinct count (simple approach: sorted array + dedup)
    // For M8: use a fixed-size hash table per column (capacity 65536)
    hash_sets[col_count] = allocate hash tables
    // Track page transitions for page_count
    last_page = INVALID_PAGE_ID
    cur = btree_cursor_open(table_tree)
    IF btree_cursor_first(cur) == 0:
        DO:
            ts->row_count++
            // Track page transitions (via cursor's internal page_id)
            IF cur->page_id != last_page:
                ts->page_count++
                last_page = cur->page_id
            FOR c = 0 TO col_count - 1:
                ColumnValue cv
                btree_cursor_column(cur, c, &cv)
                cs = &ts->columns[c]
                IF cv.type == COL_NULL:
                    cs->null_count++
                    CONTINUE
                cs->has_values = true
                // Update min/max
                IF cs->min_value.type == COL_NULL OR
                   column_value_compare(&cv, &cs->min_value) < 0:
                    column_value_copy(&cs->min_value, &cv)
                IF cs->max_value.type == COL_NULL OR
                   column_value_compare(&cv, &cs->max_value) > 0:
                    column_value_copy(&cs->max_value, &cv)
                // Insert into hash set for distinct count
                hash_set_insert(hash_sets[c], &cv)
        WHILE btree_cursor_next(cur) == 0
    btree_cursor_close(cur)
    // Finalize distinct counts
    FOR c = 0 TO col_count - 1:
        ts->columns[c].distinct_count = hash_set_count(hash_sets[c])
    // Free hash sets
    free_hash_sets(hash_sets, col_count)
    RETURN 0
```
**Hash set for distinct counting:** Use a simple open-addressing hash table with `ColumnValue` keys compared via `column_value_compare`. Initial capacity 4096, resize at 75% load. For M8, this is sufficient; production systems would use HyperLogLog for approximate counting on very large tables.
```c
typedef struct {
    ColumnValue *slots;
    bool        *occupied;
    int          capacity;
    int          count;
} DistinctSet;
DistinctSet *distinct_set_create(int capacity);
void distinct_set_destroy(DistinctSet *ds);
void distinct_set_insert(DistinctSet *ds, const ColumnValue *val);
int  distinct_set_count(DistinctSet *ds);
```
**`distinct_set_insert` hash function:** For integers: `(uint32_t)(val->u.i * 2654435761ULL) % capacity`. For text: FNV-1a over the byte content. For NULL: never inserted (NULLs counted separately).
---
## 6. Error Handling Matrix
| Error | Detected By | Recovery | User-Visible? |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------------|
| Table not found in stats | `stats_find_table` returns NULL | Use default constants | No — transparent |
| Column not found in stats | `stats_find_column` returns NULL | Use default selectivity | No — transparent |
| Division by zero (ndv=0) | `estimate_equality_selectivity` | Use `DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT` | No |
| Range estimation with non-numeric types | `estimate_range_selectivity` | Return `0.33` default | No |
| `pool_fetch_page` returns NULL during ANALYZE | Propagated from cursor | Return `-1` from `execute_analyze_table` | Yes — "analyze failed: buffer pool exhausted" |
| `btree_cursor_open` fails | Return NULL | Return `-1` | Yes |
| Join planner with > 10 tables | Check `n_tables > MAX_JOIN_TABLES` | Return error; refuse to plan | Yes — "too many tables in join" |
| No join predicate between subsets | `has_join_condition` returns false | DP skip: Cartesian product not considered | No |
| DP finds no valid plan (all costs = 1e18) | Final `dp[all].cost >= 1e18` | Fall back to left-deep sequential join order | No — degraded quality |
| `plan_node_alloc` OOM | `calloc` returns NULL | `fprintf + exit` (fatal) | Fatal |
| Stale statistics warning | `rows_current / rows_at_analyze > 1.2` | Print warning to stderr | Yes — "WARNING: statistics may be stale" |
| ANALYZE on empty table | `row_count == 0` after scan | All stats set to 0 / NULL / false. `distinct_count = 0` | No |
| Selectivity NaN/Inf | Clamp function | `CLAMP(sel, MIN_SELECTIVITY, MAX_SELECTIVITY)` always produces a finite value | No |
---
## 7. Implementation Sequence with Checkpoints
### Phase 1 — Statistics Structs and Storage (2 hours)
**Deliverables:**
1. `src/planner.h` with all constants, `ColumnStats`, `TableStats`, `Statistics`, `PlanNode`, `PlanType`, `AccessPlan`, `JoinPlanner` structs, all function declarations.
2. `src/planner.c` with `stats_find_table`, `stats_find_column`, `stats_get_row_count`, `stats_get_page_count`, `stats_get_distinct`, `plan_node_alloc`, `plan_node_free`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Define all constants (`DEFAULT_*`, `MIN_SELECTIVITY`, `MAX_SELECTIVITY`, `RANDOM_IO_FACTOR`, `MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY`).
2. Define `ColumnStats`, `TableStats`, `Statistics` structs.
3. Implement `stats_find_table` — linear scan over `stats->tables[]` matching `table_name` via `strcasecmp`.
4. Implement `stats_find_column` — linear scan over `ts->columns[]`.
5. Implement `stats_get_*` — delegates to `stats_find_*`; returns default if NULL.
6. Define `PlanNode`, `PlanType`, `AccessPlan`.
7. Implement `plan_node_alloc` — `calloc(1, sizeof(PlanNode))`, set type.
8. Implement `plan_node_free` — recursively free `outer` and `inner`, then `free(node)`.
**Checkpoint:**
```c
Statistics stats;
memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
/* No stats loaded → defaults */
assert(stats_get_row_count(&stats, "users") == DEFAULT_ROW_COUNT);
assert(stats_get_page_count(&stats, "users") == DEFAULT_PAGE_COUNT);
assert(stats_get_distinct(&stats, "users", "id") == DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT);
/* Add stats manually */
TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
strncpy(ts->table_name, "users", 63);
ts->row_count = 5000;
ts->page_count = 72;
ts->column_count = 1;
strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "id", 63);
ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 5000;
ts->columns[0].null_count = 0;
ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
stats.table_count = 1;
assert(stats_get_row_count(&stats, "users") == 5000);
assert(stats_get_page_count(&stats, "users") == 72);
assert(stats_get_distinct(&stats, "users", "id") == 5000);
assert(stats_find_table(&stats, "nonexistent") == NULL);
PlanNode *p = plan_node_alloc(PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
assert(p != NULL);
assert(p->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
plan_node_free(p);
printf("Phase 1 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 2 — Selectivity Estimation (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `estimate_selectivity`, `estimate_equality_selectivity`, `estimate_range_selectivity` in `planner.c`. Helper: `clamp_selectivity`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `clamp_selectivity(double sel)` — returns `MAX(MIN_SELECTIVITY, MIN(MAX_SELECTIVITY, sel))`.
2. Implement `estimate_equality_selectivity`.
3. Implement `estimate_range_selectivity` with numeric min/max check.
4. Implement `estimate_selectivity` as recursive dispatcher (§5.3).
**Checkpoint (validates M8-TS-04):**
```c
Statistics stats;
memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
strncpy(ts->table_name, "t", 63);
ts->row_count = 1000;
ts->page_count = 15;
ts->column_count = 2;
/* Column 'status': distinct=4 */
strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "status", 63);
ts->columns[0].column_idx = 0;
ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 4;
ts->columns[0].null_count = 0;
ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
/* Column 'val': distinct=100, min=1, max=100 */
strncpy(ts->columns[1].column_name, "val", 63);
ts->columns[1].column_idx = 1;
ts->columns[1].distinct_count = 100;
ts->columns[1].null_count = 50;
ts->columns[1].has_values = true;
ts->columns[1].min_value.type = COL_INTEGER;
ts->columns[1].min_value.u.i = 1;
ts->columns[1].max_value.type = COL_INTEGER;
ts->columns[1].max_value.u.i = 100;
stats.table_count = 1;
/* Equality: status='active' → 1/4 = 0.25 */
/* Build AST: NODE_BINARY_OP with op="=", left=NODE_COLUMN_REF("status"),
   right=NODE_STRING_LIT("active") */
AstNode *eq_expr = build_eq_expr("status", "active"); /* test helper */
double sel = estimate_selectivity(eq_expr, &stats, "t", 1000);
assert(fabs(sel - 0.25) < 0.01);
/* Range: val > 60 → (100-60)/(100-1) ≈ 0.404 */
AstNode *range_expr = build_gt_expr("val", 60); /* test helper */
sel = estimate_selectivity(range_expr, &stats, "t", 1000);
assert(sel > 0.39 && sel < 0.42);
/* IS NULL: null_count=50, row_count=1000 → 0.05 */
/* (Build IS NULL AST node) */
/* AND: 0.25 × 0.40 ≈ 0.10 */
AstNode *and_expr = build_and_expr(eq_expr, range_expr);
sel = estimate_selectivity(and_expr, &stats, "t", 1000);
assert(sel > 0.09 && sel < 0.12);
/* No stats → default */
sel = estimate_selectivity(eq_expr, NULL, "unknown", 1000);
assert(fabs(sel - (1.0 / DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT)) < 0.001);
ast_free(eq_expr); ast_free(range_expr); ast_free(and_expr);
printf("Phase 2 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 3 — Cost Model and Plan Selection (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `cost_table_scan`, `cost_index_scan`, `estimate_tree_height`, `choose_access_path`, `estimate_index_selectivity`, `plan_explain` in `planner.c`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `estimate_tree_height`.
2. Implement `cost_table_scan` — returns `(double)page_count`.
3. Implement `cost_index_scan` — formula from §4.3.
4. Implement `estimate_index_selectivity` — walks WHERE AST to find predicates on the given column.
5. Implement `choose_access_path` per §4.4.
6. Implement `plan_explain` for single-table plans.
**Checkpoint (validates M8-TS-01 and M8-TS-02):**
```c
Statistics stats;
memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
strncpy(ts->table_name, "t", 63);
ts->row_count = 10000;
ts->page_count = 143;
ts->column_count = 1;
strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "id", 63);
ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 10000;
ts->columns[0].null_count = 0;
ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
stats.table_count = 1;
/* Create index registry with one index on 'id' */
IndexRegistry idx_reg = { .count = 1 };
strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].name, "idx_id", 63);
strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].table_name, "t", 63);
strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].column_name, "id", 63);
idx_reg.indexes[0].column_idx = 0;
idx_reg.indexes[0].root_page = 10;
/* High selectivity: id = 500 → selectivity = 1/10000 = 0.0001 */
AstNode *eq = build_eq_expr("id", 500);
PlanNode *plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, eq, &idx_reg);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_INDEX_SCAN);
assert(plan->cost < 20.0);
assert(plan->estimated_rows <= 2);
assert(strcmp(plan->index_name, "idx_id") == 0);
plan_node_free(plan);
/* Low selectivity: status='active' on table with distinct=2 → sel=0.5 */
/* Should exceed MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY → table scan */
TableStats *ts2 = &stats.tables[1];
strncpy(ts2->table_name, "t2", 63);
ts2->row_count = 1000;
ts2->page_count = 15;
ts2->column_count = 1;
strncpy(ts2->columns[0].column_name, "status", 63);
ts2->columns[0].distinct_count = 2;
ts2->columns[0].null_count = 0;
ts2->columns[0].has_values = true;
stats.table_count = 2;
IndexRegistry idx_reg2 = { .count = 1 };
strncpy(idx_reg2.indexes[0].name, "idx_status", 63);
strncpy(idx_reg2.indexes[0].table_name, "t2", 63);
strncpy(idx_reg2.indexes[0].column_name, "status", 63);
idx_reg2.indexes[0].column_idx = 0;
idx_reg2.indexes[0].root_page = 20;
AstNode *eq2 = build_eq_expr("status", "active");
plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t2", 6, 1, eq2, &idx_reg2);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);  /* 0.5 > MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY */
plan_node_free(plan);
/* No WHERE → table scan */
plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, NULL, &idx_reg);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
plan_node_free(plan);
/* No index → table scan regardless of selectivity */
plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, eq, NULL);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
plan_node_free(plan);
ast_free(eq); ast_free(eq2);
printf("Phase 3 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 4 — DP Join Ordering (3 hours)
**Deliverables:** `JoinPlanner` struct operations, `join_planner_init`, `join_planner_solve`, `join_planner_extract_plan`, `has_join_condition`, `estimate_join_cardinality`, `estimate_inner_probe_cost` in `planner.c`. Extension of `plan_explain` for `PLAN_NESTED_LOOP`.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `has_join_condition`.
2. Implement `estimate_join_cardinality`.
3. Implement `estimate_inner_probe_cost`.
4. Implement `join_planner_init` — zero all `dp[]` entries, set costs to 1e18.
5. Implement `join_planner_solve` per §4.5.
6. Implement `join_planner_extract_plan` — recursive: if `popcount(mask) == 1`, return single-table plan; else split into `left_mask`/`right_mask` and create `PLAN_NESTED_LOOP` node.
7. Extend `plan_explain` for nested loop plans.
**Checkpoint (validates M8-TS-05):**
```c
JoinPlanner jp;
memset(&jp, 0, sizeof(jp));
jp.n_tables = 2;
/* Table 0: orders (10000 rows, 500 pages) */
strncpy(jp.tables[0].name, "orders", 63);
jp.tables[0].row_count = 10000;
jp.tables[0].page_count = 500;
jp.tables[0].filter_selectivity = 1.0;
jp.tables[0].filtered_rows = 10000;
/* Table 1: customers (500 rows, 10 pages) */
strncpy(jp.tables[1].name, "customers", 63);
jp.tables[1].row_count = 500;
jp.tables[1].page_count = 10;
jp.tables[1].filter_selectivity = 1.0;
jp.tables[1].filtered_rows = 500;
/* Join: orders.customer_id = customers.id */
jp.n_joins = 1;
jp.joins[0].table_a = 0;
jp.joins[0].table_b = 1;
jp.joins[0].col_a_idx = 1;   /* customer_id */
jp.joins[0].col_b_idx = 0;   /* id */
jp.joins[0].ndv_a = 500;
jp.joins[0].ndv_b = 500;
join_planner_init(&jp);
join_planner_solve(&jp);
/* Full set mask = 0b11 = 3 */
assert(jp.dp[3].cost < 1e18);
assert(jp.dp[3].estimated_rows > 0);
/* The optimal plan should have customers as the outer (smaller) table.
   Check: left_mask should contain customers (bit 1) */
bool customers_outer = (jp.dp[3].left_mask == 2);  /* bit 1 = table 1 */
bool orders_outer    = (jp.dp[3].left_mask == 1);  /* bit 0 = table 0 */
/* One of these must be true; the cheaper should be customers as outer
   because inner scan of orders with 500 pages × 500 outer rows = 250K,
   vs inner scan of customers with 10 pages × 10000 outer rows = 100K.
   So customers-outer might not always win — depends on exact cost model.
   Assert that a valid plan was found. */
assert(customers_outer || orders_outer);
/* Extract plan tree */
PlanNode *plan = join_planner_extract_plan(&jp, NULL, NULL);
assert(plan != NULL);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP);
assert(plan->outer != NULL);
assert(plan->inner != NULL);
/* The outer should be the table with lower total cost */
plan_explain(plan);  /* visual verification */
plan_node_free(plan);
/* 3-table join test: A(100), B(1000), C(50) */
memset(&jp, 0, sizeof(jp));
jp.n_tables = 3;
strncpy(jp.tables[0].name, "A", 63);
jp.tables[0].row_count = 100; jp.tables[0].page_count = 3;
jp.tables[0].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[0].filtered_rows = 100;
strncpy(jp.tables[1].name, "B", 63);
jp.tables[1].row_count = 1000; jp.tables[1].page_count = 30;
jp.tables[1].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[1].filtered_rows = 1000;
strncpy(jp.tables[2].name, "C", 63);
jp.tables[2].row_count = 50; jp.tables[2].page_count = 2;
jp.tables[2].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[2].filtered_rows = 50;
jp.n_joins = 2;
jp.joins[0].table_a = 0; jp.joins[0].table_b = 1;
jp.joins[0].ndv_a = 100; jp.joins[0].ndv_b = 100;
jp.joins[1].table_a = 1; jp.joins[1].table_b = 2;
jp.joins[1].ndv_a = 50; jp.joins[1].ndv_b = 50;
join_planner_init(&jp);
join_planner_solve(&jp);
assert(jp.dp[7].cost < 1e18);  /* mask=0b111=7 */
plan = join_planner_extract_plan(&jp, NULL, NULL);
assert(plan != NULL);
assert(plan->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP);
plan_explain(plan);
plan_node_free(plan);
printf("Phase 4 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
### Phase 5 — ANALYZE Command Implementation (2 hours)
**Deliverables:** `src/analyze.h`, `src/analyze.c` with `execute_analyze_table`, `execute_analyze_all`, `DistinctSet` implementation.
**Implementation order:**
1. Implement `DistinctSet` — `distinct_set_create`, `distinct_set_destroy`, `distinct_set_insert`, `distinct_set_count`.
2. Implement `column_value_hash` — hash function for `ColumnValue` (integer: multiplicative hash, text: FNV-1a, real: bit-cast then multiplicative).
3. Implement `execute_analyze_table` per §5.4.
4. Implement `execute_analyze_all` — iterates tables, calls `execute_analyze_table` for each.
**Checkpoint (validates M8-TS-03):**
```c
MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
page_id_t tbl_root = pool_new_page(pool);
uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, tbl_root);
page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, tbl_root, true);
BTree table_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = tbl_root };
/* Insert 100 rows: id=1..100, name alternating alice/bob */
for (int i = 1; i <= 100; i++) {
    ColumnValue cols[2];
    cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER;
    cols[0].u.i = i;
    cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
    const char *name = (i % 2 == 0) ? "alice" : "bob";
    cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t *)name;
    cols[1].u.s.length = (int)strlen(name);
    btree_insert(&table_tree, (int64_t)i, cols, 2);
}
Statistics stats;
memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
const char *col_names[] = {"id", "name"};
int rc = execute_analyze_table(&stats, &table_tree, pool, "t",
                                col_names, 2);
assert(rc == 0);
TableStats *ts = stats_find_table(&stats, "t");
assert(ts != NULL);
assert(ts->row_count == 100);
assert(ts->page_count > 0);
ColumnStats *id_stats = stats_find_column(ts, "id");
assert(id_stats != NULL);
assert(id_stats->distinct_count == 100);
assert(id_stats->null_count == 0);
assert(id_stats->has_values == true);
assert(id_stats->min_value.type == COL_INTEGER && id_stats->min_value.u.i == 1);
assert(id_stats->max_value.type == COL_INTEGER && id_stats->max_value.u.i == 100);
ColumnStats *name_stats = stats_find_column(ts, "name");
assert(name_stats != NULL);
assert(name_stats->distinct_count == 2);  /* alice and bob */
assert(name_stats->null_count == 0);
/* ANALYZE on empty table */
page_id_t empty_root = pool_new_page(pool);
rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, empty_root);
page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
pool_unpin_page(pool, empty_root, true);
BTree empty_tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = empty_root };
rc = execute_analyze_table(&stats, &empty_tree, pool, "empty",
                            col_names, 2);
assert(rc == 0);
TableStats *ets = stats_find_table(&stats, "empty");
assert(ets->row_count == 0);
assert(ets->columns[0].distinct_count == 0);
assert(ets->columns[0].has_values == false);
/* Re-ANALYZE updates stats */
ColumnValue extra = { .type = COL_INTEGER, .u.i = 101 };
ColumnValue extra_name = { .type = COL_TEXT,
    .u.s = { .data = (uint8_t*)"carol", .length = 5 }};
ColumnValue extra_cols[2] = { extra, extra_name };
btree_insert(&table_tree, 101, extra_cols, 2);
rc = execute_analyze_table(&stats, &table_tree, pool, "t",
                            col_names, 2);
assert(rc == 0);
ts = stats_find_table(&stats, "t");
assert(ts->row_count == 101);
id_stats = stats_find_column(ts, "id");
assert(id_stats->distinct_count == 101);
assert(id_stats->max_value.u.i == 101);
name_stats = stats_find_column(ts, "name");
assert(name_stats->distinct_count == 3);  /* alice, bob, carol */
pool_destroy(pool);
printf("Phase 5 checkpoint: PASS\n");
```
---
## 8. Test Specifications
### M8-TS-01 — Cost Model Prefers Index Scan for High-Selectivity Predicate
```c
void test_cost_model_index_preferred(void) {
    Statistics stats;
    memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
    TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
    strncpy(ts->table_name, "t", 63);
    ts->row_count = 10000;
    ts->page_count = 143;
    ts->column_count = 1;
    strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "id", 63);
    ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 10000;
    ts->columns[0].null_count = 0;
    ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
    stats.table_count = 1;
    IndexRegistry idx_reg = { .count = 1 };
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].name, "idx_id", 63);
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].table_name, "t", 63);
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].column_name, "id", 63);
    idx_reg.indexes[0].column_idx = 0;
    idx_reg.indexes[0].root_page = 10;
    /* id = 500 → selectivity = 1/10000 = 0.0001 */
    AstNode *eq = build_eq_expr("id", 500);
    PlanNode *plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, eq, &idx_reg);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_INDEX_SCAN);
    assert(plan->cost < 20.0);
    assert(plan->estimated_rows <= 2);
    assert(strcmp(plan->index_name, "idx_id") == 0);
    assert(plan->selectivity < 0.001);
    /* Table scan cost = 143. Index scan cost << 143. */
    double table_scan_cost = cost_table_scan(&stats, "t");
    assert(table_scan_cost > 100.0);
    assert(plan->cost < table_scan_cost);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* No index → always table scan */
    plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, eq, NULL);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* No WHERE → selectivity = 1.0 → table scan */
    plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, NULL, &idx_reg);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    ast_free(eq);
    printf("PASS: test_cost_model_index_preferred\n");
}
```
### M8-TS-02 — Cost Model Prefers Table Scan for Low-Selectivity Predicate
```c
void test_cost_model_table_scan_preferred(void) {
    Statistics stats;
    memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
    TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
    strncpy(ts->table_name, "t", 63);
    ts->row_count = 1000;
    ts->page_count = 15;
    ts->column_count = 1;
    strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "status", 63);
    ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 2;  /* 50% selectivity */
    ts->columns[0].null_count = 0;
    ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
    stats.table_count = 1;
    IndexRegistry idx_reg = { .count = 1 };
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].name, "idx_status", 63);
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].table_name, "t", 63);
    strncpy(idx_reg.indexes[0].column_name, "status", 63);
    idx_reg.indexes[0].column_idx = 0;
    idx_reg.indexes[0].root_page = 20;
    /* selectivity = 1/2 = 0.5 > MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY(0.20) → table scan */
    AstNode *eq = build_eq_expr("status", "active");
    PlanNode *plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 6, 1, eq, &idx_reg);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN);
    assert(plan->cost == 15.0);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* Now test high selectivity (distinct=20 → sel=0.05 < 0.20) → index scan */
    ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 20;
    plan = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 6, 1, eq, &idx_reg);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_INDEX_SCAN);
    assert(plan->selectivity < 0.1);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* Equal cost tie-break: prefer table scan (simpler, no double-lookup) */
    /* This is enforced by strict < in the cost comparison (not <=) */
    ast_free(eq);
    printf("PASS: test_cost_model_table_scan_preferred\n");
}
```
### M8-TS-03 — ANALYZE Updates Column Statistics
```c
void test_analyze_updates_stats(void) {
    MockDisk md; mock_disk_init(&md);
    BufferPool *pool = mock_pool_create(&md, 200);
    page_id_t root = pool_new_page(pool);
    uint8_t *rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, root);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, root, true);
    BTree tree = { .pool = pool, .root_page = root };
    /* Insert 100 rows: id=1..100, name alternating alice/bob */
    for (int i = 1; i <= 100; i++) {
        ColumnValue cols[2];
        cols[0].type = COL_INTEGER; cols[0].u.i = i;
        cols[1].type = COL_TEXT;
        const char *nm = (i % 2 == 0) ? "alice" : "bob";
        cols[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t *)nm;
        cols[1].u.s.length = (int)strlen(nm);
        btree_insert(&tree, (int64_t)i, cols, 2);
    }
    Statistics stats;
    memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
    const char *cols[] = {"id", "name"};
    assert(execute_analyze_table(&stats, &tree, pool, "t", cols, 2) == 0);
    TableStats *ts = stats_find_table(&stats, "t");
    assert(ts != NULL);
    assert(ts->row_count == 100);
    ColumnStats *id_cs = stats_find_column(ts, "id");
    assert(id_cs->distinct_count == 100);
    assert(id_cs->null_count == 0);
    assert(id_cs->min_value.type == COL_INTEGER);
    assert(id_cs->min_value.u.i == 1);
    assert(id_cs->max_value.type == COL_INTEGER);
    assert(id_cs->max_value.u.i == 100);
    ColumnStats *name_cs = stats_find_column(ts, "name");
    assert(name_cs->distinct_count == 2);
    assert(name_cs->null_count == 0);
    /* ANALYZE on empty table → zeros */
    page_id_t er = pool_new_page(pool);
    rp = pool_fetch_page(pool, er);
    page_init(rp, PAGE_TYPE_TABLE_LEAF);
    pool_unpin_page(pool, er, true);
    BTree et = { .pool = pool, .root_page = er };
    assert(execute_analyze_table(&stats, &et, pool, "empty", cols, 2) == 0);
    TableStats *ets = stats_find_table(&stats, "empty");
    assert(ets->row_count == 0);
    assert(ets->columns[0].distinct_count == 0);
    assert(ets->columns[0].has_values == false);
    /* Re-ANALYZE after INSERT updates stale stats */
    ColumnValue nc[2];
    nc[0].type = COL_INTEGER; nc[0].u.i = 101;
    nc[1].type = COL_TEXT;
    nc[1].u.s.data = (uint8_t *)"carol"; nc[1].u.s.length = 5;
    btree_insert(&tree, 101, nc, 2);
    assert(execute_analyze_table(&stats, &tree, pool, "t", cols, 2) == 0);
    ts = stats_find_table(&stats, "t");
    assert(ts->row_count == 101);
    id_cs = stats_find_column(ts, "id");
    assert(id_cs->distinct_count == 101);
    assert(id_cs->max_value.u.i == 101);
    name_cs = stats_find_column(ts, "name");
    assert(name_cs->distinct_count == 3);
    pool_destroy(pool);
    printf("PASS: test_analyze_updates_stats\n");
}
```
### M8-TS-04 — Selectivity Estimation Uses Distinct Count
```c
void test_selectivity_estimation(void) {
    Statistics stats;
    memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats));
    TableStats *ts = &stats.tables[0];
    strncpy(ts->table_name, "t", 63);
    ts->row_count = 400;
    ts->page_count = 6;
    ts->column_count = 2;
    /* status: distinct=4 */
    strncpy(ts->columns[0].column_name, "status", 63);
    ts->columns[0].distinct_count = 4;
    ts->columns[0].null_count = 20;
    ts->columns[0].has_values = true;
    /* score: distinct=100, min=0, max=99 */
    strncpy(ts->columns[1].column_name, "score", 63);
    ts->columns[1].distinct_count = 100;
    ts->columns[1].null_count = 0;
    ts->columns[1].has_values = true;
    ts->columns[1].min_value.type = COL_INTEGER;
    ts->columns[1].min_value.u.i = 0;
    ts->columns[1].max_value.type = COL_INTEGER;
    ts->columns[1].max_value.u.i = 99;
    stats.table_count = 1;
    /* Equality: 1/4 = 0.25 */
    AstNode *eq = build_eq_expr("status", "active");
    double sel = estimate_selectivity(eq, &stats, "t", 400);
    assert(fabs(sel - 0.25) < 0.01);
    int64_t est_rows = (int64_t)(sel * 400);
    assert(est_rows == 100);
    ast_free(eq);
    /* Range: score > 49 → (99 - 49) / (99 - 0) = 50/99 ≈ 0.505 */
    AstNode *gt = build_gt_expr("score", 49);
    sel = estimate_selectivity(gt, &stats, "t", 400);
    assert(sel > 0.49 && sel < 0.52);
    ast_free(gt);
    /* IS NULL: null_count=20, row_count=400 → 0.05 */
    /* (Build appropriate AST) */
    /* Predicate on non-indexed column without stats → default */
    AstNode *eq2 = build_eq_expr("unknown_col", "x");
    sel = estimate_selectivity(eq2, &stats, "t", 400);
    assert(fabs(sel - 1.0 / DEFAULT_DISTINCT_COUNT) < 0.001);
    ast_free(eq2);
    /* Compound: AND */
    AstNode *eq3 = build_eq_expr("status", "active");
    AstNode *gt2 = build_gt_expr("score", 49);
    AstNode *and_node = build_and_expr(eq3, gt2);
    sel = estimate_selectivity(and_node, &stats, "t", 400);
    assert(sel > 0.12 && sel < 0.14);  /* 0.25 × 0.505 ≈ 0.126 */
    ast_free(and_node);
    printf("PASS: test_selectivity_estimation\n");
}
```
### M8-TS-05 — Two-Table Join Ordering: DP Selects Smaller Outer
```c
void test_join_ordering_dp(void) {
    JoinPlanner jp;
    memset(&jp, 0, sizeof(jp));
    jp.n_tables = 2;
    /* orders: 10000 rows, 500 pages */
    strncpy(jp.tables[0].name, "orders", 63);
    jp.tables[0].row_count = 10000;
    jp.tables[0].page_count = 500;
    jp.tables[0].filter_selectivity = 1.0;
    jp.tables[0].filtered_rows = 10000;
    /* customers: 500 rows, 10 pages */
    strncpy(jp.tables[1].name, "customers", 63);
    jp.tables[1].row_count = 500;
    jp.tables[1].page_count = 10;
    jp.tables[1].filter_selectivity = 1.0;
    jp.tables[1].filtered_rows = 500;
    jp.n_joins = 1;
    jp.joins[0].table_a = 0;
    jp.joins[0].table_b = 1;
    jp.joins[0].ndv_a = 500;
    jp.joins[0].ndv_b = 500;
    join_planner_init(&jp);
    join_planner_solve(&jp);
    uint16_t all = 3;  /* 0b11 */
    assert(jp.dp[all].cost < 1e18);
    assert(jp.dp[all].estimated_rows > 0);
    /* Cost analysis:
       Option A: outer=customers(500), inner=orders(500 pages each probe)
         cost = 10 + 500 + 500 * 500 = 250510
       Option B: outer=orders(10000), inner=customers(10 pages each probe)
         cost = 500 + 10 + 10000 * 10 = 100510
       Option B is cheaper. So orders is outer, customers is inner.
       BUT: join cardinality = 10000*500/500 = 10000.
       Either way, the planner finds the minimum. */
    PlanNode *plan = join_planner_extract_plan(&jp, NULL, NULL);
    assert(plan != NULL);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP);
    assert(plan->outer != NULL);
    assert(plan->inner != NULL);
    /* Verify both tables appear in the plan */
    bool has_orders = (strcmp(plan->outer->table_name, "orders") == 0 ||
                       strcmp(plan->inner->table_name, "orders") == 0);
    bool has_customers = (strcmp(plan->outer->table_name, "customers") == 0 ||
                          strcmp(plan->inner->table_name, "customers") == 0);
    assert(has_orders && has_customers);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* 3-table join: evaluate all orderings */
    memset(&jp, 0, sizeof(jp));
    jp.n_tables = 3;
    strncpy(jp.tables[0].name, "A", 63);
    jp.tables[0].row_count = 100; jp.tables[0].page_count = 3;
    jp.tables[0].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[0].filtered_rows = 100;
    strncpy(jp.tables[1].name, "B", 63);
    jp.tables[1].row_count = 1000; jp.tables[1].page_count = 30;
    jp.tables[1].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[1].filtered_rows = 1000;
    strncpy(jp.tables[2].name, "C", 63);
    jp.tables[2].row_count = 50; jp.tables[2].page_count = 2;
    jp.tables[2].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[2].filtered_rows = 50;
    jp.n_joins = 2;
    jp.joins[0].table_a = 0; jp.joins[0].table_b = 1;
    jp.joins[0].ndv_a = 100; jp.joins[0].ndv_b = 100;
    jp.joins[1].table_a = 1; jp.joins[1].table_b = 2;
    jp.joins[1].ndv_a = 50; jp.joins[1].ndv_b = 50;
    join_planner_init(&jp);
    join_planner_solve(&jp);
    assert(jp.dp[7].cost < 1e18);  /* 0b111 = 7 */
    plan = join_planner_extract_plan(&jp, NULL, NULL);
    assert(plan != NULL);
    assert(plan->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP);
    /* One of outer/inner must itself be a PLAN_NESTED_LOOP (3-table → 2 joins) */
    assert(plan->outer->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP ||
           plan->inner->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP ||
           /* OR both are single-table (impossible for 3 tables) */
           false);
    plan_node_free(plan);
    /* Equal table sizes: either order acceptable */
    memset(&jp, 0, sizeof(jp));
    jp.n_tables = 2;
    strncpy(jp.tables[0].name, "X", 63);
    jp.tables[0].row_count = 1000; jp.tables[0].page_count = 15;
    jp.tables[0].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[0].filtered_rows = 1000;
    strncpy(jp.tables[1].name, "Y", 63);
    jp.tables[1].row_count = 1000; jp.tables[1].page_count = 15;
    jp.tables[1].filter_selectivity = 1.0; jp.tables[1].filtered_rows = 1000;
    jp.n_joins = 1;
    jp.joins[0].table_a = 0; jp.joins[0].table_b = 1;
    jp.joins[0].ndv_a = 100; jp.joins[0].ndv_b = 100;
    join_planner_init(&jp);
    join_planner_solve(&jp);
    assert(jp.dp[3].cost < 1e18);  /* valid plan found */
    printf("PASS: test_join_ordering_dp\n");
}
```
---
## 9. Performance Targets
| Operation | Target | Measurement Method |
|-----------|--------|--------------------|
| `estimate_selectivity` (simple equality) | ≤ 50 ns | `clock_gettime` over 1M iterations |
| `estimate_selectivity` (3-level compound AND/OR) | ≤ 200 ns | Same |
| `cost_table_scan` | ≤ 10 ns | Same (single lookup + return) |
| `cost_index_scan` | ≤ 30 ns | Same |
| `choose_access_path` (1 table, 3 indexes) | ≤ 500 ns | 100K iterations |
| `join_planner_solve` (2 tables) | ≤ 1 µs | 100K iterations |
| `join_planner_solve` (3 tables) | ≤ 5 µs | 10K iterations |
| `join_planner_solve` (10 tables) | ≤ 50 ms | Single invocation; DP over 1024 subsets |
| `execute_analyze_table` (10K rows, 3 columns) | ≤ 50 ms | End-to-end including cursor scan + hash set |
| `execute_analyze_table` (100K rows, 3 columns) | ≤ 500 ms | Same |
| `distinct_set_insert` (amortized) | ≤ 30 ns | 1M inserts into 64K-capacity set |
| `plan_explain` (single-table plan) | ≤ 10 µs | 10K calls, stdout redirected to `/dev/null` |
| `plan_explain` (3-table nested loop plan) | ≤ 50 µs | Same |
**Benchmark template:**
```c
void bench_choose_access_path(void) {
    Statistics stats;
    /* ... populate stats for a 10000-row table with 1 index ... */
    AstNode *eq = build_eq_expr("id", 42);
    IndexRegistry idx_reg = { /* ... */ };
    struct timespec t0, t1;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);
    for (int i = 0; i < 100000; i++) {
        PlanNode *p = choose_access_path(&stats, "t", 5, 1, eq, &idx_reg);
        plan_node_free(p);
    }
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);
    long ns = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec) * 1000000000L
            + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec);
    printf("choose_access_path: %ld ns/call\n", ns / 100000);
    assert(ns / 100000 <= 500);
    ast_free(eq);
}
```
---
## 10. Build Integration
```makefile
# Additions to existing Makefile
PLANNER_SRC = src/planner.c src/analyze.c
PLANNER_OBJ = $(PLANNER_SRC:.c=.o)
src/planner.o: src/planner.c src/planner.h src/ast.h src/index.h src/btree.h src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
src/analyze.o: src/analyze.c src/analyze.h src/planner.h src/btree.h \
               src/btree_cursor.h src/pager.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
tests/test_planner: tests/test_planner.c $(PLANNER_OBJ) $(INDEX_OBJ) $(DML_OBJ) \
                    $(BTREE_OBJ) src/ast.o src/parser.o src/lexer.o \
                    tests/pager_mock.o tests/mock_disk.o
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DMOCK_DISK $^ -o $@ -lm
.PHONY: test-planner
test-planner: tests/test_planner
	@echo "==> Running Planner tests..."
	./tests/test_planner
	@echo "==> All Planner tests passed."
.PHONY: bench-planner
bench-planner: CFLAGS = -std=c11 -O2 -DNDEBUG -DMOCK_DISK -Isrc
bench-planner: tests/test_planner
	./tests/test_planner --bench
```
---
## 11. Common Mistakes and Diagnostic Guide
| Symptom | Root Cause | Fix |
|---------|------------|-----|
| `choose_access_path` always returns table scan even with good index | `MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY` threshold too low, or `estimate_index_selectivity` returns -1 | Verify `estimate_index_selectivity` correctly identifies the WHERE column and matches it against the index column name (case-insensitive) |
| Selectivity returns 0.0 | `distinct_count` is very large and `1.0 / ndv` underflows | Clamped by `MIN_SELECTIVITY = 0.001` — ensure clamp is applied |
| DP join ordering gives infinite cost for valid query | `has_join_condition` misses the join edge | Verify both directions: `(left & a_bit) && (right & b_bit)` OR `(left & b_bit) && (right & a_bit)` |
| DP produces wrong cardinality estimate | `estimate_join_cardinality` divides by wrong NDV | The formula uses `MAX(ndv_a, ndv_b)` in the denominator. Verify the join condition indices match the correct tables |
| `execute_analyze_table` reports `distinct_count = 1` for a column with 1000 distinct values | Hash collisions in `DistinctSet` | Ensure hash function has good distribution. Verify `DistinctSet` resizes at 75% load factor. Test with sequential integers (worst case for bad hash functions) |
| `cost_index_scan` returns negative cost | `index_height` calculated incorrectly (0 or negative) | `estimate_tree_height` returns minimum 1 for any row_count ≥ 0 |
| Plan selection unstable: same query gets different plans on re-run | Statistics struct not zeroed before use | Always `memset(&stats, 0, sizeof(stats))` before first use. `ANALYZE` overwrites all fields |
| `execute_analyze_table` miscounts page_count | Cursor's `page_id` not exposed or always returns same value | Access `cur->page_id` directly from `BTreeCursor` struct. Verify cursor correctly updates `page_id` on page transitions |
| Range selectivity returns > 1.0 | `val_d` is outside `[min_d, max_d]` range | Clamp the raw selectivity before returning. For `val > max`, selectivity = 0 (clamped to `MIN_SELECTIVITY`). For `val < min`, selectivity = 1.0 |
| `plan_node_free` double-frees | `outer` and `inner` pointers not set to NULL after free | Set children to NULL after freeing, or ensure `plan_node_free(NULL)` is a no-op |
| ANALYZE on table with NULL-only column | `has_values == false`, min/max remain `COL_NULL` | `estimate_range_selectivity` checks `has_values` and returns default 0.33. `estimate_equality_selectivity` uses `distinct_count == 0` → default |
| `__builtin_popcount` not available on all compilers | GCC-specific builtin | Implement portable `popcount`: `int popcount(int x) { int c = 0; while (x) { c += x & 1; x >>= 1; } return c; }` |
| Cost comparison uses `<=` instead of `<` for tie-breaking | When costs are equal, table scan should win (no double-lookup overhead) | Use strict `<` in the comparison: `if (idx_cost < best->cost)` — table scan remains default |
---
## 12. State Machine — Statistics Lifecycle
```
States:
  ABSENT       No stats for this table. All lookups return defaults.
  COLLECTING   execute_analyze_table in progress. Cursor scanning rows.
  CURRENT      Stats freshly collected. row_count matches actual table.
  STALE        Table has been modified since last ANALYZE.
               rows_current / rows_at_analyze > 1.2 (20% drift).
Transitions:
  ABSENT     → COLLECTING    execute_analyze_table() called
  COLLECTING → CURRENT       scan completes successfully
  COLLECTING → ABSENT        scan fails (error return)
  CURRENT    → STALE         INSERT/DELETE changes row count by > 20%
  STALE      → COLLECTING    execute_analyze_table() called again
  STALE      → STALE         DML operations continue; stats drift further
  ANY        → ABSENT        DROP TABLE (not implemented in M8)
Illegal transitions:
  COLLECTING → COLLECTING    (re-entrant ANALYZE on same table)
  STALE → CURRENT            (only ANALYZE can refresh)
```
---
## 13. Integration with M3 Compiler
After M8 is complete, `compile_select` in `src/vdbe.c` is modified to accept a `PlanNode*`:
```c
/* Modified signature: */
static int compile_select(Compiler *c, const AstNode *stmt, PlanNode *plan);
```
**When `plan->type == PLAN_TABLE_SCAN`:** Emit the existing full-scan bytecode (OP_OPEN_READ, OP_REWIND, loop body, OP_NEXT, OP_CLOSE).
**When `plan->type == PLAN_INDEX_SCAN`:** Emit:
```
OP_OPEN_READ      table_cursor  root_page   col_count
OP_OPEN_INDEX_READ index_cursor  idx_root    0
OP_INTEGER         search_value  r_key       0
OP_IDX_SEEK        index_cursor  close_addr  r_key
[loop:]
OP_IDX_KEY         index_cursor  r_idx_val   0
OP_NE              r_idx_val     next_addr   r_key    ; stop if past matching range
OP_IDX_ROWID       index_cursor  r_rid       0
OP_SEEK_ROWID      table_cursor  r_rid       0
OP_COLUMN          table_cursor  col_idx     r_result
OP_RESULT_ROW      r_result      n_cols      0
[next_addr:]
OP_IDX_NEXT        index_cursor  loop        0
[close_addr:]
OP_CLOSE           index_cursor  0           0
OP_CLOSE           table_cursor  0           0
OP_HALT            0             0           0
```
**When `plan->type == PLAN_NESTED_LOOP`:** Emit nested OP_OPEN_READ / OP_REWIND / OP_NEXT pairs for outer and inner, with the join predicate as a comparison opcode in the inner loop body.
The compiler calls `choose_access_path` (or receives the `PlanNode*` from the caller) before emitting bytecode. The planner is invoked once per statement, never mid-execution.
---
<!-- END_TDD_MOD -->


<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m9 -->
<!-- TDD_MOD_ID: build-sqlite-m9 -->
# Module M9 — Transactions (Rollback Journal)
**Project:** Build Your Own SQLite | **Module ID:** `build-sqlite-m9`
